title: Rename module to github.com/ketang/zolem: documented `go install` cannot work against `module zolem.dev/zolem`
description:
**Current code facts:** `go.mod:1` is `module zolem.dev/zolem`; README.md:83-85 and INSTALL.md:139-141 instruct `go install github.com/ketang/zolem/cmd/zolem@latest`, which fails on module-path mismatch; no vanity-import server is known to exist for zolem.dev.

**Decision (maintainer, 2026-06-12): rename the module to `github.com/ketang/zolem`.**

**Work:** change go.mod module line; rewrite all internal import paths (`zolem.dev/zolem/...` -> `github.com/ketang/zolem/...`); check for the old path in docs, scripts, .goreleaser.yaml (ldflags/main paths), Dockerfile, and CI workflows; run full test suite.

**Acceptance checks:** `go install github.com/ketang/zolem/cmd/zolem@latest` and `.../zolemc@latest` work verbatim from a clean machine after the next tag; `grep -r zolem.dev` returns nothing load-bearing; full test suite green.

**Scope:** repo-wide mechanical rewrite; size: medium.

---

title: No LICENSE file; .goreleaser.yaml archives `LICENSE*` so releases should fail on unmatched glob
description:
**Current code facts:** repo root has no LICENSE (verified); `.goreleaser.yaml` archive `files:` includes `LICENSE*`; goreleaser errors on unmatched file globs. Independent of the packaging bug, a public project documenting binary releases needs a license for the releases to be usable at all.

**Decision (maintainer, 2026-06-12): Apache License 2.0.**

**Recommendation:** add the standard Apache-2.0 LICENSE text at repo root (copyright holder: Ketan Gangatirkar); optionally add a NOTICE file per Apache convention; then confirm goreleaser archive build passes (`goreleaser release --snapshot --clean` locally or in CI).

**Acceptance checks:** LICENSE present at root; snapshot goreleaser build succeeds; archives contain the license.

---

title: nightly.yml runs a full release on every push to main and clobbers the :latest Docker tag; no :nightly image is ever produced
description:
**Current code facts:**
- `.github/workflows/nightly.yml` triggers `on: push: branches: [main]` — not a schedule. bd-sync tracker commits land on main several times a day; each force-moves the `nightly` tag and runs full GoReleaser (multi-arch Docker builds, cosign, GHCR pushes).
- It runs `goreleaser release --clean` with `GORELEASER_CURRENT_TAG=nightly` — a release, not a snapshot, so `.IsSnapshot` is false and `.goreleaser.yaml`'s mutable templates `{{ if .IsSnapshot }}nightly{{ else }}latest{{ end }}` resolve to `latest`: every nightly overwrites `ghcr.io/ketang/zolem:latest`. The `snapshot.version_template` block is dead. `nightly` is not semver, which goreleaser release mode rejects.
- nightly.yml installs cosign but not syft, while `.goreleaser.yaml` has an `sboms:` section — SBOM generation should fail.
- The mutable `docker_manifests` entry mixes a mutable amd64 leg with immutable `{{ .Version }}-arm64`; there is no mutable arm64 image.
- INSTALL.md:145-152 documents nightly behavior (pre-releases, `:nightly` image) that the pipeline does not implement.

**Note for implementer:** two claims above are reasoned from config, not observed from a run — that SBOM generation fails (no syft) and that goreleaser rejects the non-semver `nightly` tag. Confirm both against an actual workflow run log on GitHub before finalizing the fix shape; the observable facts (push-triggered, `latest` template resolution) hold either way.

**Recommendation:** trigger on `schedule:` cron + `workflow_dispatch` (or add `paths-ignore` for `.beads/**` and `docs/**` at minimum); use snapshot semantics or a dedicated nightly goreleaser config so `:nightly` is produced and `:latest` untouched; add syft install; fix the arm64 manifest leg; align INSTALL.md.

**Acceptance checks:** pushing a docs/tracker commit to main triggers no release; scheduled run publishes `:nightly` without moving `:latest`; SBOMs attach; multi-arch nightly manifest resolves on both platforms.

**Scope:** in: nightly.yml + .goreleaser.yaml mutable templates + INSTALL.md nightly section. Out: stable release workflow (works today).

---

title: Synthesize tool-call responses honoring tool_choice (all providers)
description:
**Symptom (verified):** OpenAI `tool_choice:"required"` and Anthropic `tool_choice:{"type":"any"}` both return plain text with `finish_reason:"stop"` / `stop_reason:"end_turn"`. No provider ever emits `tool_calls` / `tool_use` blocks, so an agent loop tested against Zolem never exercises its tool path — the core code path of most modern integrations.

**Recommendation:** when the request contains tools and tool_choice requires/permits a call, synthesize a provider-native tool-call response: pick a tool (honor explicit tool_choice naming), generate schema-conformant fake arguments from the tool's JSON Schema (lorem/faker-style leaf values), set finish_reason `tool_calls` / stop_reason `tool_use`. Support streaming variants (OpenAI tool-call delta chunks; Anthropic `input_json_delta` events). Apply to OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini (functionCall part).

**Code locators:** response construction and streaming live in `internal/provider/openai/handler.go`, `internal/provider/anthropic/handler.go`, `internal/provider/gemini/handler.go` (plus each provider's `types.go` for request-side tools/tool_choice decoding). If the zolem-amn ContentBackend refactor has landed first, implement at that seam instead.

**Depends on:** zolem-4n5 (Anthropic schema must accept tool_use/tool_result turns for the round-trip acceptance check).

**Acceptance checks:** SDK-compat e2e: official OpenAI and Anthropic SDK agent loops receive a parseable tool call and can submit a tool result turn (combined with zolem-4n5); streaming tool calls parse in both SDKs; `tool_choice:"none"` still returns text.

**Scope:** in: lorem/faker backends' tool-call synthesis. Out: fixture/wasm backends (they already control full response bodies); multi-step agent simulation policy. Size: large — consider one sub-issue per provider after the shared design.

---

title: OpenAI listener rejects array-of-parts message content with 400
description:
**Symptom (verified):** `{"messages":[{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"hi"}]}]}` returns 400: `invalid JSON: json: cannot unmarshal array into Go struct field Message.messages.content of type string`. The real API accepts both string and array forms; LangChain and many frameworks always emit the array form, so they fail on first request.

**Current code facts:** `internal/provider/openai/types.go` declares `Message.Content` as `string`.

**Recommendation:** decode content as string-or-array (custom UnmarshalJSON or json.RawMessage with both branches); concatenate text parts for generators that need plain text; preserve raw form for fixtures/recording.

**Acceptance checks:** array-form content returns 200 on /v1/chat/completions (stream and non-stream); string form unchanged; a test covers both.

**Scope:** in: request decoding. Out: vision/image-part semantics in generated responses.

---

title: Anthropic vendored schema rejects valid requests: tool_use/tool_result/image content blocks get HTTP 400
description:
**Symptom (verified empirically):** a standard agentic round-trip — assistant turn containing a `tool_use` block followed by a user turn containing a `tool_result` block — returns HTTP 400 from a Zolem anthropic listener. `image` blocks also 400. The real Anthropic API accepts these. Meanwhile invalid enum values pass (schema too loose where it should be strict).

**Current code facts:**
- `internal/specs/vendored/anthropic-v1.json` content-block definition allows only `type:"text"`.
- Error message leaks internals: `jsonschema validation failed with 'mem://anthropic-v1.json#'`.

**Why it matters:** a mock that 400s requests the real API accepts breaks integration tests mid-conversation; replaying a real agentic transcript through the mock is impossible.

**Recommendation:** extend the content-block schema to cover `tool_use`, `tool_result`, `image`, `document`, and `thinking` block types per Anthropic's public Messages API docs (or supersede with a real-spec-derived snapshot via zolem-e9h). Add e2e coverage replaying a multi-turn tool conversation.

**Acceptance checks:** a multi-turn request with tool_use/tool_result blocks returns 200; an image-block request returns 200; existing text-only tests still pass.

**Scope:** in: request-side schema acceptance. Out: generating tool_use responses (zolem-53m), error message wording (zolem-cx7).

---

title: Spec validation is unwired in production: empty sources map, only a hand-written anthropic:v1 schema loads, validator silently passes without a schema
description:
**Symptom:** README claims Zolem "validates requests against real OpenAPI/discovery specs," but at runtime OpenAI and Gemini requests undergo zero schema validation, and Anthropic is validated only against a 1.8KB hand-written JSON Schema.

**Current code facts:**
- `cmd/zolem/startup.go:224` calls `loadSpecs(validator, deps.newFetcher(..., map[string]string{}))` — an empty sources map, so nothing is ever fetched.
- `specSourceMap()` at `cmd/zolem/startup.go:421-428` contains the real OpenAI/Gemini source URLs but is referenced only by `startup_local_test.go:478`.
- `internal/specs/vendored.go` `VendoredFallbacks()` returns only `anthropic:v1` (`internal/specs/vendored/anthropic-v1.json`, hand-written, ~1.8KB).
- `internal/specs/validator.go:71` `Validate` returns nil when no schema is registered for a provider/version — silent pass-through.
- The full normalization pipeline (`internal/specs/openapi.go`, `discovery.go`, `loader.go`, `registry.go`, `fetcher.go` HTTP path) is tested but unreachable from main. `fetchURL` uses timeout-less `http.Get`.
- Fixed-listener startup logs `warn: failed to fetch spec openai:v1: no spec available for openai/v1` (also openrouter, gemini v1/v1beta).

**Why it matters:** pass-through-on-missing-schema green-lights broken integrations — the worst failure mode for a validation mock.

**Recommended approach (default):** generate normalized vendored snapshots for all (provider, version) pairs at build time using the existing normalizers (offline-first matches the no-egress posture); wire them through `VendoredFallbacks()`; make a missing schema either fail startup or be visibly reported in `/_zolem/state`; remove or wire `specSourceMap` and give `fetchURL` a timeout-bearing client; update the README claim to match actual behavior in the same change.
**Open decision (does not block start):** whether the runtime HTTP fetch path has a future or should be deleted outright; default to deleting it if vendored snapshots cover all providers.

**Acceptance checks:**
- A request with a schema violation against OpenAI and Gemini listeners returns a 400 (currently passes).
- `/_zolem/state` (or startup failure) makes missing-schema status visible.
- No `warn: failed to fetch spec` lines for providers with vendored snapshots.
- README claim matches shipped behavior.

**Scope:** in: spec wiring, snapshot generation, README claim. Out: improving the schemas' content fidelity (see zolem-4n5), tool-use synthesis (zolem-53m). Size: large; may split snapshot-generation tooling into its own task.

---

title: Shatter factories for Wazero values
description:
## What

Add Zolem-owned Shatter scan support for values that depend on Wazero runtime state and cannot be synthesized structurally today. In this issue, "type factory" means a scan-only factory for a specific Go type: an expression/config/plugin entry that returns a concrete value for that type. Use that term in local notes if it maps cleanly onto Shatter's actual mechanism (`go_runtime_values`, `generators`, or the current extension/plugin hook), but do not invent a separate config vocabulary.

The concrete implementation target is Wazero-backed scan values. `parse.Node` and `interface{}`/`any` are included as bounded disposition checks because they came up in the same Shatter scan gap discussion, but they should not expand this issue into general Shatter analyzer work.

## Current Facts

- Zolem repository root: run commands from the repo root.
- Current Shatter config: `.shatter/config.yaml`.
- Existing scan-only value: `.shatter/config.yaml` already uses `go_runtime_values` for `fixture.CompiledModule`, constructing it through `NewRunner().CompileWASM(...)`.
- `internal/fixture/wasm.go` defines `Runner` with private field `rt wazero.Runtime`; `NewRunner()` creates it with `wazero.NewRuntime(context.Background())`.
- `internal/fixture/selector.go` defines `fixture.Selector`; `NewLegacySelector(runner *Runner)` can construct a selector backed by a `Runner`.
- `internal/wasmgen/generator.go` defines `Generator` with private fields `rt wazero.Runtime` and `compiled wazero.CompiledModule`; `Compile(...)` creates a runtime with `wazero.NewRuntimeWithConfig(...)`.
- A focused code search in `cmd/` and `internal/` found no direct `parse.Node` reference in Zolem. Treat `parse.Node` as an upstream Shatter first-party-specialized-factory question unless new local evidence appears.
- The current dry-run has no remaining unsupported skip mentioning `interface{}` or `any`, but Zolem has many `any`/`map[string]any` JSON paths. Treat `interface{}`/`any` as a behavior-quality/narrowing follow-up only if evidence from full scan output shows poor candidate spread or low-value behavior maps.

## Baseline Repro

Use the current Shatter CLI under test, then run:

```bash
SHATTER_SANDBOX_BACKEND=docker \
SHATTER_SANDBOX_DOCKER_IMAGE=golang:1.26-bookworm \
SHATTER_ARTIFACT_DIR=/tmp/zolem-shatter-artifacts-type-factories-dry \
SHATTER_HARNESS_CACHE=/tmp/zolem-shatter-harness-type-factories-dry \
SHATTER_GO_WORKSPACE_ROOT=/tmp/shatter-go-workspaces-type-factories-dry \
shatter scan \
  --project-dir . \
  --language go \
  --all \
  --dry-run \
  --no-cache \
  --output /tmp/zolem-type-factories-dry.md \
  --output /tmp/zolem-type-factories-dry.json \
  .
```

Recent baseline evidence from the active Shatter run:

- discovered functions: 360
- selected functions: 326
- unsupported functions: 34
- no unsupported reasons mentioning `interface{}` or `any`

## Baseline Unsupported Skip List

The current 34 unsupported dry-run skips are:

1. `runLocalAdmin`: `main.startupDeps` has no constructor.
2. `newLocalControlPlane`: `main.startupDeps` has no constructor.
3. `startLocalServer`: `http.Handler` opaque.
4. `startLocalHTTPServer`: `http.Handler` opaque.
5. `startLocalTLSServer`: `http.Handler` opaque.
6. `recordingMiddleware`: `main.Recorder` opaque.
7. `recorderListener`: `main.Recorder` opaque.
8. `runLocal`: `main.startupDeps` has no constructor.
9. `buildLocalStartupApp`: `main.startupDeps` has no constructor.
10. `buildLocalStartupAppWithRecorder`: `main.Recorder` opaque and `main.startupDeps` has no constructor.
11. `buildLocalStartupAppForRuntime`: `main.Recorder` opaque and `main.startupDeps` has no constructor.
12. `loadSpecs`: `main.specFetcher` opaque.
13. `buildLocalHandler`: `fixture.Selector` opaque and `response.Generator` opaque.
14. `generatorForBackend`: `main.startupDeps` has no constructor.
15. `runAdminHealth`: `main.adminClient` and `main.rootOptions` have no constructors.
16. `runProfiles`: `main.adminClient` and `main.rootOptions` have no constructors.
17. `runListeners`: `main.adminClient` and `main.rootOptions` have no constructors.
18. `runListenerCalls`: `main.adminClient` and `main.rootOptions` have no constructors.
19. `runListenerCallsList`: `main.adminClient` and `main.rootOptions` have no constructors.
20. `runListenerCallsClear`: `main.adminClient` and `main.rootOptions` have no constructors.
21. `runListener`: `main.rootOptions` has no constructor.
22. `runProviderRequest`: `main.rootOptions` has no constructor.
23. `NewMatcher`: `fixture.Selector` opaque.
24. `(*Matcher).SwapWithSelector`: `fixture.Selector` opaque.
25. `StartWatcher`: nested `fixture.Selector` opaque.
26. `watchLoop`: nested `fixture.Selector` opaque and `chan struct{}` runtime scheduling state.
27. `reload`: nested `fixture.Selector` opaque.
28. provider `NewHandler` for Anthropic: `fixture.Selector`, `response.Generator`, and provider generator interfaces opaque.
29. provider `NewHandler` for Gemini: `fixture.Selector`, `response.Generator`, and provider generator interfaces opaque.
30. provider `NewHandler` for OpenAI: `fixture.Selector`, `response.Generator`, and provider generator interfaces opaque.
31. `asValidationError`: nested `jsonschema.ErrorKind` opaque.
32. `collectMessages`: nested `jsonschema.ErrorKind` opaque.
33. `callU32`: `api.Function` opaque.
34. `validateSignature`: `api.FunctionDefinition` opaque.

## Work

1. Confirm the Shatter-supported factory mechanism for Go type-specific scan values and document whether Zolem should call these "type factories" locally.
2. Add the smallest Zolem `.shatter` config/plugin entries or scan-only helper constructors that provide Wazero-backed values. Start with this ordered target list:
   - `fixture.Selector` via `NewLegacySelector(NewRunner())`, expected to unlock `NewMatcher`, `SwapWithSelector`, watcher paths, and handler constructors that currently stop at selector opacity.
   - `api.FunctionDefinition` for `validateSignature`, using a real Wazero-compiled export if Shatter can keep the compiled module/runtime alive safely.
   - `api.Function` for `callU32`, using an instantiated Wazero export only if lifecycle ownership can be made explicit and safe.
3. Re-run the dry-run command above and compare the unsupported skip list to the 34-skip baseline.
4. Record the `parse.Node` disposition in the issue notes:
   - if no Zolem local use exists, file or update a Shatter issue for a first-party specialized `parse.Node` factory only when the Shatter repo has evidence or an existing extension point needing it;
   - if a Zolem local use is found, add a local config example or file a follow-up Zolem issue with the exact function/type evidence.
5. Record the `interface{}`/`any` disposition in the issue notes:
   - if the full scan shows low-value behavior maps caused by generic `any` guesses, file or update a Shatter issue for context-based narrowing with concrete functions and output evidence;
   - if not, note that current Zolem dry-run has no `interface{}`/`any` unsupported skips and leave it out of this Zolem implementation.

## Acceptance Checks

- [ ] Zolem's Shatter setup can construct at least one Wazero-backed target value (`fixture.Selector`, `api.FunctionDefinition`, or `api.Function`) without panics or unsafe leaked process-lifetime assumptions.
- [ ] The dry-run command above reports fewer unsupported skips than the 34-skip baseline, or the issue notes explain with concrete evidence why Wazero-backed skips cannot be reduced from Zolem alone.
- [ ] The issue outcome explicitly states whether "type factory" is the accepted local term, and which Shatter config/extension keys implement it.
- [ ] The issue outcome includes a concrete `parse.Node` disposition: no local Zolem work, local config/example added, or upstream Shatter issue filed/updated.
- [ ] The issue outcome includes a concrete `interface{}`/`any` disposition: no local Zolem work, local config/example added, or upstream Shatter dynamic-narrowing issue filed/updated with evidence.

## In Scope

- Zolem `.shatter` config/plugin changes.
- Small scan-only helper constructors in Zolem if needed to expose valid values cleanly.
- Filing linked upstream Shatter issues only when Zolem cannot supply the support locally.

## Out of Scope

- Reworking Zolem production runtime behavior just to satisfy Shatter.
- Building a general Shatter plugin system inside Zolem.
- Implementing broad Shatter analyzer/planner changes in this Zolem issue.
- Treating `parse.Node` or `interface{}`/`any` as implementation work without concrete local evidence.
notes: Implementation evidence: added Zolem scan-only Wazero helpers for Shatter go_runtime_values keys api.FunctionDefinition and api.Function in internal/wasmgen. Local term: "type factory" maps to Shatter's existing go_runtime_values mechanism for exact Go type spellings; no new Zolem/Shatter config vocabulary added. Verification dry-run with /home/ketan/project/shatter/target/debug/shatter wrote /tmp/zolem-type-factories-dry.{md,json}; verified merge-preview dry-run wrote /tmp/zolem-type-factories-dry-preview.{md,json}. Preview summary total_functions_discovered=363, selected_function_count=331, skipped_unsupported_functions=32 versus issue baseline 34 unsupported. Removed skips are validateSignature/api.FunctionDefinition and callU32/api.Function. fixture.Selector remains unsupported because a single global go_runtime_values expression cannot be both same-package-safe for internal/fixture wrappers and import-qualified for external packages. parse.Node disposition: focused rg in cmd/ and internal/ found no parse.Node use, so no local Zolem work and no upstream issue filed from this evidence. interface{}/any disposition: dry-run still has no unsupported reason mentioning interface{} or any; existing any uses are JSON/test/admin paths, so no local work and no upstream dynamic-narrowing issue filed.

title: Wazero factories for Shatter
description:
Title: Wazero factories for Shatter
Tracker: Beads
Repo: zolem
Type: feature
Priority: 1
Labels: shatter,wasm,testing

## Problem

Shatter's latest Zolem scan still skips the wazero-backed fixture paths because it cannot synthesize live `wazero.Runtime`-backed values. The scan evidence after the recent Shatter skip fixes was:

- 360 discovered functions
- 287 attempted functions
- 73 skipped functions
- 12 skip reasons in the wazero runtime/module/API bucket

The skipped surface includes fixture loading and WASM selector paths that need real runtime-backed values rather than JSON-serializable structs.

The repo-local scan command is:

```bash
SHATTER_BIN=${HOME}/project/shatter/target/release/shatter make shatter
```

That target runs `scripts/shatter-full-scan.sh`, which writes `shatter-report/full-scan.md` and `shatter-report/full-scan.json`.

## Current Code Facts

- `internal/fixture/wasm.go` defines `type Runner struct { rt wazero.Runtime }`.
- `internal/fixture/wasm.go` already exposes `func NewRunner() *Runner`, which constructs a runner with `wazero.NewRuntime(context.Background())`.
- `(*Runner).Close()` closes the underlying runtime with `r.rt.Close(context.Background())`.
- `internal/wasmgen/generator.go` defines `type Generator struct { rt wazero.Runtime; compiled wazero.CompiledModule; timeout time.Duration }`.
- `internal/wasmgen/generator.go` exposes `Compile(wasmBytes []byte, timeout time.Duration) (*Generator, error)`, which creates a configured wazero runtime and compiled module from valid WASM bytes.
- Zolem's `.shatter/config.yaml` currently has defaults and LLM settings, but no generator/native-generator entries.
- Shatter's config format supports `defaults.generators` and `defaults.param_generators`; `shatter build-frontend go` collects `.go` files from those entries and compiles them into a custom Go frontend.
- Shatter Go native generators have signature `func(recipe json.RawMessage) generators.NativeGeneratorResult`, where the result carries an ID, the live value, and an optional recipe. Unknown native generator names currently fail with `native generator "<name>" not registered (custom build required)`.
- Shatter's current public terminology is `generators`, `native generators`, and `runtime values`. Calling this a "type factory" is reasonable as an issue-facing concept, but the implementation should use Shatter's existing generator/runtime-value extension mechanism unless that mechanism is extended separately.

Concrete skipped targets from `shatter-report/full-scan.md` include:

- `loadLocalFixtures`: `param "runner" -> inner -> field "rt" -> wazero.Runtime`
- `loadFixtures`: `param "runner" -> inner -> field "rt" -> wazero.Runtime`
- `buildLocalHandler`: `param "matcher" -> ... -> field "rt" -> wazero.Runtime`
- `buildLocalHandler`: `param "wasmGenerator" -> inner -> field "rt" -> wazero.Runtime`
- `Score`: `param "mod" -> fixture.CompiledModule`
- `newWASMSelector`: `param "mod" -> fixture.CompiledModule`

## Expected Outcome

Zolem supplies Shatter generator configuration and Go native generator code so Shatter can construct representative wazero-backed Zolem values during scans without changing production behavior.

Use Shatter's existing extension/plugin capability for Go native generators. If the current Shatter config only supports parameter-name generator hints rather than true type-keyed factories, configure the concrete skipped parameters that need these values instead of inventing a new config format in Zolem.

## Proposed Scope

- Add Zolem-owned Shatter generator source under a support location such as `.shatter/generators/` or the repo's established equivalent.
- Add `.shatter/config.yaml` entries for the relevant generator names/parameters.
- Provide at least a `*fixture.Runner` generator using `fixture.NewRunner()`.
- Provide a generator for `*wasmgen.Generator` only if a small valid WASM fixture can be supplied cheaply through `wasmgen.Compile`; otherwise leave that specific type as documented follow-up and keep the `Runner` support independently useful.
- Ensure generated runtime-backed objects have a cleanup/lifecycle story, for example by routing cleanup through existing `Close` methods or by documenting why the scan process can safely own the values until process exit.
- If `*wasmgen.Generator` is deferred, the issue is still complete only if at least the `runner`-backed skipped functions are attempted and the deferral is recorded in the issue comments or generator README.

## In Scope

- Zolem Shatter config and generator support files.
- Small test fixture assets needed to instantiate a representative WASM generator.
- Verification that Shatter can build and use the custom Go frontend against Zolem.

## Out of Scope

- Shatter engine changes for first-party `text/template` or `parse.Node` handling.
- Shatter engine changes for `interface{}` candidate narrowing.
- Production behavior changes to Zolem's fixture matching or WASM execution.

## Acceptance Checks

- `.shatter/config.yaml` includes the generator/native-generator entries needed for the wazero-backed Zolem values.
- `shatter build-frontend go` succeeds from the Zolem repo with the new generator support.
- `SHATTER_BIN=${HOME}/project/shatter/target/release/shatter make shatter` completes from the Zolem repo.
- The new scan attempts `loadLocalFixtures` or `loadFixtures`, which are currently skipped for `param "runner" -> inner -> field "rt" -> wazero.Runtime`.
- The wazero skip bucket drops below the current 12 skipped reasons without introducing new scan failures.
- The generator lifecycle is explicit in the generator source comments or a nearby README: either generated values are closed through a documented path, or process-lifetime ownership is justified for scan-only values.
acceptance_criteria: Generator config builds a custom Go frontend; make shatter attempts loadLocalFixtures/loadFixtures and wazero skips drop below 12.
notes:
Investigation blocker: current Shatter Go build-frontend copies configured .go generators into a separate shatter-custom-frontend/usergens module. A Zolem generator for fixture.NewRunner/wasmgen.Compile would need zolem.dev/zolem/internal imports, which Go rejects from that module. Also defaults.param_generators names are registered verbatim, so a concrete parameter generator for runner would generate usergens.runner and fail Go export/type checking. Zolem-side generator config cannot satisfy this issue until Shatter supports project-module generator builds, a name alias/sanitization path, or another runtime-value bridge for internal project types.
2026-05-31 update from latest Shatter main dry-run: total skipped functions are now 70; parse.Node skips are 0 after Shatter's first-party template/runtime-value support; interface/any skips are 14 after bounded JSON-encode candidates; wazero.Runtime skips are 13 and remain the main Zolem-owned extension/plugin factory gap. Terminology: 'type factory' is a useful umbrella for the desired behavior, but Shatter's current implemented terms are runtime values, native generators, and generator config. The actionable Zolem-side path remains supplying wazero-backed values through Shatter's extension/plugin/native-generator capabilities where possible; if blocked by current Shatter generator isolation/internal-import limits, the corresponding Shatter-side work should be linked before unblocking this issue. parse.Node should be tracked as a Shatter first-party specialized runtime/type factory pattern rather than Zolem-owned work. Remaining interface{} work should be treated separately as Shatter-side candidate generation/narrowing for dynamic JSON-like values, not as a wazero generator requirement.
2026-05-31 update after Shatter merge 96bd92330914855d55c82c518de758e9b7171ea9: Shatter main now supplies exact github.com/tetratelabs/wazero.Runtime as a first-party runtime value, so the remaining Zolem-side factory work should focus on Zolem-owned values that still need project/plugin/native-generator support rather than exact wazero.Runtime alone. Latest dry-run evidence from /tmp/zolem-str-ibba-dry3.json: 66 total skipped functions; wazero.Runtime skips 0; wazero.CompiledModule skips 12; fixture.CompiledModule appears in 18 skip reasons; interface/any skips 14; parse.Node skips 0. Terminology: "type factory" is acceptable as a design shorthand for values supplied by expected type, but current Shatter code/config terminology remains runtime values, native generators, generator config, and param_generators. parse.Node should be tracked as a Shatter first-party specialized runtime/type factory pattern, not Zolem-owned work, and current Zolem evidence has no parse.Node skips. interface{} / any should be handled as separate Shatter-side dynamic candidate generation and partial type-narrowing work, akin to dynamically typed JSON-like candidate values; it should not be bundled into the Wazero generator issue except as scan evidence.
2026-05-31 update: Shatter main now includes configurable exact Go runtime values via top-level .shatter/config.yaml go_runtime_values (landed in shatter f5a3c271bc8efe112f7ec2835882ef9f79d85e65, issue str-bu1r). Validation against a temporary Zolem clone with a fixture.CompiledModule entry reduced Shatter dry-run skips from 58 to 45, and fixture.CompiledModule skip references from 18 to 0. The Zolem-side work can now focus on adding a committed .shatter/config.yaml entry for fixture.CompiledModule using fixture.NewRunner().CompileWASM with a tiny deterministic WASM module. Remaining fixture-related skips are separate types such as fixture.Selector and fixture.sequenceKey, not fixture.CompiledModule.

title: zolem-4h3
zolem-x4s
zolem-8js
Document and test Gemini tool_config.mode=AUTO behavior (currently falls through to text)
description: In zolem-53m, Gemini tool synthesis only fires for mode=ANY. mode=AUTO falls through to lorem text with no test or documentation. An SDK expecting a function call in AUTO mode will get a text response. Add a test asserting the text-path behavior for AUTO, and document it in local-runtime.md or a comment.

title: Use atomic counter for chatcmpl streaming ID instead of UnixNano (collision under concurrent requests)
description: streamToolCallCompletions in internal/provider/openai/sse.go generates chatcmpl-zolem<timestamp> using time.Now().UnixNano(). Two concurrent goroutines at the same nanosecond get identical IDs. Switch to an atomic counter (as toolCallID already uses) for uniqueness.

title: Improve wasmContentBackend error messages to avoid internal phrasing in client-visible 502s
description: wasmContentBackend returns fmt.Errorf (not *BackendError) for nil-generator and nil-FixtureMatch cases. The raw message 'wasm generator is not configured' surfaces in client 502 responses. Wrap these as BackendError with a cleaner user-facing message (e.g. 'the requested backend is not available'). Found during zolem-amn review.

title: materialize_seed_for_plan drops hint seeds for functions with context.Context params
description:
## Symptom

`.shatter/config.yaml` `defaults:` hints for string/byte parameters are
silently ignored for functions that also take a `context.Context` (or any
other parameter resolved as `RuntimeValue` by the planner).

`HTTPChatCompletion(ctx context.Context, upstream string, ...)` with
`upstream: "http://localhost:11434"` hint → all explored paths use
`upstream=""`.

## Root cause

`materialize_value()` in `shatter-core/src/planner_consumer.rs` returns
`None` for `ValuePlanKind::RuntimeValue`. `materialize_seed_for_plan`
uses `?` on the result, so when the planner emits a `runtime_value` plan
for `ctx` (Go wrapper bakes `context.Background()` at compile time), the
whole seed is dropped — taking the valid `Literal` plans for `upstream`
and `model` with it.

The actual input array already has `null` at the ctx slot confirming that
passing `null` for RuntimeValue params is correct.

## Fix

Emit `Value::Null` for `RuntimeValue` in `materialize_value` so
Literal/Zero plans for sibling params survive.

## Files

- `shatter-core/src/planner_consumer.rs` — `materialize_value`, line ~334

title: Converge admin {"error":"..."} and data-plane {"zolem_error":"..."} error envelopes to one shape
description: After zolem-cx7, two error envelope shapes still coexist: admin uses {"error":"msg"} and data-plane infra uses {"zolem_error":"msg"}. zolem-cx7 deferred this convergence. Pick one canonical shape and migrate callers. Reduces the number of shapes clients need to handle.

title: Return 404 (not 400) for unknown data-plane paths in provider NotFound handlers
description: zolem-cx7 added NotFound handlers to all three provider chi muxes, but they call writeInvalidRequest which returns HTTP 400. An unknown path should return 404 with a provider-native JSON body. OpenAI-compatible clients that check for 404 to detect unsupported endpoints will misclassify the error.

title: Expose -stream-delay-seed flag in zolemc profiles create
description: StreamDelay.Seed (*int64) was added to the wire type in zolem-g6n but no corresponding -stream-delay-seed CLI flag was exposed in zolemc. Seed-controlled stream pacing requires direct curl to set. Add the flag alongside the other -stream-delay-* flags.

title: Block link-local/cloud-metadata ranges even when allow_external_ollama_upstream is set
description: ollamaUpstreamHostIsPrivate returns nil immediately when profile.AllowExternalOllamaUpstream is true, bypassing all IP checks. This means a user with that flag enabled can point ollama_upstream at 169.254.169.254 (AWS/GCP/Azure cloud metadata endpoint) or any other link-local address, enabling SSRF to instance metadata. Fix: even when the opt-out is active, still reject link-local (169.254.0.0/16), the IPv6 link-local range (fe80::/10), and cloud-metadata addresses before returning nil. For hostname targets, DNS-resolve all returned addresses and check each against the denied ranges. Found by automated security review of zolem-lku.

title: Strip port from AllowedHosts entries to avoid silent 403 when user includes port
description: HostHeaderAllowed strips the port from the incoming Host header before comparing against AllowedHosts entries. If a user specifies an entry with a port (e.g. 'zolem.test:8090'), it will never match the stripped host and every request will be rejected with 403 — silently, with no error message. Fix: normalize allowlist entries by stripping ports on load, or document that allowlist values must be bare hostnames.

title: Expand Gemini vendored schema to allow non-text Part types (functionCall, inlineData)
description: The vendored gemini-v1.discovery.json schema marks Part.text as the only valid Part type (required). This causes zolem to reject valid Gemini requests containing functionCall or inlineData parts with a 400. The real Gemini API accepts multi-modal and tool-use requests. Fix the vendored schema to allow all Part variants. Related to zolem-53m (tool-call synthesis). Found during zolem-e9h code review.

title: Add .shatter/config.yaml defaults hints for low-coverage functions
description:
Several functions score <25% line coverage because shatter generates empty strings/bytes for key parameters, causing early-exit errors before exploring the main code paths.

Target functions and their blockers:
- NormalizeGeminiDiscovery: data=[] (empty JSON parse fails) and version=''
- NormalizeOpenAPI: data=[] and provider='', version=''
- (OpenAPINormalizer).Normalize: source.Kind='' causes 'not implemented' error, raw=[]
- (PassThroughNormalizer).Normalize: source.Kind='' causes 'not implemented' error
- (*Validator).LoadRaw: provider='', version='', data=[]
- (*Validator).Validate: provider='', version='', body=[]
- HTTPChatCompletion/HTTPChatCompletionStream: upstream='' causes 'unsupported protocol scheme'

Fix: add per-function 'defaults' entries in .shatter/config.yaml with valid seed values:
- version/provider/model strings with realistic values
- data/body/raw []byte params seeded with base64-encoded valid JSON documents from testdata/

Zolem config changes only; no production code touched.

title: str-r2q7: scan parallel path missing planner seeds (landed)
description:
Root cause: `fetch_planner_seeds_for_scan` was committed inside `pub async fn scan`
(~line 1700), which is never called by the CLI. The live path is
`parallel_scan_with_progress → run_phased → explore_single_function →
explore_with_scan_mode`. Result: all configured defaults and string-literal boundary
seeds from `.shatter/config.yaml` were silently dropped during `shatter scan`.
Fix: Relocated `fetch_planner_seeds_for_scan` into `explore_single_function` (the
live path) so ALL targets (free functions + methods) receive configured `candidate_inputs`
and `default_execute_plan`. Also eliminated a redundant `GetInvocationPlan` RPC that
would have fired twice for method targets.
Related: str-r2q7.1 (backfill Analysis.SourceFile in buildTargetContext), str-79t9
(wire hint planner into custom frontends via frontendsetup).
Landed: commit 051e85be on main.

title: AGENTS.md governance fixes: supersede the beads session-completion block, forbid unsandboxed shatter scan, override the global 0.0.0.0 bind rule, name the verification gate
description:
**Current facts:**
- AGENTS.md:94-118 (vendored beads block, hash-marked) mandates "Work is NOT complete until git push succeeds... NEVER stop before pushing... resolve and retry until it succeeds" — conflicting with global branch discipline (no code changes on main) and bento land-work's ownership of merge/push. Observed damage: direct-to-main code commits 2beab60 (+138 LoC Go), 1813ff3, 6fede1b, c2c58b5; duplicate close commits d41b70b/e7584e5 (both "Close zolem-n0a").
- Zolem is loopback-only by design (README:140; commit 1813ff3 enforces it) but the user-global "Running Servers: bind 0.0.0.0" rule is unconditional and AGENTS.md never overrides it locally.
- Nothing in AGENTS.md forbids direct `shatter scan` (see zolem-vdt evidence); the sandboxed path exists (`make shatter`, scripts/shatter-full-scan.sh with write guard).
- AGENTS.md:53 references an undefined "relevant zolem verification gate" (fixed by zolem-rok's `make check`; reference it here).

**Recommendation:** add a section outside the vendored beads markers stating: (1) on conflict, repo branch/landing rules supersede the beads session-completion text — push applies to tracker-sync commits and already-landed branches only, never license for direct-to-main code commits; (2) never invoke `shatter scan` directly — use `make shatter`/the sandbox script; (3) zolem is loopback-only by design; ignore the global 0.0.0.0 binding instruction here; (4) the verification gate is `make check`.

**Acceptance checks:** AGENTS.md contains the four rules outside the vendored block; wording does not get clobbered by `bd` regeneration (verify markers).

title: One-time working-tree cleanup + Shatter escape guardrails in-repo
description:
**Current facts (root cause identified):**
- ~30 junk untracked files in repo root are Shatter fuzz inputs escaping to host cwd: the longest filename is exactly the error-format literal at `cmd/zolem/recording.go:227` ("open calls file %q: %w"); prefixes ("op", "ope", ..., "open calls file %q: %") are input-mutation steps; each candidate passed to `newJSONLRecorder(path)` created a 0-byte file. Others: `Bob Smith`, `192.168.1.42`, `qelmm`, `a`, `ab`, `" "`. Timestamps May 25 - Jun 8 = recurring unsandboxed runs.
- `off` (53KB) contains Shatter scan-resume state JSON (flag/redirect accident).
- Untracked `internal/admincli/.shatter/seeds/pool.json` proves direct `shatter scan` outside the sandboxed `scripts/shatter-full-scan.sh` path; .gitignore's `.shatter/` patterns are root-anchored and miss nested dirs.
- Root `zolem` (31.3MB) is gitignored `go build` output from May 24; `.codex` is an empty stray; `/home/ketan/project/.json` contains `{"findings": []}` (empty-$NAME output-path accident, outside this repo but same cleanup pass).
- `make shatter-clean` does not touch `.shatter-cache/` (259MB) or `shatter-artifacts/` (45MB).

**Recommendation:** delete the junk files, `off`, `.codex`, root binary; add `**/.shatter/` (or `.shatter/` non-anchored with tracked-config exception) to .gitignore; add a Makefile `build` target outputting to `bin/` (gitignored); extend `shatter-clean` to the cache/artifacts dirs; `git worktree prune`. The behavioral guardrails live in companion issues: shatter default-deny host writes (shatter tracker) and AGENTS.md rules (zolem-246).

**Acceptance checks:** `git status --porcelain` clean on main; `make shatter-clean` reclaims the cache dirs; nested .shatter dirs ignored.

**Note:** the file deletions are host-state-specific and must run on the development machine where the junk exists (the .gitignore/Makefile changes are normal repo work).

---
close_reason: Landed on origin/main as merge 6da8eb2 (commit c382fbc). Added **/.shatter/* gitignore guardrail (preserving tracked .shatter/config.yaml) + bin/; added Makefile build target -> bin/; extended shatter-clean to .shatter-cache/ and shatter-artifacts/; deleted escaped fuzz junk + stray zolem binary; git worktree prune. NOTE: a teammate is actively re-running Shatter in the primary checkout, so root fuzz files regenerate live — root-cause recorder fix is a separate issue.

title: Restructure user docs: split local-runtime.md, fix the self-contradicting fixture-matcher guidance, de-dupe README/INSTALL, canonical mode names
description:
**Current facts:**
- docs/local-runtime.md (28KB): "Call Recording" (line 269) and a ~130-line Bubblewrap recipe (line 299) are nested under "## Fixture Backend"; fixture file-layout prose resumes at line 432 after the digression.
- The deprecation story contradicts itself: line 444 deprecates `match.cel`; the "Minimal example" at 462 teaches it; line 544 calls the deprecated per-fixture CEL mechanism "recommended" while the current mechanism is fixtures.yaml expressions. A new author following the page adopts the deprecated path.
- README.md:33-85,325-408 duplicate INSTALL.md nearly verbatim and have already drifted (README pins :v0.1.0 in examples; INSTALL uses :latest). INSTALL.md:160 promises a "full flag reference" in README that doesn't exist anywhere.
- Mode terminology drifts across "local runtime mode", "fixed-listener mode", "admin mode", "local admin server"; the README "Response modes" table actually lists backends.
- Agent/contributor content (refute README:237-264, Shatter README:293-323) sits between user quick starts and the TLS guide; docs/ has no index; specs live in specs/, docs/specs/, and docs/superpowers/specs/ (the latter violating the documentation-paths rule — fold the move into this restructure).

**Recommendation (target structure):** README = what/why + positioning vs generic HTTP mocks + two canonical mode names + one quick start per mode + backends table + short install pointer. INSTALL.md = sole owner of platforms/verification/Docker/nightly. docs/README.md index. Split local-runtime.md into admin-mode guide (incl. call history per zolem-y2d), fixed-listener guide, fixture authoring guide (fixtures.yaml first, deprecated matchers in an appendix), admin API reference, recipes/ (bubblewrap). Move refute/Shatter sections to AGENTS.md or CONTRIBUTING. Consolidate specs dirs.

**Acceptance checks:** no deprecated mechanism taught as the primary path; no duplicated install content; every doc reachable from the index; fixture guide is linear.

**Scope:** size: large — sequence as 2-3 PRs, and the FIRST must be the fixture-matcher contradiction fix (lines 444/462/544 of docs/local-runtime.md), since it actively teaches a deprecated mechanism today; the rest of the restructure can follow. Out: prose-quality rewrites of wasm-modules.md/testing-e2e.md (already good).

---

title: Document admin-mode call history (API + zolemc) in user docs; fix the story's false doc evidence
description:
**Current facts:**
- Implemented but absent from all user docs: `GET/DELETE /_zolem/listeners/<name>/calls` (`cmd/zolem/local_admin.go:385-423`), listener payload caps `record_request_body_cap_bytes` / `record_response_body_cap_bytes` / `record_stream_event_cap` (local_admin.go:39-47), and `zolemc listeners calls list/clear` (cmd/zolemc/main.go:293-417).
- README and docs/local-runtime.md cover only fixed-listener JSONL recording; the design spec (`docs/specs/2026-05-22-call-history-design.md`) calls post-hoc assertions the primary consumer use case.
- `docs/stories/zolemc-call-history-list.md` lists README.md as Docs evidence — currently false.

**Recommendation:** add a call-history section (endpoints, cap fields, zolemc commands, -since semantics) to the admin-mode doc, one README example, and correct the story evidence. Document the status display convention: for streamed responses, `zolemc listeners calls list` prefixes the HTTP status with `~` (e.g. `~200`) to indicate the status was sent before streaming completed — currently undocumented. Coordinate with zolem-qfi's restructure if concurrent.

**Acceptance checks:** a user can discover and use call history from README/docs alone; storystore audit on that story passes.

---

title: E2E harness: stop per-service full-module rebuilds (~415s package), reap orphaned servers, un-hardcode port 18090
description:
**Current facts:**
- `cmd/zolem` tests take ~415s wall / ~30 CPU-min because the harness sets a fresh GOCACHE per spawned service and `go run .`s a full rebuild: `local_runtime_e2e_test.go:216`, `integration_helpers_test.go:79`, `tls_listener_e2e_test.go:339`, `cmd/zolemc/main_e2e_test.go:752,782`. Zero `t.Parallel`. `local_calls_e2e_test.go:28` already demonstrates the build-once pattern.
- Interrupted runs strand spawned servers: ~97 orphaned zolem processes accumulated on this host during review runs.
- `scripts/test-local-runtime.sh` hardcodes admin port 18090 and collides with any running admin server; full run took ~8 minutes.
- `pickPort` is duplicated in two packages and has a close-then-rebind TOCTOU (latent until t.Parallel).

**Recommendation:** build each binary once per package (TestMain or sync.Once) with the normal GOCACHE; run spawned servers in a process group killed via t.Cleanup so interrupts reap children; make the script's admin port overridable/dynamic; share pickPort or replace with listener-fd handoff.

**Acceptance checks:** `go test ./cmd/...` wall time drops by roughly an order of magnitude; killing a test run mid-flight leaves zero zolem processes; the e2e script passes alongside a running admin server.

---

title: Error-response consistency: stop leaking Go internals, JSON 404s, Content-Type on zolemerr, converge error envelope shapes
description:
**Current facts (verified):**
- Malformed request bodies return raw Go decoder text: `invalid JSON: json: cannot unmarshal string into Go struct field ChatCompletionRequest.messages of type []openai.Message`; admin API echoes `json: unknown field "bakend"` verbatim.
- Anthropic schema failures leak `jsonschema validation failed with 'mem://anthropic-v1.json#'`.
- Unknown data-plane paths return Go's plain-text `404 page not found`, not a provider-native JSON error.
- `internal/zolemerr/error.go:8-12` `Write` sets `X-Zolem-Error` but never Content-Type, so JSON bodies get sniffed.
- Three error body shapes coexist: admin `{"error":"msg"}`, data-plane infra `{"zolem_error":"msg"}`, provider-native `{"error":{...}}`. The zolem-internal writer is also implemented three times (`internal/zolemerr/error.go:8`, `internal/response/generator.go:23`, `cmd/zolem/startup.go:408`), all hardcoding 502.
- zolemc connection failures print raw dial errors with no hint (`dial tcp 127.0.0.1:9999: connect: connection refused`).

**Recommendation:** map validation failures to provider-native error envelopes/messages (no Go type names, no mem:// URIs); provider-native JSON 404 for unknown paths; set Content-Type in zolemerr and consolidate the three writers; converge admin + infra envelopes on one shape; wrap zolemc dial failures with "is the zolem admin server running at <url>? Start it with zolem -local-admin-addr ...".

**Acceptance checks:** no Go decode/type strings reach any client-visible error; unknown-path 404 is provider-native JSON; one internal error writer; zolemc connection error names the likely fix.

**Scope:** size medium-large; may split provider-native validation messages from the envelope consolidation.

---

title: Fixed-listener mode silently accepts -local-backend error and serves lorem success responses
description:
**Symptom:** README.md:31 documents the `error` backend as "always returns a provider-native error," but `zolem -local-provider anthropic -local-backend error` serves normal lorem 200 responses.

**Current code facts:** `cmd/zolem/startup.go:380` (`generatorForBackend`) maps `error` to the lorem generator in fixed mode; there is no `-local-error-type` flag, and `ForcedErrorTypeForRequest` (`internal/runtime/error_type.go:15`) requires a non-empty ErrorType to trigger. (`-local-backend wasm` at least fails at startup for lack of a module flag.)

**Recommendation:** either reject `error` in fixed-listener mode with a clear message, or add `-local-error-type` and make it work. Rejecting is the smaller correct fix; pick at implementation time unless the maintainer states a preference.

**Acceptance checks:** fixed mode with `-local-backend error` either errors at startup or returns provider-native errors; README matches.

---

title: `zolemc listener state` reports the requested address (127.0.0.1:0), not the bound address
description:
**Symptom (verified):** after creating a listener with default addr `127.0.0.1:0` (actual bind e.g. :44409), `listener state` shows `listener=127.0.0.1:0` while `zolemc listeners list` correctly shows the bound port.

**Current code facts:** `cmd/zolem/local_admin.go:225` builds the runtime app from the pre-bind spec; `actualSpec.Addr = server.Addr()` happens at :248-249, after the handler captured the runtime config.

**Recommendation:** bind first and build the app from the resolved spec, or inject the resolved addr into the runtime config post-bind.

**Acceptance checks:** `listener state` shows the bound host:port; e2e asserts state addr == base_url addr.

---

title: zolemc -json output silently drops fields; same command emits different shapes with/without -since
description:
**Symptom (verified):**
- `zolemc -json profiles list` omits `stream_delay` (and other fields) that the admin API returns, because output is re-marshaled through narrow view structs.
- `zolemc listeners calls list -json -since N` re-marshals through `recordedCallView` keeping only id/method/path/status — request/response headers and bodies vanish; the same command **without** `-since` passes raw API bytes through. Same data, two shapes, lossy in one.

**Current code facts:** view structs at `cmd/zolemc/main.go:307-319` and `348-362`; profile view lacks stream_delay/ollama_upstream (related: zolem-g6n).

**Recommendation:** for `-json`, emit the raw (or filtered-but-complete) API JSON; reserve view structs for human-readable output only. Add a test asserting `-json` output round-trips every field the API returned.

**Acceptance checks:** `-json` outputs are field-complete and identical in shape with and without `-since`.

---

title: `zolemc -h` lists no subcommands, prints `flag: help requested`, exits 1
description:
**Symptom (verified):** `zolemc -h` prints only the four global flags, no command list, then `flag: help requested`, exit code 1. The hand-written command list appears only via `zolemc help` (exit 0) or after an error.

**Current code facts:** `cmd/zolemc/main.go:78-85` uses `flag.ContinueOnError` and propagates `flag.ErrHelp` as a fatal error; the FlagSet's default usage is never replaced with the hand-written `usage()` at main.go:578.

**Recommendation:** set `fs.Usage` to the full usage func; translate `flag.ErrHelp` into printing usage on stdout and exiting 0. Also document the undocumented global `-timeout` flag (main.go:83) in the usage text while there.

**Acceptance checks:** `zolemc -h` prints global flags + full command list, exit 0; `zolemc help` unchanged.

title: Harden the no-egress posture: Host-header validation on admin/data listeners; restrict ollama_upstream to loopback/private ranges by default
description:
**Current code facts:**
- The admin API is auth-less (documented, fine for loopback) but does no Host-header validation (`cmd/zolem/local_admin.go:357`) — a DNS-rebinding page can drive it from a browser.
- `ollama_upstream` accepts any http(s) host (`internal/runtime/store.go:237-252`), so any local process or rebinding attacker can configure Zolem to forward request bodies (prompts) to an arbitrary external host — the one hole in an otherwise consistently loopback-only design that docs/local-runtime.md's bubblewrap no-egress recipes market.

**Recommendation:** reject requests whose Host is not a loopback literal (or configured allowlist) on admin and data listeners; default ollama_upstream validation to loopback/RFC1918 with an explicit opt-out flag.

**Acceptance checks:** admin request with `Host: evil.example` is rejected; profile create with public ollama_upstream is rejected without the opt-out; existing local flows pass.

---

title: Admin control-plane concurrency defects: UpsertListener leaks servers on concurrent replace; profile TOCTOU; shutdown never force-closes; ListListeners nil-index
description:
**Current code facts:**
- `cmd/zolem/local_admin.go:230-283`: `UpsertListener` releases `c.mu` between tearing down the old listener and registering the new one; two concurrent PUTs to one name both pass teardown, both bind, and the second map insert (line 276) orphans the first `http.Server` + `net.Listener` (goroutine + port leak until exit). Fixed-port replacement can also spuriously fail.
- `local_admin.go:183` checks `GetProfile`, then line 198 re-fetches discarding ok — a concurrent DeleteProfile in between lets the listener bind with a zero-valued profile (ErrProfileInUse can't trip because the listener isn't registered yet).
- `local_admin.go:597-601`: `Close` does a 2s `Shutdown` with no `server.Close()` fallback; an active SSE stream with stream_delay outlives shutdown.
- `local_admin.go:295-301`: `ListListeners` indexes `c.listeners[spec.Name]` without a nil check; store and listener map are updated under different critical sections.

**Recommendation:** hold the lock across the whole replace (bind new first, then swap-and-close-old in one critical section, or serialize per-name); snapshot the profile once; add `server.Close()` after Shutdown deadline; nil-guard or unify the two maps' locking.

**Acceptance checks:** a concurrent-PUT stress test passes with no leaked goroutines (goleak or listener-count assertion); shutdown completes with an in-flight delayed SSE stream.

---

title: Single source of truth for admin wire types; zolemc cannot express ollama_upstream or stream_delay
description:
**Current code facts:**
- Payload/view structs are duplicated between `cmd/zolem/local_admin.go:25-60` and `cmd/zolemc/main.go:21-67`, already drifting: the zolemc copy lacks `ollama_upstream` and `stream_delay`, so creating an ollama-backed profile with an upstream via zolemc is impossible (curl required).
- `internal/admincli` (recent extraction) contains only `Options` + a generic JSON HTTP client — it did not take the types.

**Recommendation:** create `internal/adminapi` owning all admin request/response types; use it from the server handlers, zolemc, and admincli. Add zolemc flags `-ollama-upstream` and stream-delay flags (or a documented JSON passthrough). Keeps server/CLI from drifting again.

**Acceptance checks:** one definition of each wire type; zolemc can create a profile with ollama_upstream and stream_delay end-to-end; e2e covers it.

---

title: Wire or delete dead production code: fixture watcher, ollama CLI client, internal/router, profile seed, /v1beta branch
description:
**Current code facts (each has zero production callers/effect):**
- `internal/fixture/watcher.go` (fsnotify hot reload): tested, never started; fixtures load once at listener creation (`cmd/zolem/startup.go:261-272`). docs/local-runtime.md:576 still says "fails startup or hot reload". fsnotify is a production dependency for nothing.
- `internal/ollama/client.go` (309-line CLI-exec client): never constructed; `buildLocalHandler` (`startup.go:341-343`) passes nil TextGenerator, so every handler's `generateText` fallback branch is dead. The HTTP client is the only live path.
- `internal/router`: 4-line vestige of the abandoned virtual-host design; all three providers import it for `labelsFromContext`, but nothing ever sets `router.LabelsKey` — labels are permanently empty, including as exposed to CEL/WASM fixture matchers.
- Profile `seed` (`internal/runtime/runtime.go:12`) and zolemc `-seed`/`-seed-set` (cmd/zolemc/main.go:162-163): stored and echoed, never read by any generator (only `StreamDelay.Seed` does anything).
- `internal/provider/anthropic/handler.go:65` checks `strings.HasPrefix(path, "/v1beta")` but only `/v1/messages` is registered.

**Recommendation:** delete each unless there is a concrete near-term plan to wire it (decide per item at pickup; deletion is the default). Update docs/local-runtime.md hot-reload claim and remove `-seed`/`-seed-set` help text accordingly.

**Coordination:** the `internal/router` deletion and ollama-CLI-client deletion touch the same provider handler files as zolem-amn (ContentBackend refactor); sequence this either before zolem-amn (shrinks the refactor) or fold those two items into zolem-amn.

**Acceptance checks:** `go build`/tests pass; no doc references remain to deleted features; fsnotify removed from go.mod if watcher deleted.

---

title: Extract a shared ContentBackend seam: provider handlers are near-triplicated (~1,300 lines)
description:
**Current code facts:**
- `internal/provider/anthropic/handler.go` (424 lines), `openai/handler.go` (406), `gemini/handler.go` (477) each hand-roll the same backend dispatch chain (error → fixture → ollama → wasm → lorem/faker) with structurally identical `tokenize`, `labelsFromContext`, `renderFixtureBody`, `generateText`, `generateWASM`, `handleOllamaBackend`, `handleOllamaStream`; ollama SSE envelope emission is copied three times (e.g. `anthropic/handler.go:346-409`).
- Within `openai/handler.go`, the ChatCompletionResponse construction block repeats 4x (124-131, 144-151, 165-171, 324-331).
- Backend selection happens per-request via context value (`runtimecfg.BackendForRequest`) though the backend is statically known at listener-build time.
- The WASM backend already proves the seam: backends produce content chunks ([]string); providers own envelopes.
- Adding a backend currently also touches `internal/runtime/store.go:180` (`ValidateProfile`) and `cmd/zolem/startup.go:374` (`generatorForBackend`).

**Recommendation:** define a `ContentBackend` interface (generate text/chunks + optional stream) in a new internal package, resolved once at listener construction; reduce each provider handler to parse → validate → envelope. Fix ctx-not-first params (`handleOllamaStream(w, ctx, ...)`) and the variadic-as-optional `wasmGenerator ...*wasmgen.Generator` (`openai/handler.go:34` and siblings) during the extraction.

**Acceptance checks:** all existing provider/backend e2e tests pass unchanged; ollama SSE logic exists once; line count of the three handlers drops substantially; adding a hypothetical backend touches one registry, not three handlers.

**Scope:** refactor only — no behavior change. Size: large; do after zolem-4n5-zolem-53m land or coordinate to avoid conflicts.

---

title: CI gates are thin: add go vet, gofmt check, -race; create `make check`; define the canonical "verification gate" in AGENTS.md
description:
**Current code facts:**
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` runs `go test ./...`, two builds, and `scripts/smoke.sh`. No vet, no gofmt check, no race detector, no lint, no `go mod tidy -diff`.
- Result: 3 files currently fail `gofmt -l`: `cmd/zolem/integration_helpers_test.go`, `cmd/zolem/tls_listener_e2e_test.go`, `internal/provider/openai/types.go`.
- Makefile has only `smoke` and `shatter` targets; AGENTS.md:53 and README.md:258 tell agents to run "the relevant zolem verification gate" but no gate is defined anywhere.

**Recommendation:** `gofmt -w` the 3 files; add `make check` = vet + gofmt-diff-check + `go test ./...` (and `-race` at least for internal packages); run `make check` in CI; reference `make check` from AGENTS.md and README. Consider golangci-lint as a follow-up, not a blocker.

**Acceptance checks:** CI fails on a deliberately unformatted file; AGENTS.md names the gate; `make check` passes on main.

---

title: Add ancillary provider endpoints: /v1/models (OpenAI + Anthropic), Anthropic count_tokens, Gemini countTokens
description:
**Symptom (verified):** `/v1/models` 404s on both OpenAI and Anthropic listeners; Anthropic `/v1/messages/count_tokens` and Gemini `:countTokens` 404. SDK init paths, model pickers, and token-budgeting code hit these endpoints and fail. Unknown paths also return Go's plain-text `404 page not found` instead of a provider-native error (tracked in zolem-cx7).

**Recommendation:** static, cheap implementations — model list with a few plausible entries (respecting `response_model_policy` where sensible); count_tokens returning the same deterministic tokenizer estimate the handlers already use for usage fields.

**Acceptance checks:** official SDK model-list calls succeed against mock listeners; count_tokens returns a well-formed response; documented in the endpoint reference.

**Scope:** in: the four endpoints above. Out: embeddings, /v1/completions (legacy), realtime API — file separately if demanded.

---

title: Shared provider generator interfaces
description:
## What

Consolidate duplicated provider-local generator interfaces into shared constructible interfaces so Shatter and tests can instantiate Anthropic, Gemini, and OpenAI handlers with safe fake backend clients.

## Current code facts

- `internal/provider/anthropic/handler.go`, `internal/provider/gemini/handler.go`, and `internal/provider/openai/handler.go` each define package-local unexported `textGenerator` and `chatGenerator` interfaces.
- The three `textGenerator` interfaces have the same shape: `Generate(context.Context, string) (string, error)`.
- The three `chatGenerator` interfaces have the same shape: `NonStreaming(ctx context.Context, upstream string, messages []ollama.ChatMessage, model string) (string, error)` and `Streaming(ctx context.Context, upstream string, messages []ollama.ChatMessage, model string, fn func(delta string) error) error`.
- Each provider `NewHandler` accepts `response.Generator`, the provider-local `textGenerator`, and the provider-local `chatGenerator`.
- Because these interfaces are unexported and duplicated per provider package, Shatter needs package-local fake values for each provider instead of one reusable factory.

## Proposed change

Introduce shared internal provider backend interfaces and safe fake implementations. One acceptable shape:

- `internal/provider/backend.TextGenerator`
- `internal/provider/backend.ChatGenerator`
- `internal/provider/backend.NewNoopTextGenerator(...)`
- `internal/provider/backend.NewNoopChatGenerator(...)`

Then update provider handlers to accept the shared interfaces while preserving behavior. The fake implementations should not contact Ollama or any upstream service.

## Acceptance checks

- Anthropic, Gemini, and OpenAI handlers can all be constructed with the same shared fake backend values.
- Existing runtime behavior with the real Ollama adapters remains unchanged.
- Shatter can configure one reusable factory for text generation and one for chat generation.
- `go test ./...` passes.

## In scope

- Introducing a shared internal provider backend interface package or equivalent common location.
- Updating the three provider handlers to use the shared interface names.
- Adding no-op or deterministic fake backend implementations for tests/tooling.

## Out of scope

- Changing provider HTTP API behavior.
- Reworking Ollama integration.
- Changing generated response text semantics.

## Coverage value

This should unlock provider handler construction paths and avoid one-off Shatter factories for each unexported provider-local interface.

title: Fixture selector factory surface
description:
## What

Provide first-class fixture selector and response generator construction surfaces so Shatter can instantiate matcher and provider handler dependencies directly.

## Current code facts

- `internal/fixture/selector.go` exports `Selector`, which has `Select(ctx context.Context, req MatchRequest, candidates []Fixture) (*Fixture, error)`.
- `internal/fixture/selector.go` exports `NewLegacySelector(runner *Runner) *LegacySelector`.
- `internal/fixture/matcher.go` exports `NewMatcher(runner *Runner, fixtures []Fixture, selector Selector) *Matcher`; nil selector falls back to a legacy selector.
- `internal/fixture/matcher.go` exports `(*Matcher).SwapWithSelector(fixtures []Fixture, selector Selector)`.
- `internal/response/generator.go` exports `Generator`, and `internal/response/faker.go` / `internal/response/lorem.go` expose `NewFakerGenerator()` and `NewLoremGenerator()`.
- Shatter can use the response generator constructors, but the selector side is still awkward because the cheapest selector value is either nil at specific call sites or a legacy selector tied to runner state.

## Proposed change

Add a deliberately simple fixture selector constructor suitable for tests, scan tools, and extension/plugin integrations. One acceptable shape:

- `fixture.NewStaticSelector(index int) Selector`, selecting a deterministic candidate when present
- or `fixture.NewNoopSelector() Selector`, returning nil without error

Document that this selector is intended for tests/tooling and not fixture matching policy. If useful, add a helper that returns a complete matcher dependency pair:

- `fixture.NewEmptyMatcher() *Matcher`
- or `fixture.NewMatcherWithStaticSelector(runner *Runner) *Matcher`

## Acceptance checks

- Shatter can construct a non-nil `fixture.Selector` through a simple exported expression.
- Shatter can construct `fixture.Matcher` dependencies without relying on package-local nil special cases.
- Existing fixture selection semantics remain unchanged unless callers explicitly opt into the new helper.
- `go test ./...` passes.

## In scope

- Adding one or two small exported fixture helpers.
- Adding focused tests for deterministic selector behavior.
- Documenting that the helper is for tests/tooling.

## Out of scope

- Replacing `LegacySelector`.
- Changing fixture YAML semantics or matching policy.
- Changing provider request behavior.

## Coverage value

This should unlock matcher and fixture routing paths, and it is a prerequisite for cleaner Shatter coverage of provider handler construction.

title: Startup deps scan constructors
description:
## What

Add safe constructors for local startup dependencies and recording dependencies so Shatter can exercise local runtime setup without starting real listeners or touching real persistent resources by default.

## Current code facts

- `cmd/zolem/startup.go` defines unexported `startupDeps` with function fields for validator creation, spec fetching, runner creation, response generators, file reads, listener startup, TLS listener startup, and logging.
- `startupDeps.withDefaults()` fills nil fields with production defaults such as `specs.NewValidator`, `specs.NewFetcherWithFallback`, `fixture.NewRunner`, `response.NewLoremGenerator`, `response.NewFakerGenerator`, `os.ReadFile`, `http.ListenAndServe`, `http.ListenAndServeTLS`, and `log.Printf`.
- Local startup helpers accept `startupDeps` directly: `runLocal`, `buildLocalStartupApp`, `buildLocalStartupAppWithRecorder`, and `buildLocalStartupAppForRuntime`.
- `cmd/zolem/recording.go` defines exported `Recorder`, unexported `newInMemoryRecorder(listener string)`, unexported `noopRecorder`, and `recordingMiddleware(recorder Recorder, caps RecordCaps)`.
- Shatter currently cannot construct `startupDeps` or `Recorder` values cleanly, so local startup/control-plane and recording paths are skipped or partially blocked.

## Proposed change

Expose a small scan/test-safe construction surface for these dependencies. One acceptable approach:

- add `newScanStartupDeps()` or an exported equivalent in an internal package
- make it use no-op `listen` and `listenTLS` functions that record the provided handler and return nil instead of binding sockets
- make it use deterministic `response` generators and a bounded/no-op spec fetcher
- add a constructible recorder helper such as `NewInMemoryRecorder(listener string)` or `NewNoopRecorder()`

The production `withDefaults()` behavior should remain unchanged.

## Acceptance checks

- Shatter can construct a startup dependency value without real network binding.
- Shatter can construct a `Recorder` implementation without file I/O.
- Existing local runtime behavior remains unchanged for normal CLI use.
- Existing recording behavior remains unchanged.
- `go test ./...` passes.

## In scope

- Adding constructors or moving dependency types to a reusable internal package.
- Adding tests for the new constructors where useful.
- Using no-op listener functions for scan/test-safe startup paths.

## Out of scope

- Reworking local runtime semantics.
- Starting real local listeners from Shatter factories.
- Changing JSONL recording behavior.

## Coverage value

This should unlock roughly seven startup/control-plane paths plus several recording-related paths. It also provides the safe foundation needed before Shatter executes more local runtime code.

title: CLI admin values for Shatter
description:
## What

Make the `zolemc` command dependency values constructible from a stable internal package so Shatter and tests can exercise admin/listener/profile command helpers without needing to synthesize unexported `package main` structs.

## Current code facts

- `cmd/zolemc/main.go` defines unexported `rootOptions` with `adminURL`, `baseURL`, `json`, and `timeout` fields.
- `cmd/zolemc/main.go` defines unexported `adminClient` with `baseURL` and `http *http.Client` fields.
- The following command helpers accept these concrete unexported types directly: `runAdminHealth`, `runProfiles`, `runListeners`, `runListenerCalls`, `runListenerCallsList`, and `runListenerCallsClear`.
- Shatter currently cannot construct these concrete `package main` values through reusable runtime value factories, so the admin/profile/listener command helpers are skipped during selection.

## Proposed change

Move the reusable CLI admin dependency shape behind a small constructible API. One acceptable shape is an internal package such as `internal/admincli` that exposes:

- an options value or constructor representing the current `rootOptions` fields
- an admin client interface or struct constructor that can be backed by a caller-provided `*http.Client`
- a test/scan-safe constructor that points at an inert local URL and does not perform network I/O until command logic explicitly calls it

Keep the executable entrypoint behavior unchanged.

## Acceptance checks

- Shatter can be configured with a simple constructor expression for the admin CLI options value.
- Shatter can be configured with a simple constructor expression for the admin client value, preferably backed by a no-op or caller-supplied HTTP transport.
- Existing `zolemc` command behavior and flags remain unchanged.
- `go test ./...` passes.

## In scope

- Refactoring `cmd/zolemc` internals into an importable internal package if needed.
- Adding small constructors or interfaces that are useful to tests and Shatter.
- Updating tests that need the moved types.

## Out of scope

- Changing command output, flag names, or admin API semantics.
- Adding new admin commands.

## Coverage value

This should unlock roughly eight currently skipped Shatter-selected paths in `zolemc` admin, profile, listener, and listener-call command handling.

title: Raise feature-code unit test line coverage to 80%
description:
# Raise feature-code unit test line coverage to 80%

## Objective
Increase automated unit test line coverage for zolem feature code from the current measured baseline of 66.21% to at least 80%. Do not delete feature code to raise coverage. Very small refactorings are allowed only when they make code easier to test without changing behavior.

## Current Evidence
- Coverage command used for baseline: `env GOCACHE=/tmp/zolem-go-build-cache go test ./... -covermode=count -coverpkg=./... -coverprofile=/tmp/zolem-coverage.out`.
- The generated profile was usable and reported 66.21% line coverage, 64.95% unique coverage-block coverage, and 78.73% function coverage.
- The full run currently fails in `internal/specs` at `TestSourceVerification_AnthropicV1SnapshotInvariants` with `source_verify_test.go:43: missing text in [type]`. This failure should be considered separately from the coverage-improvement work unless it blocks verification.
- Deduped raw coverage profile blocks show the largest uncovered statement opportunities: `cmd/zolemc/main.go` (207), `cmd/zolem/local_admin.go` (73), `internal/runtime/store.go` (67), `internal/specs/openapi.go` (67), `internal/provider/openai/responses_ws.go` (62), and `cmd/zolem/recording.go` (55).

## Scope
- Add or extend automated unit tests for existing feature code.
- Prefer focused package tests over slow cross-process e2e tests where behavior can be verified in-process.
- Preserve feature code. If feature code appears dead or unreachable, record it in notes for reporting after the work instead of deleting it.
- Very small refactorings are allowed if needed to make an existing behavior unit-testable.

## Out of Scope
- Deleting feature code to improve coverage.
- Changing user-facing behavior solely for coverage.
- Opening a pull request.

## Acceptance Checks
- A current coverage run proves feature-code line coverage is at least 80%.
- New or extended tests are committed with the implementation.
- Any dead-looking feature code encountered is recorded for reporting and not removed.
- Relevant package tests pass. If the existing `internal/specs` snapshot failure remains, document it separately with exact command output.

## Resolved Review Clarifications
- For this issue, feature code means non-test Go source included by `go test ./... -coverpkg=./...`, excluding generated temporary artifacts and test-only files. CLI entrypoints and runtime/provider package code count as feature code when they are in the coverage denominator.
- If the known `internal/specs` snapshot test failure still occurs but Go emits a usable `-coverprofile`, use that current profile for the coverage measurement and report the failure separately. If the failure prevents profile generation, fix or isolate that blocker before claiming the coverage target is met.
- Regenerate coverage and uncovered-file rankings locally before deciding final test targets; the summarized baseline is starting evidence, not a substitute for current measurement.

title: Enable Ollama LLM backend in shatter config
description:
# Enable Ollama LLM backend in shatter config

## Summary

Add an `llm:` section to this project's `.shatter/config.yaml` so shatter uses the Ollama local model adapter as its seed oracle. This lets the concolic explorer call a locally-running Ollama model to propose candidate inputs for branch conditions the solver cannot satisfy.

## Prerequisite

Blocked on shatter `str-g5b` ("Custom LLM adapter framework", currently in_progress), which wires `LlmConfig` onto `ShatterConfig`. Until that lands, the `llm:` block below will be silently ignored or rejected by the config parser.

## What to do (once str-g5b lands)

Add to `.shatter/config.yaml`:

```yaml
llm:
  enabled: true
  adapter: local
  local:
    command: ["ollama", "serve"]
    model: llama3.1
    port: 11434
    startup_timeout_seconds: 60
```

`adapter: local` is the correct value — it selects `LocalAdapterConfig` in `shatter-core/src/config.rs:239`. Adjust `model` to whichever Western-origin model is installed locally (`llama3.1`, `mistral`, `phi4`). Do not use Chinese-origin models (DeepSeek, Qwen, Yi, Baichuan, GLM/ChatGLM).

To check which models are available: `ollama list`.

## Scope

- **In scope:** adding the `llm:` YAML block to `.shatter/config.yaml`
- **Out of scope:** implementing the adapter (str-g5b), adding a new E2E test suite, or modifying any Rust source in this repo

## Acceptance checks

- Config parses cleanly: run any `shatter` subcommand after adding the block and confirm no unknown-field deserialization error
- `task smoke` passes with the new config present
- With Ollama running locally (`ollama serve`), running `shatter explore` on a target function causes oracle queries to appear in Ollama's request log (or a local proxy log)
- No cloud API key is required for the run to succeed

title: Clarify admin-mode precedence in local admin server story
description:
# Clarify admin-mode precedence when local-provider is also supplied

## Problem
The story `docs/stories/zolem-start-local-admin-server.md` contains conflicting wording about what happens when both `-local-admin-addr` and `-local-provider` are supplied. Its Expected Behavior says zolem uses admin mode and ignores `-local-provider`, but its Boundaries section says admin mode and fixed-listener mode are mutually exclusive, which can imply the combined flags should be rejected.

## Current Code Facts
- `cmd/zolem/main.go` checks `-local-admin-addr` before `-local-provider`. If `-local-admin-addr` is non-empty, zolem calls `runLocalAdmin(...)` and returns.
- Because of that ordering, supplying both `-local-admin-addr` and `-local-provider` starts admin mode; the fixed-listener `-local-provider` branch is not reached.
- `docs/stories/zolem-start-local-admin-server.md` already has an auditable claim saying "supplying both -local-admin-addr and -local-provider uses admin mode; -local-provider is ignored".
- The same story's Boundaries section says "Only one of admin mode or fixed-listener mode may be used at a time (mutually exclusive flags)", which conflicts with the current precedence behavior.

## Desired Outcome
