Update `docs/stories/zolem-start-local-admin-server.md` so the story consistently describes the current intended behavior: admin mode takes precedence when both flags are supplied, and `-local-provider` is ignored in that case. Avoid wording that implies the combined flags are rejected unless the implementation is intentionally changed in a separate issue.

## Acceptance Checks
- The story no longer contains contradictory wording about combined `-local-admin-addr` and `-local-provider` usage.
- The Boundaries section is revised to match the existing auditable claim and current `cmd/zolem/main.go` branch order.
- The story remains clear that a single process runs in admin mode or fixed-listener mode, with admin mode taking precedence when both flags are present.
- Evidence still points to tests and code/docs that support the behavior, or the story's Drift Notes identify any missing test coverage if none exists.

## In Scope
- Story text and auditable claims for `zolem-start-local-admin-server`.

## Out of Scope
- Changing `cmd/zolem/main.go` flag precedence.
- Making combined `-local-admin-addr` and `-local-provider` usage a startup error.
- Changing admin API behavior.

title: Correct WASM module story for omitted backend flag
description:
# Correct WASM module story for omitted backend flag

## Problem
The story `docs/stories/zolemc-create-response-profile.md` says `zolemc profiles create` exits before making the request when `-wasm-module-file` is set without `-backend wasm`. Current CLI behavior intentionally treats `-wasm-module-file` as implying backend `wasm` when `-backend` is omitted.

## Current Code Facts
- `cmd/zolemc/main.go` help for `-wasm-module-file` says it "implies -backend wasm when -backend is unset".
- `cmd/zolemc/main.go` checks whether `-backend` was explicitly set. If `-wasm-module-file` is present and `-backend` was not set, it sets `payload.Backend = "wasm"` and sends the request.
- `cmd/zolemc/main.go` rejects `-wasm-module-file` only when `-backend` was explicitly set to a value other than `wasm`.
- `cmd/zolemc/main_e2e_test.go` has coverage for sending WASM fields when `-wasm-module-file` is supplied without an explicit backend, and for rejecting `-wasm-module-file` with an explicit non-WASM backend.
- `README.md` documents that `-wasm-module-file` selects the `wasm` backend when `-backend` is not set explicitly.

## Desired Outcome
Update `docs/stories/zolemc-create-response-profile.md` so it describes the current intended behavior: `-wasm-module-file` implies backend `wasm` when `-backend` is omitted, and errors only when paired with an explicitly non-WASM backend.

## Acceptance Checks
- The story no longer claims that omitted `-backend wasm` is an error when `-wasm-module-file` is present.
- The auditable claims distinguish omitted backend from explicitly contradictory backend.
- Evidence still points to the current CLI tests and docs that cover this behavior.

## In Scope
- Story text and auditable claims for `zolemc-create-response-profile`.

## Out of Scope
- Changing `zolemc` behavior or CLI flags.

title: Reference docs/stories in AGENTS.md
description:
# Reference docs/stories in AGENTS.md

## Problem
The repository contains storystore intent stories under `docs/stories/`, but root agent instructions do not mention that convention. A repo-level storystore audit reports `agent-pointer-missing` when at least one root agent-instruction file exists and none reference the storystore convention.

## Current Code Facts
- `docs/stories/README.md` says intent stories for this repository are maintained by the storystore plugin, and that each story describes a durable user-facing capability.
- `docs/stories/INDEX.md` lists the current story set.
- Root `AGENTS.md` exists and contains repository instructions, but it does not reference `docs/stories/` or storystore.

## Desired Outcome
Add a concise pointer to root `AGENTS.md` so future agents know to consult `docs/stories/` for user-facing intent stories where relevant.

## Acceptance Checks
- `AGENTS.md` includes a short, durable reference to `docs/stories/` and explains that these files contain storystore intent stories for user-facing behavior.
- The addition does not replace or duplicate existing Beads, testing, PR, or CLI-help instructions.
- The new text is brief enough to avoid increasing startup instruction noise unnecessarily.

## In Scope
- Root `AGENTS.md` pointer text.

## Out of Scope
- Rewriting story contents or changing the storystore schema.
- Adding new story tooling.

title: Enforce loopback-only binding for fixed-listener mode
description:
# Enforce loopback-only binding for fixed-listener mode

## Problem
The story `docs/stories/zolem-start-fixed-listener-mode.md` says fixed-listener mode only accepts loopback addresses and rejects non-loopback addresses. Current fixed-listener startup does not enforce that rule for `-local-addr`, and `README.md` documents Docker fixed-listener usage with `-local-addr 0.0.0.0:18080`.

## Current Code Facts
- `cmd/zolem/startup.go` function `localOptions.runtime()` validates TLS and provider, defaults empty `Addr` to `127.0.0.1:8080`, and returns the listener runtime without calling `runtimecfg.ValidateLoopbackAddr` or `runtimecfg.ValidateListenerSpec`.
- `internal/runtime/store.go` function `ValidateListenerSpec()` rejects non-loopback listener addresses, but that path is used for admin-created listeners, not fixed-listener startup.
- `cmd/zolem/local_admin.go` function `runLocalAdmin()` calls `runtimecfg.ValidateLoopbackAddr()` for `-local-admin-addr`.
- `README.md` Docker examples currently bind admin mode and fixed-listener mode to `0.0.0.0`, so behavior may intentionally differ between host-local execution and container usage.

## Desired Outcome
Make fixed-listener mode enforce the same loopback-only binding rule described by the story. Non-loopback `-local-addr` values such as `0.0.0.0:<port>` should fail before the listener starts, with a clear startup error.

- `zolem -local-provider <provider> -local-addr 0.0.0.0:<port>` fails before listening with a clear error.
- Fixed-listener startup has focused test coverage for rejecting non-loopback `-local-addr`.
- Existing admin-mode loopback enforcement remains covered and unchanged unless intentionally revised.
- README Docker examples are updated so fixed-listener container usage does not instruct users to bind `0.0.0.0` if that is no longer supported.
- CLI help in `cmd/zolem/main.go` is updated if needed to make the loopback-only fixed-listener address rule visible.
- `docs/stories/zolem-start-fixed-listener-mode.md` remains accurate after the code and docs changes.

## In Scope
- Fixed-listener `-local-addr` behavior and associated docs/story/tests.

## Out of Scope
- Changing admin-mode `-local-admin-addr` validation unless required for consistency and explicitly justified.
- Preserving fixed-listener non-loopback or container-wide binding behavior.
- Broader network exposure, auth, or container networking design beyond this mismatch.
close_reason: Loopback-only enforcement implemented in cmd/zolem/startup.go; tests, README, CLI help, and story updated. Merged to main.

title: Document bwrap no-egress recipes for Codex and Claude
description:
## Problem

Zolem now supports OpenAI Responses WebSocket clients, but the docs do not show how to prove a client such as Codex or Claude is using Zolem without accidentally reaching provider servers. Users need a documented recipe that blocks external network egress while keeping a local Zolem listener reachable.

## Current code facts

- `docs/local-runtime.md` already documents local listeners, fixture backends, `-local-calls-file`, and OpenAI Responses WebSocket fixtures with `version: v1-responses`.
- `README.md` has short runtime limitations and links to `docs/local-runtime.md` for detailed behavior.
- `bwrap` is available in the local environment (`bubblewrap 0.9.0`), and `--unshare-net` is the relevant no-egress isolation mechanism.

## Desired change

Add a recipe to Zolem docs showing how to run provider-client smoke checks inside Bubblewrap so Codex or Claude can reach a Zolem listener on sandbox loopback but cannot reach OpenAI/Anthropic servers.

## Acceptance checks

- `docs/local-runtime.md` includes a Bubblewrap/no-egress recipe for Codex against the OpenAI Responses WebSocket endpoint.
- The recipe runs Zolem and the client inside the same `bwrap --unshare-net` sandbox or otherwise clearly explains why the host loopback listener is not reachable from an isolated network namespace.
- The Codex recipe uses an isolated/ephemeral Codex configuration, fake API key, local provider base URL, and `-local-calls-file` assertion.
- The docs also include the analogous Claude recipe or clearly state the Claude configuration placeholders needed to point Claude at the local Zolem listener.
- The recipe explains observable proof: client exits successfully, the calls file records a local request, and external provider egress would fail at the network layer.

## Out of scope

- Adding automated Codex or Claude smoke tests to CI.
- Supporting every client-specific configuration style.
- Changing Zolem runtime behavior.
notes: Implemented docs plus a narrow Anthropic compatibility fix required by the Claude recipe: Zolem now accepts Anthropic system content blocks such as Claude Code sends. Verification: go build -o ./.tmp/zolem ./cmd/zolem passed; Codex bwrap no-egress smoke passed with /v1/responses status 101 in calls file; Claude bwrap no-egress smoke passed with /v1/messages status 200 in calls file; go test ./internal/provider/anthropic ./internal/specs passed; go test ./cmd/zolem -run TestLocalRuntimeAnthropicSystemContentBlocks_E2E -count=1 passed; go test ./... was interrupted after several minutes with 308 reported passes across 13 packages and no failures observed.

title: Add WebSocket Responses API support to the OpenAI provider
description:
# Add WebSocket Responses API support to the OpenAI provider

## Summary

Zolem only mocks `POST /v1/chat/completions` for the OpenAI provider. Clients that use the
Responses API (e.g., codex 0.133.0) connect via WebSocket to `wss://<host>/v1/responses`
and are not intercepted at all. Add a WebSocket handler so fixture sequences and lorem/faker
backends work for Responses API clients.

## Motivation / Blocking Issue

`codex exec` 0.133.0 connects exclusively to `wss://api.openai.com/v1/responses`. No
existing zolem endpoint intercepts this; the client falls through to the real API. This
blocks end-to-end testing of the bento `permission-request.py` PermissionRequest hook
(bento-buu in the bento repo).

## Desired behavior

```
codex exec "…"
  └──connects──> ws://127.0.0.1:<port>/v1/responses  (zolem)
                    ├── zolem authenticates Bearer token (reject 403 on mismatch)
                    ├── for each response.create frame received, zolem sends the next
                    │   fixture step's events as WS text frames (or lorem events)
                    └── zolem records the exchange via -local-calls-file
```

## Scope

### In scope

- New route in `internal/provider/openai/handler.go`:
  `h.mux.Get("/v1/responses", h.handleResponses)` — WebSocket upgrade handler
- **WebSocket library:** Add `github.com/gorilla/websocket` to go.mod (currently absent).
  No other WS library is present; gorilla is the established choice for `net/http`-based
  servers.
- **Connection model:** One persistent WS connection per `codex exec` session. The client
  sends multiple `{"type":"response.create",...}` frames over the same connection (one
  per conversation turn). Codex reconnects at connection level (up to 5 retries) but does
  not open a new connection per turn.
- **Incoming frame handling:** Zolem reads each WS text frame, parses the `"type"` field,
  and advances the sequence counter when `type == "response.create"`. The body of
  `response.create` frames is ignored for fixture selection; zolem does not need to parse
  the model, tools, or instructions fields.
- **Fixture backend:** Each sequence step's `response.json` is a JSON array of Responses
  API event objects (text frames to send in order); see fixture format below.
- **Fixture version key:** WS handler calls `matcher.Match` with `Provider="openai"`,
  `Version="v1-responses"`. Fixture namespaces for the WS backend set `version: v1-responses`
  in their top-level `fixtures.yaml`. This keeps WS fixtures distinct from chat/completions
  fixtures (`version: v1`).
- **Lorem / faker backends:** Generate a minimal `response.completed` event with a
  lorem-generated `output_text` item; enough for tests that don't need tool calls.
- **Call recording:** Add a `RecordedWSCall` shape to `recording.go` with fields
  `call_id` (int64 monotonic, same counter as `RecordedCall`), `method` (`"GET"`),
  `path` (`"/v1/responses"`), `status` (101), `frames_sent` (int), `frames_received`
  (int). Written as one JSONL line per WS connection (not per frame). This keeps
  bento-side `assertZolemHit(min_calls=1)` working without changes.

### Out of scope

- Anthropic or Gemini WebSocket support
- Full protocol fidelity (audio, interrupts, bidirectional input, schema validation)
- TLS WebSocket (`wss://`) — existing `-local-tls-cert / -local-tls-key` can be extended later
- Per-frame call recording (one entry per connection is sufficient for bento-4e6)

## Fixture format (verified from openai-go v3.15.0)

Event type strings confirmed from `github.com/openai/openai-go/v3@v3.15.0/internal/constant/`
(already a direct dependency of zolem). The implementer may use the SDK's `responses` package
types for serialization.

**`fixtures.yaml` for a 2-turn sequence:**
```yaml
provider: openai
version: v1-responses
fixtures:
  - expression: 'true'
    sequence:
      id: conversation
      on_exhaust: last
      steps: [turn-tool, turn-end]
```

**`turn-tool/response.json`** (function call → triggers PermissionRequest hook in codex):
```json
[
  {"type": "response.created",          "sequence_number": 0, "response": {"id": "resp_01", "status": "in_progress", "output": []}},
  {"type": "response.output_item.added","sequence_number": 1, "output_index": 0,
   "item": {"type": "function_call", "id": "item_01", "call_id": "call_01",
            "name": "shell_command", "arguments": "", "status": "in_progress"}},
  {"type": "response.function_call_arguments.done", "sequence_number": 2,
   "item_id": "item_01", "name": "shell_command", "output_index": 0,
   "arguments": "{\"command\": \"python3 /path/to/script.py\"}"},
  {"type": "response.output_item.done", "sequence_number": 3, "output_index": 0,
   "item": {"type": "function_call", "id": "item_01", "call_id": "call_01",
            "name": "shell_command", "arguments": "{\"command\": \"python3 /path/to/script.py\"}",
            "status": "completed"}},
  {"type": "response.completed",        "sequence_number": 4,
   "response": {"id": "resp_01", "status": "completed", "output": []}}
]
```

**`turn-end/response.json`** (text end_turn):
```json
[
  {"type": "response.created",          "sequence_number": 0, "response": {"id": "resp_02", "status": "in_progress", "output": []}},
  {"type": "response.output_item.added","sequence_number": 1, "output_index": 0,
   "item": {"type": "message", "id": "msg_01", "role": "assistant", "content": [], "status": "in_progress"}},
  {"type": "response.output_text.delta","sequence_number": 2, "item_id": "msg_01",
   "output_index": 0, "content_index": 0, "delta": "Done."},
  {"type": "response.output_text.done", "sequence_number": 3, "item_id": "msg_01",
   "output_index": 0, "content_index": 0, "text": "Done."},
  {"type": "response.output_item.done", "sequence_number": 4, "output_index": 0,
   "item": {"type": "message", "id": "msg_01", "role": "assistant",
            "content": [{"type": "output_text", "text": "Done."}], "status": "completed"}},
  {"type": "response.completed",        "sequence_number": 5,
   "response": {"id": "resp_02", "status": "completed", "output": []}}
]
```

## Current code facts (zolem repo)

- `internal/provider/openai/handler.go`: `NewHandler()` registers
  `h.mux.Post("/v1/chat/completions", h.handleChatCompletions)`. New: add
  `h.mux.Get("/v1/responses", h.handleResponses)`.
- `cmd/zolem/local_admin.go` `startLocalHTTPServer()` (lines 559–574): starts
  `net.Listener` + `http.Server` via standard `net/http`; gorilla WebSocket upgrade
  works without server-level changes.
- `go.mod` (module `zolem.dev/zolem`, go 1.26.1): no WebSocket library present.
  Direct deps include `github.com/go-chi/chi/v5 v5.2.1`, `github.com/openai/openai-go/v3 v3.15.0`.
  Add: `github.com/gorilla/websocket`.
- `internal/fixture/sequence.go` `SequenceCounters.Step(profile, namespace, id, total, onExhaust)`:
  unchanged; WS handler calls it once per `response.create` frame received.
- `internal/fixture/selector.go` `Selector` interface: unchanged; WS handler calls
  `matcher.Match(ctx, MatchRequest{Provider:"openai", Version:"v1-responses"}, candidates)`.
- `cmd/zolem/recording.go`: existing `RecordedCall` for HTTP; add `RecordedWSCall` (see
  scope section for required fields).
- `internal/provider/openai/handler.go` token check: `handleChatCompletions` validates
  `Authorization: Bearer <token>`; the WS handler must perform the same check before
  upgrading and return HTTP 403 on mismatch (not 101).

## CI and test strategy

- CI (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`): `go test ./...` on ubuntu-latest; no codex binary
  in CI environment.
- Zolem-side integration test: use a Go gorilla WebSocket client within the test binary
  (no external binary), following the pattern in `cmd/zolem/local_runtime_e2e_test.go`.
  Test should: connect, send `{"type":"response.create","response":{}}`, receive frames,
  assert the final frame has `"type":"response.completed"`.
- The "codex exits 0" check is a bento-side acceptance check (bento-buu); not a CI gate
  in zolem.

## Acceptance checks

1. `GET /v1/responses` with valid `Authorization: Bearer <token>` + WS upgrade headers
   returns HTTP 101 and establishes a connection
2. `GET /v1/responses` with wrong or missing Bearer token returns HTTP 403 (no upgrade)
3. A Go test client sends `{"type":"response.create","response":{}}` and receives a frame
   sequence ending in `{"type":"response.completed",...}` using the fixture backend
4. The 2-step sequence (turn-tool → turn-end, `version: v1-responses`) advances correctly:
   first `response.create` yields turn-tool events; second yields turn-end events; third
   replays turn-end (`on_exhaust: last`)
5. WS fixtures (version `v1-responses`) do not match when a request hits
   `POST /v1/chat/completions`; chat/completions fixtures (version `v1`) do not match
   the WS handler — no cross-contamination
6. `lorem` backend sends at least one `response.completed` frame for any `response.create`
7. `-local-calls-file` JSONL contains at least one valid JSON line per WS connection
   (parseable by `json.loads`)
8. Existing `POST /v1/chat/completions` tests and `go test ./...` pass unchanged

## Size signal

Medium: new handler file (~200–300 lines), one new dependency, fixture format extension
(array-of-events response.json), one new recording struct, and one Go integration test.
Estimated 1–2 days.

title: Include zolemc in release tarballs and update install docs
description:
## Problem

`zolemc` (the admin CLI companion to `zolem`) is not included in the release
tarballs produced by GoReleaser. Only the `zolem` server binary is built and
packaged. Users who install from a tarball must separately `go install` or
build from source to get `zolemc`, but the install documentation now only
partially reflects this limitation.

Specifically:
- `.goreleaser.yaml` defines a single `builds` entry with `id: zolem` and
  `main: ./cmd/zolem`. There is no entry for `./cmd/zolemc`.
- `INSTALL.md` (added in the `zolem-g0l-binary-docs` branch) documents the
  tarball as containing only `zolem` and directs users to `go install` for
  `zolemc`. This is accurate for the current state but is a friction point.
- `README.md` installation section has the same limitation documented.

The desired end state is that each release tarball contains both `zolem` and
`zolemc`, and the install documentation is updated to reflect that.

## Resolved Code Facts

- **GoReleaser config**: `.goreleaser.yaml`, `builds:` section. Currently one
  entry (`id: zolem`, `main: ./cmd/zolem`). Adding `zolemc` requires a second
  entry with `id: zolemc`, `main: ./cmd/zolemc`, and the same `goos`/`goarch`/
  `ignore`/`flags`/`ldflags` block. The `archives` section references builds
  by `ids:`; to include both binaries in a single archive, add `zolemc` to the
  `ids:` list on the existing archive entry.
- **Archive naming**: `name_template: "zolem-{{ .Version }}-{{ .Os }}-{{ .Arch }}"` —
  the archive name stays the same; the tarball will simply contain two binaries.
- **INSTALL.md**: repo root, added in `zolem-g0l-binary-docs`. Lines 44–55
  currently say the archive contains only `zolem` and give a `go install`
  fallback for `zolemc`. Both lines need updating once `zolemc` is in the
  tarball.
- **README.md**: `## Installation` section, lines 49–57. Same fix required:
  restore `sudo mv zolem zolemc /usr/local/bin/` and remove the `go install`
  fallback note.
- **Build flags**: `zolemc` has version ldflags in its `main` package
  (`main.version`, `main.commit`, `main.date`) matching `zolem` — confirmed
  by `cmd/zolemc/main.go`. The same ldflags template applies.
- **CGO**: `CGO_ENABLED=0` applies to both; no change needed there.

## Proposed Changes

1. **`.goreleaser.yaml`**: Add a second `builds` entry for `zolemc`:
   ```yaml
   - id: zolemc
     main: ./cmd/zolemc
     binary: zolemc
     env:
       - CGO_ENABLED=0
     goos:
       - linux
       - darwin
     goarch:
       - amd64
       - arm64
     ignore:
       - goos: darwin
         goarch: amd64
     flags:
       - -trimpath
     ldflags:
       - -s -w
       - -X main.version={{ .Version }}
       - -X main.commit={{ .Commit }}
       - -X main.date={{ .Date }}
   ```
   Update the `archives` entry to reference both build IDs:
   ```yaml
   archives:
     - id: zolem
       ids:
         - zolem
         - zolemc
       ...
   ```

2. **`INSTALL.md`**: Update Option 1 install snippet to `sudo mv zolem zolemc /usr/local/bin/`. Remove the `go install` fallback paragraph for `zolemc`. Update the archive-contents note.

3. **`README.md`**: Same targeted fix in the `## Installation > Binary` section.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `.goreleaser.yaml` has a `zolemc` builds entry with the same platform
      matrix and ldflags as `zolem`
- [ ] The archive `ids:` list includes both `zolem` and `zolemc`
- [ ] A local `goreleaser build --snapshot --clean` produces `zolemc` binaries
      alongside `zolem` binaries in `dist/`
- [ ] `INSTALL.md` install snippet reads `sudo mv zolem zolemc /usr/local/bin/`
      with no `go install` fallback paragraph for `zolemc`
- [ ] `README.md` installation section matches

## Out of Scope

- Changing the Docker image (distroless; no shell; `zolemc` is not useful
  inside the container — users run it from the host)
- Changing archive naming or splitting into separate archives per binary
- Adding `zolemc` to the Docker image
close_reason: goreleaser already included zolemc in tarballs since cacdc44 on main; doc corrections (INSTALL.md + README.md) committed as e300c13 on zolem-g0l-binary-docs, which lands with that branch

title: Document how users obtain zolemc from release artifacts
description:
# Document how users obtain zolemc from release artifacts

## Problem

`zolemc` is now included in release archives alongside `zolem`, but the user-facing documentation still only demonstrates running it from source with `go run ./cmd/zolemc`. Users downloading a release archive do not have clear instructions that the archive contains both binaries or how to put `zolemc` on their `PATH`.

## Current Code Facts

- `.goreleaser.yaml` defines a `zolemc` build with `main: ./cmd/zolemc` and `binary: zolemc`.
- `.goreleaser.yaml` archive `id: zolem` includes both build ids: `zolem` and `zolemc`.
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` explicitly builds `./cmd/zolemc`.
- `README.md` currently uses `go run ./cmd/zolemc` in local runtime examples and the WASM profile example.
- `docs/local-runtime.md` also demonstrates `go run ./cmd/zolemc` for the WASM profile path.
- Existing issue `zolem-g0l` covers broad binary-user installation documentation and is already in progress; this issue is narrower and specifically tracks documenting `zolemc` acquisition now that `zolemc` is a release artifact.

## Desired Behavior

Users should be able to tell from the docs that release archives contain both `zolem` and `zolemc`, and they should know how to extract or install both commands from the downloaded archive.

## Scope

In scope:

- Update installation or quick-start documentation to state that release archives include both `zolem` and `zolemc`.
- Show or describe extracting the archive and placing both binaries on `PATH`.
- Adjust examples or nearby text so binary users understand they can run `zolemc` directly instead of `go run ./cmd/zolemc`.
- Keep source-development examples clear where `go run ./cmd/zolemc` remains appropriate.
- Link or cross-reference the relevant local runtime/WASM docs if the acquisition instructions live primarily in `README.md`.

Out of scope:

- Changing release packaging, CI, or GoReleaser configuration.
- Changing `zolemc` CLI behavior.
- Adding package-manager integrations such as Homebrew, Scoop, npm, or Docker images for `zolemc`.
- Rewriting the full installation guide beyond the `zolemc` acquisition gap.

## Acceptance Checks

- The docs explicitly state that release archives include both `zolem` and `zolemc`.
- The docs show or describe an install/extract flow that makes both binaries runnable, including `zolemc`.
- At least one `zolemc` example or surrounding note is suitable for binary users, not only source checkouts.
- Source-development usage remains discoverable for contributors who prefer `go run ./cmd/zolemc`.
- Run the repository's relevant docs or bounded verification gate; if there is no docs-specific gate, run a focused check such as `go test ./cmd/zolemc ./cmd/zolem` and note that the change is docs-only.

title: Document how to build Zolem WASM modules in common languages
description:
## Summary

Zolem documents the runtime WASM ABIs, but does not currently provide practical build instructions for users writing modules in common languages. Add documentation and minimal examples that show how to produce compatible binary `.wasm` modules for Zolem's supported WASM surfaces.

## Current Code Facts

- `README.md` documents creating a WASM-generator profile with `zolemc profiles create ... -wasm-module-file ./generator.wasm -wasm-timeout-ms ...` and says the generator ABI is documented in `docs/local-runtime.md#wasm-backend`.
- `docs/local-runtime.md` documents the WASM generator backend input JSON shape, result shape, required exports, and constraints.
- The generator backend requires a freestanding binary WASM module with exports: `memory`, `alloc(len: i32) -> i32`, `dealloc(ptr: i32, len: i32)`, `generate(input_ptr: i32, input_len: i32) -> i32`, `result_ptr(handle: i32) -> i32`, `result_len(handle: i32) -> i32`, and `result_free(handle: i32)`.
- `internal/wasmgen/generator.go` validates that generator modules import nothing, export only the required ABI plus accepted linker boundary globals, and create a fresh instance per request with a 16 MiB host memory limit.
- `internal/fixture/wasm.go` still supports legacy per-fixture `match.wasm` modules that export `memory` and `match(ptr i32, len i32) -> f32`; this path is deprecated in favor of namespace-level selectors or CEL but remains supported.
- `internal/fixture/wasm_selector.go` supports namespace-level `selector.wasm` modules that export `memory` and `select(inputPtr i32, inputLen i32) -> (outputPtr i32, outputLen i32)`.
- Fixture namespace placement today is: `selector.wasm` at the fixture namespace root, such as `<local-fixtures-dir>/team-a/selector.wasm`; legacy `match.wasm` inside a single fixture directory, such as `<local-fixtures-dir>/team-a/anthropic-demo/match.wasm`.
- `docs/plans/2026-04-02-zolem-implementation.md` has an unchecked Post-MVP item: "WASM module compilation: document how to build `match.wasm` using TinyGo or Rust".

## Problem

A user can see the ABI, but cannot easily determine how to compile a valid module from Rust, TinyGo, C, or another common WASM-capable language without reverse-engineering tests or runtime validation errors. This is especially easy to get wrong because Zolem rejects WASI/`env` imports and extra exports for generator modules.

## Desired Behavior

Add user-facing docs that explain how to build Zolem-compatible WASM modules from common languages and how to verify them before loading them into a profile or fixture namespace.

The documentation should cover at least:

- the difference between the three WASM surfaces: generator backend, namespace `selector.wasm`, and legacy per-fixture `match.wasm`
- which surfaces are recommended for new users, and which are deprecated
- how to compile freestanding modules without WASI or host imports
- how to expose the exact ABI Zolem expects
- how to create or pass the module to Zolem (`zolemc -wasm-module-file` for generator profiles; fixture directory placement for selector/legacy matcher)
- common validation failures and what they mean, such as unsupported imports, missing exports, wrong signatures, and WAT text instead of binary WASM

## Suggested Scope

In scope:

- Add or extend docs under `docs/`, likely `docs/local-runtime.md` plus a focused `docs/wasm-modules.md` if the examples are long.
- Link the new docs from `README.md` near the existing WASM-generator profile example.
- Include minimal working examples for at least Rust and TinyGo if feasible.
- Include shell commands for building the examples and loading a generator module through `zolemc`.
- Include expected output/result shape for a smoke check, for example a generator returning `[]string` JSON such as `["Hello ","from WASM."]`.

Out of scope:

- Changing the WASM runtime ABI.
- Adding support for WASI, imports, component model, or new host functions.
- Replacing deprecated `match.wasm`; documentation should steer new fixture users toward `fixtures.yaml` or `selector.wasm` while still describing legacy behavior clearly enough for existing users.
- Building an SDK or code generator for WASM modules.

## Acceptance Criteria

- A new user can follow the docs to build a binary `.wasm` generator module in at least one common language without needing headers from Zolem source.
- The docs state that Zolem does not require C headers or vendored source headers; compatibility is defined by exported WASM symbols and JSON payloads.
- The docs explicitly warn that generator modules must not import WASI or `env` and must not expose extra exports beyond the accepted ABI/boundary globals.
- README links to the language/build instructions from the existing WASM profile section.
- The docs distinguish generator backend ABI from `selector.wasm` ABI and deprecated `match.wasm` ABI.
- Any included examples are either checked in as source snippets or have commands that are realistically runnable by users with the named toolchain installed.
- Run the repository's relevant documentation or test gate if one exists; otherwise run a bounded verification such as `go test ./internal/wasmgen ./internal/fixture ./cmd/zolem ./cmd/zolemc` and note any skipped external toolchain checks.

title: Build zolemc as a releaseable artifact
description:
# Build zolemc as a releaseable artifact

## Problem

`zolemc` exists as a Go command and is exercised by tests, but the release pipeline only builds and packages the `zolem` binary. Users can run `go run ./cmd/zolemc`, but tagged and nightly releases do not publish a standalone `zolemc` binary.

## Current Code Facts

- `cmd/zolemc/main.go` defines the `zolemc` CLI.
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` runs `go test ./...`, which includes `cmd/zolemc` tests, but its explicit build step is only `go build ./cmd/zolem`.
- `.goreleaser.yaml` has one build entry: `id: zolem`, `main: ./cmd/zolem`, `binary: zolem`.
- `.goreleaser.yaml` archive `id: zolem` currently includes only the `zolem` build id, so the released tarball does not include a `zolemc` binary.
- `Dockerfile` copies only the `zolem` binary and uses it as the entrypoint; this issue is about releaseable CLI artifacts, not changing the container entrypoint.

## Scope

In scope:

- Add `zolemc` to the release build matrix as a standalone binary.
- Include `zolemc` in release archives alongside `zolem`.
- Add an explicit CI build check for `./cmd/zolemc`.
- Keep existing `zolem` binary names, archives, Docker images, signing, checksum, and SBOM behavior intact.

Out of scope:

- Changing `zolemc` CLI behavior.
- Changing Docker image entrypoints or publishing a separate `zolemc` image.
- Renaming `zolem` artifacts or altering existing release tag behavior.

## Acceptance Checks

- `go build ./cmd/zolem` succeeds.
- `go build ./cmd/zolemc` succeeds.
- `go test ./...` succeeds.
- A GoReleaser snapshot/check path confirms both `zolem` and `zolemc` are part of archive artifacts, or the config change is otherwise validated with the closest available local GoReleaser command.

title: Initialize storystore docs/stories/ with ~10 observed-mode stories
description:
## Summary

Run `stories-init` to scaffold `docs/stories/` and seed approximately 10
observed-mode draft stories covering the top user-facing CLI surfaces and
workflows in zolem.

## Desired Behavior

- `docs/stories/README.md` and `docs/stories/INDEX.md` are created.
- `docs/stories/drift-todo.md` is added to `.gitignore`.
- Approximately 10 observed-mode draft stories are written, each covering a
  distinct user-facing CLI surface or workflow (not infrastructure, build, or
  lint targets).
- Stories are written with `status: draft`, `authority: observed`, and
  `change_resistance: low`.
- Each story captures the happy path and, where evidence supports it,
  non-happy-path behavior (validation errors, missing inputs, auth failures, etc.).

## Acceptance Checks

- `docs/stories/README.md` exists with non-stub content.
- `docs/stories/INDEX.md` exists and lists all seeded stories.
- `docs/stories/drift-todo.md` appears in `.gitignore`.
- Each story file passes the storystore schema (slug rules, required sections).
- Approximately 10 story files exist under `docs/stories/`.
- No story invents failure modes not supported by code evidence.

## In Scope

- One-time fresh-init scaffolding of `docs/stories/`.
- ~10 observed-mode draft stories for zolem's user-facing CLI commands and workflows.
- Adding the storystore pointer to AGENTS.md (with user approval only).

## Out of Scope

- Promoting stories from `draft` to `active` (requires human acceptance).
- Authoring stories for non-user-facing infrastructure (build, lint, CI).
- Ongoing story maintenance or drift detection.

## Rough Size

Small-to-medium; primarily LLM authoring with deterministic scaffolding scripts.
No implementation code changes.

title: Add installation and getting-started documentation for binary users
description:
## Problem

The README and docs were written for developers running from source (`go run ./cmd/zolem`). There is no documentation for users who want to install and run a pre-built binary. With v0.1.0 shipped, this is the primary gap before the project is usable by anyone without a Go toolchain.

Specific problems:

1. **No installation section** — the README has no mention of GitHub Releases, no download instructions, no path-to-binary setup, no `go install`, and no `docker pull`. A user landing on the repo has no idea how to get a runnable binary.

2. **Quick-start assumes `go run`** — both quick-start examples in the README open with `go run ./cmd/zolem ...`. This is a developer workflow that requires Go installed. Binary users are stranded.

3. **Docker entirely undocumented** — `ghcr.io/ketang/zolem` is published and multi-arch, but the README never mentions it. There are no `docker run` examples, no guidance on mounting fixtures or certs into a container, no mention of available tags.

4. **Platform matrix not stated** — binaries are available for linux/amd64, linux/arm64, and darwin/arm64. None of this is documented. Users don't know what platforms are supported.

5. **No artifact verification guidance** — archives are cosign-signed (`.bundle` files) and have CycloneDX SBOMs. There are no instructions for verifying signatures or inspecting SBOMs.

6. **No v0.1.0 release notes** — no CHANGELOG or human-readable summary of what v0.1.0 includes, what's in scope, and what's known to be out of scope.

## Resolved Code Facts

- **Go minimum version**: `go.mod` line 3 declares `go 1.26.1` → document minimum as Go 1.26
- **Docker registry path**: `.goreleaser.yaml` confirms `ghcr.io/ketang/zolem` (owner: `ketang`)
- **Docker platforms**: `.goreleaser.yaml` builds `linux/amd64` and `linux/arm64` only (no `darwin` Docker target)
- **CHANGELOG location**: No existing `CHANGELOG.md` or `docs/changelog.md`; use repo-root `CHANGELOG.md`
- **Cosign verify command** (confirmed against v0.1.0 release):
  ```
  cosign verify-blob \
    --bundle <archive>.bundle \
    --certificate-oidc-issuer https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com \
    --certificate-identity-regexp "https://github.com/ketang/zolem/.github/workflows/release.yml@refs/tags/.*" \
    <archive>
  ```
- **Binary platforms**: `.goreleaser.yaml` builds matrix: linux/amd64, linux/arm64, darwin/arm64 (darwin/amd64 explicitly excluded)
- **Archive naming pattern**: `zolem-<version>-<os>-<arch>.tar.gz` (e.g. `zolem-0.1.0-linux-amd64.tar.gz`)

## Proposed changes

### 1. New "Installation" section in `README.md` (before the existing quick-start)

Three sub-paths:

**Binary (recommended)**
- Link to `https://github.com/ketang/zolem/releases/latest`
- Per-platform download + extract + PATH instructions using the confirmed archive naming pattern
- Platforms: Linux amd64, Linux arm64, macOS arm64
- Checksum verification: `sha256sum -c checksums.txt`
- Cosign verification snippet using the confirmed command above

**Docker**
- `docker pull ghcr.io/ketang/zolem:v0.1.0` (versioned) and `:latest`
- Available platforms: linux/amd64, linux/arm64
- Basic `docker run` example replacing `zolem` in the quick-start commands

**From source**
- `go install github.com/ketang/zolem/cmd/zolem@latest`
- Note: requires Go 1.26+

### 2. Quick-start rewrite in `README.md`

- Replace `go run ./cmd/zolem` with `zolem` (bare binary name) throughout
- Add a one-line note pointing source builders to the "From source" install path
- Keep both quick-start scenarios (local runtime mode, fixed-listener mode)

### 3. Docker usage section in `README.md`

Alongside the binary quick-start, show the Docker equivalent:
- `docker run --rm ghcr.io/ketang/zolem:v0.1.0 -local-admin-addr 0.0.0.0:18090`
- Mounting fixtures: `-v $PWD/fixtures:/fixtures -local-fixtures-dir /fixtures`
- Mounting TLS certs: `-v $PWD/certs:/certs -local-tls-cert /certs/cert.pem -local-tls-key /certs/key.pem`

### 4. Platform and release notes

- Add a one-line supported-platforms note near the install section (linux/amd64, linux/arm64, darwin/arm64 binaries; linux/amd64, linux/arm64 Docker)
- Add `CHANGELOG.md` at repo root with a v0.1.0 entry summarizing: initial release, all backends (lorem, faker, fixture, WASM, CEL matching, error), both runtime modes, `zolemc` CLI, call recording

### 5. Artifact verification appendix in `README.md`

Required (not optional). Use a collapsible `<details>` block:
- Checksum verification command
- Cosign verify-blob command using the confirmed regexp above
- One-line note on SBOMs: what they are, that they're CycloneDX format, and how to inspect with `syft`

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `README.md` has an "Installation" section with binary, Docker, and source sub-paths
- [ ] All quick-start commands in `README.md` use `zolem` (bare binary), not `go run ./cmd/zolem`
- [ ] `README.md` has a Docker usage section with `docker run` examples including fixture and cert mounting
- [ ] Supported platforms stated explicitly (linux/amd64, linux/arm64, darwin/arm64 binaries; linux/amd64+arm64 Docker)
- [ ] `CHANGELOG.md` exists at repo root with a v0.1.0 entry
- [ ] `README.md` has an artifact verification appendix (collapsible `<details>` block) with checksum, cosign, and SBOM guidance

## Out of scope

- Homebrew tap, Scoop bucket, or other package manager integrations
- Man page generation
- Changes to `docs/local-runtime.md` (already comprehensive)
- Changes to CLI flag help text
- Website or hosted docs
