title: smoke.sh: go run orphan leaks zolem process across test legs, causing spurious 400 on fixture-backend listener create
Cut v0.1.0 and verify release artifacts
description:
## What

Tag `v0.1.0` on main after all pipeline changes land. Verify the release
workflow produces the complete expected artifact set.

## Prerequisites

All five of the following must be merged to main before starting:
- "Expand ci.yml: add PR trigger and smoke step"
- "Add Dockerfile"
- "Add .goreleaser.yaml"
- "Rewrite release.yml as GoReleaser wrapper"
- "Rewrite nightly.yml as GoReleaser wrapper"

## Steps

1. Confirm all prerequisite branches are merged to main and CI is green.
2. `git tag v0.1.0 && git push origin v0.1.0`
3. Wait for `release.yml` to complete: `gh run watch` or `gh run list --workflow=release.yml`
4. Verify GitHub Release at `github.com/ketang/zolem/releases/tag/v0.1.0`.
5. Verify Docker image at `ghcr.io/ketang/zolem`.

## Resolved facts

- Repo slug: `ketang/zolem` (confirmed from `git remote -v`)
- SBOM naming: goreleaser produces `<archive-name>.sbom` per archive (e.g., `zolem-0.1.0-linux-amd64.tar.gz.sbom`)
- Docker smoke test: `docker run --rm ghcr.io/ketang/zolem:v0.1.0` exits code 1 and prints `choose either -local-admin-addr or -local-provider` to stderr — confirms binary starts and entrypoint is correct (standard Go `flag.Parse()` exit)
- Cosign verify command: `cosign verify-blob --bundle <artifact>.bundle --certificate-oidc-issuer https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com --certificate-identity-regexp https://github.com/ketang/zolem/.github/workflows/release.yml@refs/tags/v0.1.0 <artifact>`

## Acceptance checks

**GitHub Release** (`github.com/ketang/zolem/releases/tag/v0.1.0`):
- [ ] `zolem-0.1.0-linux-amd64.tar.gz` present
- [ ] `zolem-0.1.0-linux-arm64.tar.gz` present
- [ ] `zolem-0.1.0-darwin-arm64.tar.gz` present
- [ ] `checksums.txt` present
- [ ] `sha256sum -c checksums.txt` passes after downloading all three archives
- [ ] `zolem-0.1.0-linux-amd64.tar.gz.sbom` present
- [ ] `zolem-0.1.0-linux-arm64.tar.gz.sbom` present
- [ ] `zolem-0.1.0-darwin-arm64.tar.gz.sbom` present
- [ ] `<archive>.bundle` cosign signature files present (one per archive)
- [ ] `cosign verify-blob` passes for at least one archive using the resolved command above

**Docker**:
- [ ] `docker pull ghcr.io/ketang/zolem:v0.1.0` succeeds
- [ ] `docker pull ghcr.io/ketang/zolem:latest` succeeds and resolves to same digest as `v0.1.0`
- [ ] `docker run --rm ghcr.io/ketang/zolem:v0.1.0` exits 1 with `choose either -local-admin-addr or -local-provider`
- [ ] `docker manifest inspect ghcr.io/ketang/zolem:v0.1.0` shows `linux/amd64` and `linux/arm64` platforms

## In scope

- Tagging and release verification only
- No source code changes

## Notes

- `darwin/amd64` is intentionally excluded from the release matrix (Intel Mac is not a supported target).
- Secrets: `GITHUB_TOKEN` is automatic on GitHub Actions; `id-token: write` permission (set in `release.yml`) is sufficient for cosign keyless OIDC — no additional secrets required.

## Out of scope

- Changelog or release notes authoring
- Follow-on packaging (brew tap, aqua, npm, etc.)

title: Rewrite nightly.yml as GoReleaser wrapper
description:
## What

Replace the current hand-rolled `nightly.yml` with a thin wrapper that uses
GoReleaser to produce the same artifact set as tagged releases, published to a
rolling `nightly` prerelease on GitHub and `ghcr.io/ketang/zolem:nightly`.

## Current state

`.github/workflows/nightly.yml` (trigger: `push` to `main`):
- Computes a timestamp asset name
- Builds one `linux-amd64` binary
- Deletes nightly release assets older than 7 days
- Publishes to a rolling `nightly` prerelease

## Target state

**Trigger**: unchanged — `push` to `main`.

**Permissions**:
```yaml
permissions:
  contents: write
  packages: write
  id-token: write
```

**Steps**:
1. `actions/checkout@v4` with `fetch-depth: 0`
2. `actions/setup-go@v5` with `go-version-file: go.mod`
3. `docker/setup-buildx-action@v3`
4. `docker/login-action@v3` to `ghcr.io` with `GITHUB_TOKEN`
5. `sigstore/cosign-installer@v3`
6. Force-create or update the `nightly` git tag:
   ```bash
   git tag -f nightly
   git push -f origin nightly
   ```
7. `goreleaser/goreleaser-action@v6` with `version: "~> v2"` and
   `args: release --clean` with env `GORELEASER_CURRENT_TAG=nightly`

**Why this approach**: GoReleaser's `--snapshot` mode does not publish to
GitHub releases. To publish to the existing `nightly` prerelease (GitHub
release with `tag: nightly, prerelease: true`), goreleaser must run in full
release mode with a `nightly` tag. Force-pushing the tag ensures goreleaser
finds the correct ref. The goreleaser config sets `make_latest: false` and
`prerelease: true` for nightly builds.

**Artifact set**: same three platform archives as tagged releases
(`linux/amd64`, `linux/arm64`, `darwin/arm64`) plus checksums, SBOMs, cosign
signatures, and Docker image tagged `nightly`. GoReleaser replaces assets on
the existing `nightly` release on each run — no accumulation, no manual
cleanup needed.

## Resolved facts

- `goreleaser/goreleaser-action@v6`, `version: "~> v2"` — same pin as `release.yml`
- `GITHUB_TOKEN` is sufficient for ghcr.io pushes with `packages: write`
- The 7-day asset cleanup step in the current `nightly.yml` is dropped; goreleaser replaces assets in-place
- The nightly Docker tag is `nightly`, not `latest`; the `.goreleaser.yaml` snapshot config must distinguish the two

## Dependency

Requires `.goreleaser.yaml` to exist and handle the `GORELEASER_CURRENT_TAG=nightly` case.

## Acceptance checks

- [ ] Push to main triggers the workflow
- [ ] GitHub `nightly` prerelease is updated with all three platform archives + `checksums.txt` + `.sbom` files
- [ ] `ghcr.io/ketang/zolem:nightly` is updated after each push to main
- [ ] A second push to main updates the `nightly` release in-place (no duplicate assets)
- [ ] `nightly` Docker tag does not update `latest`

## In scope

- `.github/workflows/nightly.yml` only

## Out of scope

- The goreleaser config (separate issue)
- Tagged release workflow (separate issue)

title: Rewrite release.yml as GoReleaser wrapper
description:
## What

Replace the current hand-rolled `release.yml` with a thin wrapper that invokes
`goreleaser release --clean`.

## Current state

`.github/workflows/release.yml` (trigger: `push` on `v*` tags):
- Sets up Go
- Builds one `linux-amd64` binary with `go build`
- Attaches binary to GitHub Release via `softprops/action-gh-release@v2`
- No checksums, no signing, no SBOM, no Docker

## Target state

**Trigger**: unchanged — `push` on `v*` tags.

**Permissions**:
```yaml
permissions:
  contents: write    # publish release assets
  packages: write    # push to ghcr.io
  id-token: write    # cosign keyless OIDC
```

**Steps**:
1. `actions/checkout@v4` with `fetch-depth: 0` (goreleaser needs full tag history)
2. `actions/setup-go@v5` with `go-version-file: go.mod`
3. `docker/setup-buildx-action@v3` (multi-arch image builds)
4. `docker/login-action@v3` to `ghcr.io` with `GITHUB_TOKEN`
5. Install cosign via `sigstore/cosign-installer@v3`
6. `goreleaser/goreleaser-action@v6` with `version: "~> v2"`, `args: release --clean`

## Resolved facts

- `ghcr.io/ketang/zolem` is confirmed from `git remote -v` (`git@github.com:ketang/zolem.git`)
- `darwin/arm64` cross-compiles via Go's `GOOS`/`GOARCH`; no macOS runner required
- `GITHUB_TOKEN` is sufficient for ghcr.io pushes when `packages: write` is set
- Cosign keyless uses OIDC from GitHub Actions identity; no `COSIGN_PASSWORD` or key secrets

## Artifacts produced (via .goreleaser.yaml)

- `zolem-<version>-linux-amd64.tar.gz`
- `zolem-<version>-linux-arm64.tar.gz`
- `zolem-<version>-darwin-arm64.tar.gz`
- `checksums.txt`
- `<archive>.sbom` per archive (CycloneDX)
- `<archive>.bundle` cosign signatures
- Docker multi-arch manifest: `ghcr.io/ketang/zolem:vX.Y.Z` + `:latest`
- Cosign signature on Docker manifest in ghcr.io

## Dependency

Requires `.goreleaser.yaml` to exist and pass `goreleaser check`.

## Acceptance checks

- [ ] Workflow file passes actionlint
- [ ] Triggering with a `v*` tag produces all expected artifacts on the GitHub Release
- [ ] `ghcr.io/ketang/zolem:latest` is pullable after the release

## In scope

- `.github/workflows/release.yml` only

## Out of scope

- The goreleaser config (separate issue)
- Nightly workflow (separate issue)

title: Add .goreleaser.yaml
description:
## What

Add a GoReleaser config at the repo root that drives the full release artifact
set: multi-platform binaries, archives, checksums, SBOM, cosign signatures,
and multi-arch Docker image.

## Resolved facts

- Module path: `zolem.dev/zolem` (`go.mod` line 1)
- Go version: `1.26.3` (`go.mod` line 3)
- Binary: `./cmd/zolem`
- GitHub repo slug: `ketang/zolem` (`git remote -v`)
- Docker registry: `ghcr.io/ketang/zolem`
- Docker base image: `gcr.io/distroless/base:nonroot` (see Dockerfile issue)
- GoReleaser action: `goreleaser/goreleaser-action@v6` with `version: "~> v2"`
- No existing `.goreleaser.yaml`

## Target config shape

### Builds

Three targets, all `CGO_ENABLED=0`. Archives as `.tar.gz`, no Windows:

```
linux/amd64   → zolem-{{ .Version }}-linux-amd64.tar.gz
linux/arm64   → zolem-{{ .Version }}-linux-arm64.tar.gz
darwin/arm64  → zolem-{{ .Version }}-darwin-arm64.tar.gz
```

### Checksums

`checksums.txt` with SHA256 for all archives.

### SBOM

CycloneDX format via goreleaser's syft integration. One SBOM per archive,
named `<archive-name>.sbom`. Config:

```yaml
sboms:
  - artifacts: archive
    documents:
      - "{{ .ArtifactName }}.sbom"
```

### Signs (cosign keyless, binary archives)

```yaml
signs:
  - artifacts: all
    cmd: cosign
    args:
      - sign-blob
      - --yes
      - "--bundle=${signature}"
      - "${artifact}"
    signature: "${artifact}.bundle"
```

Requires `id-token: write` in calling workflow. No secrets.

### Docker

```yaml
dockers:
  - image_templates:
      - "ghcr.io/ketang/zolem:{{ .Version }}"
      - "ghcr.io/ketang/zolem:latest"     # only on non-snapshot builds
    goos: linux
    goarch: amd64
    use: buildx
    dockerfile: Dockerfile
    # goreleaser copies the pre-built binary; builder stage is skipped

  - image_templates:
      - "ghcr.io/ketang/zolem:{{ .Version }}-arm64"
    goos: linux
    goarch: arm64
    use: buildx

docker_manifests:
  - name_template: "ghcr.io/ketang/zolem:{{ .Version }}"
    image_templates:
      - "ghcr.io/ketang/zolem:{{ .Version }}"
      - "ghcr.io/ketang/zolem:{{ .Version }}-arm64"
  - name_template: "ghcr.io/ketang/zolem:latest"
    image_templates:
      - "ghcr.io/ketang/zolem:{{ .Version }}"
      - "ghcr.io/ketang/zolem:{{ .Version }}-arm64"
```

### Docker signs

```yaml
docker_signs:
  - artifacts: all
    args:
      - sign
      - --yes
      - "${artifact}"
```

Requires `packages: write` + `id-token: write`.

### Nightly / snapshot

Snapshot name template: `{{ .Version }}-nightly`. Docker tag for snapshot
builds: `nightly` (not `latest`). The nightly workflow uses
`GORELEASER_CURRENT_TAG=nightly` with a force-updated `nightly` git tag;
see the nightly.yml issue for the full approach.

## Acceptance checks

- [ ] `goreleaser check` passes with no errors
- [ ] `goreleaser build --snapshot --clean` produces binaries for all three platforms locally
- [ ] `goreleaser release --snapshot --clean` produces archives, `checksums.txt`, and `.sbom` files locally (without pushing)

## In scope

- `.goreleaser.yaml` at repo root only

## Out of scope

- GitHub Actions workflows (separate issues)
- Homebrew tap, Scoop bucket, aqua registry entries
- Windows or darwin/amd64 targets

title: Add Dockerfile
description:
## What

Add a two-stage Dockerfile to the repo root for local `docker build` use and
as the source for GoReleaser's Docker image builds.

## Target state

File: `Dockerfile` at repo root.

```dockerfile
FROM golang:1.26-bookworm AS builder
WORKDIR /src
COPY . .
RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 go build -o zolem ./cmd/zolem

FROM gcr.io/distroless/base:nonroot
COPY --from=builder /src/zolem /zolem
ENTRYPOINT ["/zolem"]
```

## Resolved facts

- **Base image**: `gcr.io/distroless/base:nonroot`, not `distroless/static:nonroot`. Reason: `internal/specs/fetcher.go:82` makes outgoing HTTPS calls to fetch OpenAPI specs from github.com, googleapis.com, and openrouter.ai. `distroless/base:nonroot` includes CA certs; `distroless/static:nonroot` does not. Embedded fallbacks exist for all providers (`internal/specs/vendored.go`, `internal/specs/loader.go`) so the binary works without network, but CA certs are needed for fresh spec validation
- **Builder image**: `golang:1.26-bookworm` — matches `go.mod` (`go 1.26.3`) and `scripts/shatter-full-scan.sh` (`SHATTER_SANDBOX_DOCKER_IMAGE=golang:1.26-bookworm`)
- **Binary target**: `./cmd/zolem` only; `cmd/zolemc` is a companion CLI, not packaged in the image
- **`CGO_ENABLED=0`**: required for static binary; distroless base has no libc
- **GoReleaser path**: in the release pipeline, goreleaser copies its pre-compiled binary into only the final stage (no builder stage needed in the release path). The two-stage Dockerfile is for `docker build .` locally
- **Smoke test command**: `docker run --rm <image>` exits with code 1 and prints `choose either -local-admin-addr or -local-provider` to stderr — standard Go `flag.Parse()` behavior when no required flag is set; confirms the binary starts correctly and the entrypoint is wired

## Acceptance checks

- [ ] `docker build .` completes successfully
- [ ] `docker run --rm <image>` exits 1 and prints `choose either -local-admin-addr or -local-provider`
- [ ] `docker inspect <image>` shows `User: nonroot` (or UID 65532)

## In scope

- `Dockerfile` at repo root only

## Out of scope

- `.dockerignore`
- docker-compose files
- Publishing the image (covered by GoReleaser workflow issues)
- Multi-arch manifest (GoReleaser handles that via buildx)

title: Expand ci.yml: add PR trigger and smoke step
description:
## What

Add `pull_request` trigger to `ci.yml` so feature branches are tested before
merge. Add `./scripts/smoke.sh` as a final step in the existing CI job.

## Current state

`.github/workflows/ci.yml` triggers only on `push` to `main`. Steps:
1. `go test ./...`
2. `go build ./cmd/zolem`

No PR trigger. No smoke test.

## Target state

Triggers:
```yaml
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
```

Job steps (single runner `ubuntu-latest`, no matrix):
1. Check out repo
2. Set up Go (via `go-version-file: go.mod`, currently `go 1.26.3`)
3. `go test ./...`
4. `go build ./cmd/zolem`
5. `./scripts/smoke.sh`

## Resolved facts

- `scripts/smoke.sh` is mode `100755` in git; `./scripts/smoke.sh` works on an ubuntu-latest runner without chmod
- `scripts/smoke.sh` calls `scripts/test-local-runtime.sh` twice (default backend, then `PROFILE_BACKEND=fixture`). It starts zolem on loopback addresses, creates profiles and listeners via zolemc, makes HTTP requests, verifies responses. No internet access required; no ports conflict with standard CI services
- `go-version-file: go.mod` already used in `release.yml` and `nightly.yml`; same approach applies here

## Acceptance checks

- [ ] A pull_request targeting main triggers the CI job
- [ ] `smoke.sh` runs and passes in CI on ubuntu-latest
- [ ] Existing push-to-main trigger still works
- [ ] No matrix: one runner, three steps

## In scope

- Modifying `.github/workflows/ci.yml` only

## Out of scope

- Matrix builds
- Linting, staticcheck, or other quality gates

title: CI, releases, and Docker
description:
Replace the existing hand-rolled GitHub Actions release pipeline with GoReleaser,
add a multi-arch Docker image published to ghcr.io, expand CI to cover pull
requests and include the smoke test, and cut the first stable release tag (v0.1.0).

## Background

Current state:
- `ci.yml`: fires on push to main only; runs `go test ./...` and `go build ./cmd/zolem`; no PR trigger; no smoke test
- `release.yml`: fires on `v*` tags; builds one linux-amd64 binary only; no checksums, no signing, no SBOM; no tagged releases exist yet (only `nightly`)
- `nightly.yml`: fires on push to main; builds one linux-amd64 binary; rolling `nightly` prerelease; 7-day asset cleanup

Target state (from approved design doc at `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-23-ci-releases-docker-design.md`):
- GoReleaser drives all binary and Docker builds
- Binary matrix: linux/amd64, linux/arm64, darwin/arm64
- Docker image: ghcr.io/ketang/zolem, multi-arch (linux/amd64 + linux/arm64), distroless/static:nonroot base
- Integrity: SHA256 checksums, CycloneDX SBOM, cosign keyless signatures
- CI: PR trigger added, smoke.sh added to CI job
- First stable release: v0.1.0 tagged after pipeline lands

## Child tasks

1. Expand ci.yml — PR trigger + smoke step
2. Add Dockerfile
3. Add .goreleaser.yaml
4. Rewrite release.yml as GoReleaser wrapper
5. Rewrite nightly.yml as GoReleaser wrapper
6. Cut v0.1.0 and verify release artifacts

## Out of scope

- Homebrew tap, Scoop bucket, aqua registry, npm/PyPI packages
- Windows binaries
- Intel Mac (darwin/amd64) binaries
- Docker Hub
- KMS-backed signing, TUF roots, SLSA Level 3+
- Shatter CI wiring

title: Deprecate per-fixture match.cel and match.wasm
description:
# Deprecate per-fixture match.cel and match.wasm

## Why

After the Selector interface, `fixtures.yaml`, sequences, and the WASM selector land, per-fixture `match.cel` and `match.wasm` are legacy paths: they scatter selection logic across fixture directories and rely on the continuous scoring model. This issue marks them deprecated with a startup warning to guide authors toward `fixtures.yaml`.

Depends on: zolem-5ef.1, zolem-5ef.2, zolem-5ef.3 all landed.

## What to build

### Deprecation warning at fixture load time

In `loadFixtures()` (`cmd/zolem/startup.go:214`) or in `loadOne()` (`loader.go:56`): when a fixture is loaded from a namespace that has no `fixtures.yaml` and no `selector.wasm`, and the fixture has a `match.cel` expression or a `match.wasm` file, append a deprecation warning to the `warnings []string` slice, following the pattern at `startup.go:230-232`:

```go
warnings = append(warnings, fmt.Sprintf(
    "fixture %q: match.cel is deprecated; migrate to fixtures.yaml — see docs/local-runtime.md", f.ID))
warnings = append(warnings, fmt.Sprintf(
    "fixture %q: match.wasm is deprecated; migrate to fixtures.yaml or a namespace-level selector.wasm — see docs/local-runtime.md", f.ID))
```

Per-fixture matchers continue to work. Warning-only deprecation.

The namespace check (no `fixtures.yaml`, no `selector.wasm`) can be done in `loadFixtures()` at `startup.go:214` where the namespace directory path is already known — check for both files before calling `loadOne` on subdirs.

### Documentation update (`docs/local-runtime.md`)

- Mark the `match.cel` and `match.wasm` per-fixture sections as deprecated.
- Add a migration section showing how to convert a per-fixture `match.cel` to a `fixtures.yaml` entry.
- Point readers to `fixtures.yaml` as the recommended path.

## Acceptance checks

- [ ] A namespace with a fixture containing `match.cel` but no `fixtures.yaml` emits a deprecation warning at startup; the fixture still serves correctly.
- [ ] A namespace with a fixture containing `match.wasm` but no `fixtures.yaml` emits a deprecation warning at startup; the fixture still serves correctly.
- [ ] A namespace with `fixtures.yaml` and no per-fixture matchers emits no deprecation warnings.
- [ ] Warning text references `docs/local-runtime.md`.
- [ ] `docs/local-runtime.md` includes a migration example.
- [ ] `go test ./internal/fixture/...` and `go test ./cmd/zolem/...` pass with no regressions.

## Out of scope

- Removing per-fixture matcher support — future breaking change after deprecation window.
- Automated migration tooling.

## Current code facts

- `cmd/zolem/startup.go:230-232` — existing "has no matcher" warning pattern to follow.
- `cmd/zolem/startup.go:214-250` — `loadFixtures()` — appropriate site for namespace-level check and warnings.
- `internal/fixture/loader.go:86-92` — `match.wasm` and `match.cel` detection in `loadOne()`; trigger condition for warnings.
- `internal/fixture/fixture.go:33-35` — `HasMatcher()` — a fixture returning true here with no `fixtures.yaml`/`selector.wasm` in its namespace is the trigger.
- `internal/fixture/matcher_test.go` — WASM scoring tests; must still pass.
- `internal/fixture/error_paths_test.go` — error handling for bad WASM/CEL; must still pass.
- `cmd/zolem/local_runtime_e2e_test.go` — CEL fixture E2E test added in d20fa08; must still pass.

title: Add namespace-level WASM selector
description:
# Add namespace-level WASM selector

## Why

`fixtures.yaml` handles the common case (CEL expressions, sequences) but cannot express selection logic that requires arbitrary computation: stateful round-robin across matching fixtures, request-body-driven state machines, or picking fixtures based on logic that doesn't fit CEL. A namespace-level WASM selector provides an opaque escape hatch with the full fixture pool available as input.

Depends on: zolem-5ef.1 (Selector interface).

## What to build

### selector.wasm in the namespace root

A file named `selector.wasm` in the namespace root activates the WASM selector for that namespace. Mutually exclusive with `fixtures.yaml`; the loader returns a load-time error if both are present.

### ABI: `select(inputPtr i32, inputLen i32) -> (outputPtr i32, outputLen i32)`

Input JSON written to WASM memory before the call:

```json
{
  "request": {
    "provider": "anthropic",
    "version": "v1",
    "labels": {"method": "GET", "path": "/v1/messages/batches/{id}"},
    "body": { ... }
  },
  "fixtures": [
    {"id": "batch-in-progress", "tags": {"state": "in_progress", "endpoint": "batch-poll"}},
    {"id": "batch-ended",       "tags": {"state": "ended",       "endpoint": "batch-poll"}},
    {"id": "message-create",    "tags": {}}
  ]
}
```

Output JSON written to WASM memory by the function:

```json
{"id": "batch-in-progress"}
```

No match: `{"id": null}` or empty body. Framework falls through to the configured backend.

The WASM module must export `memory` and the `select` function. Host writes input at offset 0, calls `select(0, len)`, copies output immediately from the returned pointer+length (buffer lifetime bounded to the call). Follow the locking pattern in `internal/fixture/wasm.go`.

If the returned ID is not in the candidates slice, return a runtime error from `wasmSelector.Select`.

### tags field in meta.yaml

Add optional `tags: map[string]string` to `meta` struct (`internal/fixture/loader.go:11`). Include in `Fixture` struct and in the fixtures list passed to the WASM selector. Fixtures without `tags` appear with an empty map.

```yaml
# meta.yaml
id: batch-in-progress
status: 200
tags:
  state: in_progress
  endpoint: batch-poll
```

### wasmSelector

Implements `Selector`. On `Select`: serialise input JSON, write to WASM memory, call `select`, read and parse output, look up returned ID in candidates.

Reuse `Runner.CompileWASM()` and `CompiledModule` from `internal/fixture/wasm.go` for compilation. One compiled instance per namespace, shared across concurrent requests; follow existing locking pattern.

### Loader changes

In `Loader.Load()`: detect `selector.wasm` in the namespace root, compile it, construct a `wasmSelector`. Return load-time error if `fixtures.yaml` is also present.

### E2E test WASM

Build the test WASM as a raw byte literal in the test file, following the pattern in `internal/fixture/matcher_test.go` (`highMatchWASM`, `lowMatchWASM` etc.). Include the WAT source as a comment above the bytes.

## Acceptance checks

- [ ] A namespace with `selector.wasm` routes requests to the fixture ID returned by the WASM function.
- [ ] WASM returning `{"id": null}` falls through to the configured backend.
- [ ] WASM returning an ID not in the fixture pool returns an error from `Select`.
- [ ] A namespace with both `selector.wasm` and `fixtures.yaml` fails to load with a clear error.
- [ ] `tags:` declared in `meta.yaml` appear correctly in the fixtures list passed to the WASM.
- [ ] Fixtures without `tags:` appear with an empty map, not null.
- [ ] `go test ./internal/fixture/...` passes.
- [ ] E2E test: WASM selector picks the fixture whose tag matches a label from the request.

## Out of scope

- WASM selector returning a response body or template — selector returns a fixture ID only.
- Deprecation of per-fixture matchers — zolem-5ef.4.

## Current code facts

- `internal/fixture/wasm.go` — `Runner`, `CompiledModule`, `Runner.Score()`; reuse `Runner.CompileWASM()`.
- `internal/fixture/loader.go:86-92` — existing `match.wasm` detection and mutual-exclusion check; mirror for `selector.wasm`.
- `internal/fixture/loader.go:11` — `meta` struct — add `Tags map[string]string \`yaml:"tags"\``.
- `internal/fixture/fixture.go:6-19` — `Fixture` struct — add `Tags map[string]string`.
- `internal/fixture/matcher_test.go` — raw WASM byte literal pattern for test fixtures.
- `cmd/zolem/startup.go:214-250` — `loadFixtures()` — Selector from `Loader.Load()` already wired after zolem-5ef.1.

comments.author: Ketan Gangatirkar
comments.text:
Design review note: mutex scope and intent need to be explicit in the implementation spec.

The issue says 'follow existing locking pattern' but the existing pattern (wasm.go) avoids the problem entirely via fresh instantiation per call — a stateless model. The selector.wasm pattern is intentionally stateful, so that pointer is misleading.

The mutex must cover the entire write-call-copy round trip (write input to memory → call select → copy output from returned pointer). It cannot be split: releasing the lock between write and copy allows a concurrent request to overwrite linear memory before the first caller reads the output.

The serialization is correct and intentional: shared linear memory is what enables statefulness (round-robin, request counters, protocol simulation), and coherent state mutations across concurrent callers require serialization regardless of implementation. The real cost is throughput — all requests to the namespace queue behind one lock when the WASM module is slow.

The spec should state: (1) the critical section spans write-call-copy, (2) serialization is load-bearing for state coherence, and (3) under concurrent load this becomes a per-namespace bottleneck.

title: Add sequence entries to fixtures.yaml
description:
# Add sequence entries to fixtures.yaml

## Why

Users mocking state-transition APIs (batch polling, job status) and paginated endpoints today must hand-roll a single templated fixture that branches on `.Sequence.ProfileRequest`. That counter is per-profile and shared across all endpoints, so "request N to this endpoint" is not expressible without coupling to other fixture counters in the same profile.

This issue adds first-class sequence support to `fixtures.yaml`: an entry whose right-hand side is an ordered list of fixture IDs stepped through on successive matching requests.

Depends on: zolem-5ef.1 (fixtures.yaml + Selector interface).

## What to build

### Sequence entry shape in fixtures.yaml

```yaml
provider: anthropic
version: v1
fixtures:
  - expression: 'labels["method"] == "GET" && labels["path"] == "/v1/messages/batches/{id}"'
    sequence:
      id: batch-poll
      on_exhaust: last
      steps:
        - batch-in-progress
        - batch-in-progress
        - batch-ended

  - expression: 'labels["method"] == "GET" && labels["path"] == "/v1/models"'
    sequence:
      id: list-models
      on_exhaust: cycle
      steps:
        - models-page-1
        - models-page-2
        - models-page-3
```

A `sequence:` entry and a `fixture:` (single) entry are mutually exclusive on the same entry. Load-time error: `"fixtures.yaml entry %q: fixture and sequence are mutually exclusive"`.

`on_exhaust` values:
- `last` — keep returning the final step fixture indefinitely.
- `cycle` — wrap back to step 0.
- `error` — return a provider-native error response. Inspect how `BackendError` generates responses near `cmd/zolem/startup.go:293` and reuse that path. Default to `overloaded_error` / `server_error`; document the chosen default.
- `fallthrough` — stop matching this entry; continue evaluating subsequent `fixtures.yaml` entries for this request. Counter is incremented when the sequence matches; on exhaustion with `fallthrough`, selector returns nil for this entry and stays at `len(steps)` for future requests.

### Per-sequence counter

Each sequence has its own step counter keyed by `(profile name, sequence id)`. Counters are in-memory, reset on server restart. Follow the `sync.Mutex` pattern in `ProfileCounters` (`internal/runtime/counters.go:19-28`). Add a parallel counter store or extend `ProfileCounters` with a nested sequence map.

### New file: `internal/fixture/sequence.go`

Sequence state management: counter store, step resolution, `on_exhaust` dispatch.

### fixturesYAMLSelector changes

When a sequence entry matches: read current step index, resolve `steps[index]` to a fixture, increment the counter, apply `on_exhaust` if at the last step.

## Acceptance checks

- [ ] Three successive matching requests to a 3-step sequence return step 0, step 1, step 2 bodies in order.
- [ ] `on_exhaust: last` — request 4 through N all return the final step body.
- [ ] `on_exhaust: cycle` — request 4 returns step 0 body.
- [ ] `on_exhaust: error` — request 4 returns a provider-native error response with appropriate status code.
- [ ] `on_exhaust: fallthrough` — request 4 continues evaluating subsequent `fixtures.yaml` entries; falls through to the configured backend if none match.
- [ ] Two sequences in the same namespace advance independently (request to sequence A does not advance sequence B's counter).
- [ ] Steps referencing a non-existent fixture ID fail to load with a clear error.
- [ ] `fixture:` and `sequence:` on the same entry produce a load-time error.
- [ ] `go test ./internal/fixture/...` passes.
- [ ] E2E test: Anthropic batch-poll pattern — GET /v1/messages/batches/{id} returns `processing_status: in_progress` twice then `processing_status: ended`.

## Out of scope

- WASM selector — zolem-5ef.3.
- Deprecation of per-fixture matchers — zolem-5ef.4.
- Exposing current sequence step as a template variable.

## Current code facts

- `internal/runtime/counters.go:5-13` — `ProfileCounters` with `map[string]*ProfileSequence`; model new sequence counter store on this.
- `internal/runtime/counters.go:19-28` — `IncrementProfileRequest` mutex pattern to follow.
- `cmd/zolem/startup.go:141` — `runtimecfg.NewProfileCounters()` passed into startup; create sequence counters at the same site.
- `cmd/zolem/startup.go:293` — `BackendError` dispatch; check adjacent code for error response generation to reuse for `on_exhaust: error`.

comments.author: Ketan Gangatirkar
comments.text:
Design review note: on_exhaust: error is a layering violation.

The issue directs implementors to reuse BackendError near cmd/zolem/startup.go:293 for generating provider-native error responses. BackendError lives in the handler/startup layer; internal/fixture/ cannot reach it without importing cmd-layer code into an internal package, which is an import cycle.

Fix: the selector should return a typed sentinel value (e.g. *ExhaustError or a flag on the return) rather than constructing the error response itself. The handler layer translates the sentinel into a provider-native error response using the existing BackendError path. This keeps the fixture package ignorant of provider wire formats.

comments.author: Ketan Gangatirkar
comments.text:
Design review note: sequence counter keys are too narrow.

The issue specifies keying by (profile name, sequence id). If two namespaces each define a sequence with the same id (e.g. id: poll), they share a counter and advance each other's step index.

The key must include a namespace discriminator — something like (profile, namespace, sequence id) where namespace is derived from the fixture directory path or a canonical (provider, version) pair. Without this, sequence isolation across namespaces breaks under any real fixture layout.

title: Add fixtures.yaml for namespace-level expression-based fixture selection
description:
# Add fixtures.yaml for namespace-level expression-based fixture selection

## Why

Currently, each fixture that wants to be matched must embed a `match.cel` expression or `match.wasm` file. Selection logic is scattered across N fixture directories, the matcher uses a continuous scoring loop to pick the highest non-negative scorer, and fixtures without a matcher silently never serve (only a warning is emitted). This is hard to reason about: understanding what a namespace matches requires opening every `meta.yaml`.

This issue introduces `fixtures.yaml` as the namespace-level selection manifest: a single file that declares ordered expressions paired with fixture IDs. It is the foundation for sequences (Issue 2) and the WASM selector (Issue 3).

## What to build

### New: `Selector` interface (`internal/fixture/selector.go`)

```go
type Selector interface {
    Select(ctx context.Context, req MatchRequest, candidates []Fixture) (*Fixture, error)
}
```

`Matcher.Match()` currently runs the scoring loop itself (`matcher.go:41-68`). Replace that loop with delegation to the active `Selector`. `NewMatcher` accepts a `Selector`; if nil it falls back to the current per-fixture scoring behavior for backward compatibility during migration.

### New: `fixturesYAMLSelector`

Loaded from `fixtures.yaml` in the namespace root. Evaluates entries in declared order; returns the first fixture whose `expression` matches the request. Uses `CompileCELMatcher` (`cel.go:18`) internally — the CEL compilation and evaluation infrastructure is unchanged, it just moves from per-fixture to per-entry-in-fixtures.yaml.

`fixtures.yaml` schema:

```yaml
provider: anthropic
version: v1
fixtures:
  - expression: 'labels["method"] == "POST" && labels["path"] == "/v1/messages"'
    fixture: message-create

  - expression: 'labels["method"] == "GET" && labels["path"] == "/v1/models"'
    fixture: list-models-response
```

- `expression` is a CEL boolean expression. Same variables as today: `provider`, `version`, `labels`, `body`.
- `fixture` is the ID of a fixture directory in the same namespace.
- Entries are evaluated in declaration order; first match wins. No scoring.

### Loader changes (`internal/fixture/loader.go`)

`Loader.Load()` currently walks subdirectories and calls `loadOne` for each (`loader.go:36-53`). Extend to:

1. Check if `fixtures.yaml` exists in the namespace root.
2. If yes: parse it, build a `fixturesYAMLSelector`, return it alongside the loaded fixtures. Emit a load-time error if any fixture directory in the namespace also has a `match.cel` or `match.wasm` file — the two mechanisms are mutually exclusive per namespace.
3. If no: existing behavior unchanged (per-fixture matchers, scoring loop).

### Fixture struct (`internal/fixture/fixture.go`)

`CELMatcher *CompiledCELMatcher` (line 18) moves off the `Fixture` struct into the selector layer. The field is no longer populated by `loadOne`. `HasMatcher()` (line 33) becomes meaningful only in the legacy (no `fixtures.yaml`) path — document this.

### Startup wiring (`cmd/zolem/startup.go`)

`loadFixtures()` (line 214) calls `fixture.NewLoader(fixturesDir)` then `loader.Load()`. It then calls `fixture.NewMatcher(runner, fixtures)` (line 161). After this change: `loader.Load()` returns `([]Fixture, Selector, error)`. Pass the selector into `NewMatcher`. The warning at line 230-232 ("fixture has no matcher - will never match") only applies when no `fixtures.yaml` is present.

## Acceptance checks

- [ ] A namespace with `fixtures.yaml` correctly routes two requests with different CEL expressions to different fixtures.
- [ ] Entries are evaluated in declaration order; an earlier entry wins over a later one that also matches.
- [ ] A namespace with `fixtures.yaml` and a fixture directory that also has `match.cel` or `match.wasm` fails to load with a clear error.
- [ ] A namespace without `fixtures.yaml` behaves identically to today (per-fixture CEL/WASM scoring, existing tests pass).
- [ ] A fixture referenced in `fixtures.yaml` but not found in the directory fails to load with a clear error.
- [ ] The "fixture has no matcher" warning is not emitted for fixtures in a `fixtures.yaml` namespace.
- [ ] `go test ./internal/fixture/...` passes.
- [ ] E2E test in `cmd/zolem/local_runtime_e2e_test.go` exercises a `fixtures.yaml` namespace with two differently-matched fixtures.

## Out of scope

- Sequences (`steps:`, `on_exhaust:`) — Issue 2.
- WASM selector — Issue 3.
- Deprecation of per-fixture matchers — Issue 4.
- `tags:` field on `meta.yaml` — Issue 3.

## Current code facts

- `internal/fixture/matcher.go:10-15` — `MatchRequest` struct (Provider, Version, Labels, Body); use as the `req` parameter in `Selector.Select`.
- `internal/fixture/matcher.go:41-68` — `Match()` scoring loop to replace.
- `internal/fixture/matcher.go:71-79` — `score()` dispatcher; remains for legacy path.
- `internal/fixture/fixture.go:18` — `CELMatcher *CompiledCELMatcher` field to remove from struct.
- `internal/fixture/fixture.go:33-35` — `HasMatcher()` — still valid in legacy path.
- `internal/fixture/cel.go:18` — `CompileCELMatcher(expr string, score float64)` — reuse for `fixtures.yaml` entries; pass `score=1`. Score value is not consulted by `fixturesYAMLSelector` (first-match-wins); if `CompiledCELMatcher` only exposes `Score()`, call it and treat any non-negative return as a match.
- `internal/fixture/loader.go:11-24` — `meta` and `match` structs; `Match.CEL` becomes illegal when `fixtures.yaml` present.
- `internal/fixture/loader.go:36-53` — `Loader.Load()` — extend to detect and parse `fixtures.yaml`; change return to `([]Fixture, Selector, error)`.
- `cmd/zolem/startup.go:214-250` — `loadFixtures()` — wiring point for selector handoff.
- `cmd/zolem/startup.go:161` — `fixture.NewMatcher(runner, fixtures)` — add selector parameter.
- `cmd/zolem/startup.go:230-232` — "has no matcher" warning — scope-gate to legacy path.
close_reason: Landed in main via merge 16c1af0a. Added fixtures.yaml namespace-level CEL selector and Selector interface; loader returns ([]Fixture, Selector, error); legacy per-fixture scoring preserved on no-yaml path.

title: Fixture selection and sequencing overhaul
description:
Replace per-fixture scoring matchers with a namespace-level fixtures.yaml manifest supporting CEL expression matching, ordered sequences, and an escape-hatch WASM selector. Deprecate per-fixture match.cel / match.wasm as a follow-on.

Child issues:
1. fixtures.yaml expression-based selection (Selector interface foundation)
2. Sequence entries in fixtures.yaml (steps, on_exhaust)
3. Namespace-level WASM selector
4. Deprecate per-fixture matchers

title: E2E test coverage for call history
description:
# E2E test coverage for call history

## Summary

Add end-to-end tests covering the full call-history feature: admin-server
recording, fixed-listener JSONL recording, and zolemc CLI integration.

## Context

Design spec: `docs/specs/2026-05-22-call-history-design.md`

**Depends on issues 1–4** all being merged. This issue adds tests only; it does
not change any production code.

## Current code facts

- `cmd/zolem/local_runtime_e2e_test.go` — main E2E test file, 33 KB. Tests
  start a real admin server using `integration_helpers_test.go`, create
  profiles and listeners via the HTTP API, and make provider-shaped requests
  through the listener. New admin-server recording tests go here.
- `cmd/zolem/integration_helpers_test.go` — 14 KB. Provides `startAdminServer`,
  `createProfile`, `createListener`, `doRequest` helpers. Any new helpers for
  call-history assertions (e.g., `getCalls`, `clearCalls`) go here.
- `cmd/zolem/startup_local_test.go` — 12 KB. Tests for fixed-listener startup
  path. New JSONL recording tests go here or in a dedicated
  `cmd/zolem/local_calls_e2e_test.go` file.
- `cmd/zolemc/main_e2e_test.go` — 8.9 KB. Tests for zolemc CLI subcommands,
  using a real admin server. New `listeners calls list/clear` tests go here.
- Test pattern: tests use `t.TempDir()` for ephemeral directories and
  `httptest` or `net/http` for real HTTP calls. No mocking of the zolem
  internals in E2E tests.

## Work

### Admin server (local runtime) tests in `cmd/zolem/local_runtime_e2e_test.go`

Add the following test cases:

**Basic recording**
- Create a listener with a lorem backend.
- Send one `POST /v1/chat/completions` request.
- `GET /_zolem/listeners/<name>/calls` → assert `calls` has length 1.
- Assert `call_id == 1`, correct `method`, `path`, `status`.
- Assert `request.body` contains the posted JSON body.
- Assert `response.body` is non-empty (non-streaming lorem response).

**Body cap**
- Create a listener with `record_request_body_cap_bytes = 10`.
- Send a request with a body longer than 10 bytes.
- Assert `request.body_truncated_bytes > 0` and `len(request.body) == 10`.

**Streaming**
- Create a listener with a lorem or faker backend that serves SSE.
- Send a streaming request.
- Assert `response.stream != null` and `response.stream.events` is non-empty.
- Assert `response.body == ""`.

**Reset**
- Create a listener, send two requests.
- `DELETE /_zolem/listeners/<name>/calls` → assert `{"cleared": 2}`.
- `GET /_zolem/listeners/<name>/calls` → assert `calls == []`.
- Send one more request; assert `call_id == 1` (reset to 1).

**Idempotent clear**
- `DELETE /_zolem/listeners/<name>/calls` on empty history → `{"cleared": 0}`.

**Listener deletion drops history**
- Create a listener, send a request, delete the listener, re-create the listener
  with the same name. Assert `GET /calls` returns an empty list.

**History preserved across upsert**
- Create a listener, send a request, upsert the listener (same name, different
  profile). Assert `GET /calls` returns the pre-upsert call.

**Concurrent requests**
- Fire 10 requests in parallel using goroutines.
- Assert `len(calls) == 10` and all `call_id` values are distinct.

**404 on nonexistent listener**
- `GET /_zolem/listeners/no-such/calls` → assert 404.
- `DELETE /_zolem/listeners/no-such/calls` → assert 404.

### Fixed-listener (filesystem JSONL) tests in `cmd/zolem/startup_local_test.go`
or `cmd/zolem/local_calls_e2e_test.go`

**Basic JSONL recording**
- Start zolem in fixed-listener mode with `-local-calls-file <tmp>`.
- Send two requests.
- Read the JSONL file; assert two valid JSON objects, both with correct fields.

**Body cap in JSONL**
- Start with `-local-record-request-body-cap-bytes 10`.
- Send a request with a longer body.
- Assert `body_truncated_bytes > 0` in the JSONL record.

**File append semantics**
- Send two requests, verify two lines.
- Send two more requests without restarting zolem, verify four lines total.

**No file without flag**
- Start without `-local-calls-file`.
- Assert the file is not created (use a deterministic path that would not exist).

### zolemc integration tests in `cmd/zolemc/main_e2e_test.go`

**`listeners calls list` human output**
- Start admin server, create listener, send one request.
- Run `zolemc listeners calls list <name>`.
- Assert stdout contains the call ID, method, path, and status.

**`listeners calls list -json`**
- Assert stdout is valid JSON with a `calls` array.

**`listeners calls list -since`**
- Send three requests, run `zolemc listeners calls list <name> -since 2`.
- Assert only calls with `call_id > 2` are returned.

**`listeners calls clear`**
- Send two requests, run `zolemc listeners calls clear <name>`.
- Assert output contains "Cleared 2 calls".
- Run `zolemc listeners calls list <name>`, assert empty list.

**`listeners calls clear -json`**
- Assert output is `{"cleared": N}`.

**Nonexistent listener**
- Run against a nonexistent listener name, assert non-zero exit and a
  `zolem:` prefixed error message.

## Acceptance checks

- [ ] All listed admin-server test cases pass.
- [ ] All listed JSONL test cases pass.
- [ ] All listed zolemc integration test cases pass.
- [ ] `go test ./cmd/zolem/ ./cmd/zolemc/ -race` passes with no data races.
- [ ] No existing tests broken.

## Out of scope

- Any production code changes. This issue adds tests only.

## Resolved lookups (from review)

**Streaming backend**: Use the `lorem` backend with `"stream": true` in the
OpenAI request body. Confirmed pattern from `local_runtime_e2e_test.go:327-337`:
```go
doRequest(t, listenerBaseURL, http.MethodPost, "/v1/chat/completions",
    `{"model":"gpt-4o","stream":true,"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"hello"}]}`,
    "Content-Type: application/json", "Authorization: Bearer sk-test")
```
The lorem backend produces at least 30 delta events plus framing events
(`assertOpenAIStreamShape(t, body, 30)`).

**Helper signatures** for `cmd/zolem/integration_helpers_test.go`:
```go
// getCalls fetches the full call list for a listener via the admin API.
func getCalls(t *testing.T, adminBaseURL, listenerName string) []RecordedCall

// clearCalls clears the call history for a listener and returns the cleared count.
func clearCalls(t *testing.T, adminBaseURL, listenerName string) int
```
Both call `t.Fatal` on unexpected HTTP status.

**Flag names**: Use the names as defined in issues 1–4. If this issue is
started before the others land, read the implementation to get the exact names
rather than assuming from the design spec.

**Schema reference**: Field names in test assertions must match
`docs/specs/2026-05-22-call-history-design.md`. If that spec drifts from the
implementation, trust the implementation and update the spec as part of this
issue.

title: Fixed-listener JSONL call recording (--local-calls-file)
description:
# Fixed-listener JSONL call recording (--local-calls-file)

## Summary

Add a `--local-calls-file <path>` flag to fixed-listener mode that writes each
completed call record as a newline-delimited JSON line to the named file.

## Context

Design spec: `docs/specs/2026-05-22-call-history-design.md`

**Depends on issue 1** (Recorder interface, inMemoryRecorder, capture
middleware). This issue adds a second `Recorder` implementation
(`jsonlRecorder`) and wires it into the fixed-listener startup path.

The admin HTTP endpoints (issue 2) and zolemc (issue 3) are not relevant here
— fixed-listener mode has no admin server. Tests read the file directly.

## Current code facts

- `cmd/zolem/main.go:10-16` — flag definitions for fixed-listener mode:
  `local-addr`, `local-provider`, `local-profile`, `local-backend`,
  `local-fixtures-dir`, `local-tls-cert`, `local-tls-key`. A new flag
  `local-calls-file` is added here.
- `cmd/zolem/startup.go:63` — `localOptions` struct currently has `Addr`,
  `Provider`, `Profile`, `Backend`, `FixturesDir`, `TLS`. A `CallsFile string`
  field is added.
- `cmd/zolem/startup.go:104` — `runLocal(opts localOptions, deps startupDeps)`
  calls `buildLocalStartupApp`. If `opts.CallsFile != ""`, create a
  `jsonlRecorder` and pass it to `buildLocalStartupApp`/
  `buildLocalStartupAppForRuntime`; otherwise pass a `noopRecorder`.
- `cmd/zolem/startup.go:133` — `buildLocalStartupApp` calls
  `buildLocalStartupAppForRuntime`. After issue 1, this function accepts a
  recorder and caps and wraps the handler with the recording middleware.
- `cmd/zolem/main.go:10` — existing cap flags (analogous to the listener-create
  body cap fields in issue 2) are added as fixed-listener flags:
  `local-record-request-body-cap-bytes` (default 262144),
  `local-record-response-body-cap-bytes` (default 262144),
  `local-record-stream-event-cap` (default 1024).

## Work

### 1. `jsonlRecorder`

Create a `jsonlRecorder` in `cmd/zolem/recording.go` (same file as the
interface and `inMemoryRecorder`):

```go
type jsonlRecorder struct {
    mu   sync.Mutex
    file *os.File
    next atomic.Int64
}

func newJSONLRecorder(path string) (*jsonlRecorder, error)
```

- Opens `path` with `os.OpenFile(path, os.O_APPEND|os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)`.
- `Record(call)`: marshals the call to JSON, appends `<json>\n` to the file
  under the mutex, and syncs (or at minimum flushes). Uses the same
  `RecordedCall` type as `inMemoryRecorder`.
- `List()` — returns nil (not supported in fixed-listener mode; the file is read
  directly by tests).
- `Clear()` — returns 0 (not supported; tests truncate the file themselves).
- `Close()` — closes the file.

`call_id` assignment: same `atomic.Int64` pattern as `inMemoryRecorder`.

### 2. `noopRecorder`

A zero-allocation `noopRecorder` implementing `Recorder` with no-op methods.
Used when `--local-calls-file` is absent so the middleware path is uniform.

```go
type noopRecorder struct{}
func (noopRecorder) Record(RecordedCall) {}
func (noopRecorder) List() []RecordedCall { return nil }
func (noopRecorder) Clear() int           { return 0 }
func (noopRecorder) Close()               {}
```

### 3. Flags

In `cmd/zolem/main.go`, add:
```
-local-calls-file string       (default ""; empty = no recording)
-local-record-request-body-cap-bytes int   (default 262144)
-local-record-response-body-cap-bytes int  (default 262144)
-local-record-stream-event-cap int         (default 1024)
```

Populate `localOptions.CallsFile` and pass cap values through.

### 4. Wiring in `runLocal`

In `runLocal`, after building the app, if `opts.CallsFile != ""`:
1. Call `newJSONLRecorder(opts.CallsFile)` and defer `recorder.Close()`.
2. Wrap `app.handler` with `recordingMiddleware(recorder, caps)`.

If `opts.CallsFile == ""`, wrap with `recordingMiddleware(noopRecorder{}, caps)`
(or skip the middleware wrap entirely as an optimization — either is acceptable).

### 5. Help text

Update `cmd/zolem/main.go` (the flag description strings, not a usage block) so
`-local-calls-file` has a clear description.

Also update `README.md` (or `docs/local-runtime.md`) to document the new flag,
consistent with the pattern used for other fixed-listener flags.

## Acceptance checks

- [ ] Starting zolem with `-local-calls-file /tmp/calls.jsonl` creates the file
      and appends one JSON line per completed request.
- [ ] Each line is a complete, valid JSON object matching the `RecordedCall`
      schema from the design spec.
- [ ] Body caps are respected (bodies truncated beyond cap with non-zero
      `body_truncated_bytes`).
- [ ] Streaming calls: `response.body` is empty; `response.stream.events` is
      populated.
- [ ] Second batch of requests appends to the existing file (not overwritten).
- [ ] Without `-local-calls-file`, no file is created and no recording overhead
      is observable.
- [ ] `noopRecorder` implements `Recorder`.
- [ ] No existing fixed-listener tests broken.

## Out of scope

- Admin HTTP call-history endpoints (those only exist in local runtime mode).
- zolemc calls subcommands.
- E2E tests (issue 5).

## Resolved lookups (from review)

**Sync vs flush**: Commit to `file.Sync()` after appending each record. This
ensures the file is readable by a test immediately after the HTTP response
is received, without timing races.

**Cap fields on `localOptions`**: Add these fields to the `localOptions` struct
(`cmd/zolem/startup.go:63`):
```go
RecordRequestBodyCapBytes  int
RecordResponseBodyCapBytes int
RecordStreamEventCap       int
```
Populated in `main.go` from the corresponding flags. Zero values in
`buildLocalStartupApp` should fall back to `DefaultRecordCaps()` (same as
local runtime mode).

**Documentation file**: Update `docs/local-runtime.md` (not README.md) to
document the four new flags: `-local-calls-file`, `-local-record-request-body-cap-bytes`,
`-local-record-response-body-cap-bytes`, `-local-record-stream-event-cap`. Place
the documentation near the existing `-local-fixtures-dir` flag description
(around line 266).

**`call_id` pattern**: Same as `inMemoryRecorder`: an `atomic.Int64` field;
`NextCallID()` calls `r.next.Add(1)` and returns the new value.

Add these acceptance checks:
- [ ] `recorder.Close()` is deferred after creation in `runLocal`; the file is
      closed cleanly on process shutdown.
- [ ] Concurrent requests written to the same JSONL file produce valid,
      non-interleaved JSON lines (each line is a complete record).

title: zolemc: listeners calls list/clear subcommands
description:
# zolemc: listeners calls list/clear subcommands

## Summary

Add `zolemc listeners calls list <name>` and `zolemc listeners calls clear <name>`
as subcommands under the existing `listeners` group.

## Context

Design spec: `docs/specs/2026-05-22-call-history-design.md`

**Depends on issue 2** (admin HTTP endpoints) being merged first. This issue is
a thin client over those endpoints.

## Current code facts

- `cmd/zolemc/main.go:254` — `runListeners` dispatches on `args[0]` ("list",
  "create", "delete"). `"calls"` is a new subcommand dispatched here.
- `cmd/zolemc/main.go:154` — `runProfiles` is the analogous function for
  profiles; its structure is the pattern to follow.
- `cmd/zolemc/main.go:101` — `run` dispatches top-level subcommands including
  `"listeners"` → `runListeners`.
- `rootOptions` struct (line ~23) has `adminURL string`, `baseURL string`,
  `json bool`, `timeout time.Duration`. The `-json` flag is already present on
  the root flag set.
- `adminClient` (line ~42) exposes `getJSON`, `delete`, and other HTTP helpers;
  all admin calls go through it.
- `cmd/zolemc/main.go:568` — the usage/help string at the bottom lists existing
  commands. Update it to include the two new subcommands.

## Work

### 1. Dispatch in `runListeners`

In `runListeners`, when `args[0] == "calls"`, call `runListenerCalls(ctx,
client, opts, args[1:], stdout, stderr)`.

### 2. `runListenerCalls`

```go
func runListenerCalls(ctx context.Context, client adminClient, opts rootOptions,
    args []string, stdout, stderr io.Writer) error
```

Dispatches on `args[0]`:
- `"list"` → `runListenerCallsList`
- `"clear"` → `runListenerCallsClear`
- else → error "calls requires list or clear"

### 3. `runListenerCallsList`

Parses flags: `since int` (default 0; client-side filter, `call_id > since`).
Requires exactly one positional arg (listener name). Calls
`GET /_zolem/listeners/<name>/calls`.

**Default (human) output** — compact table with columns:
```
ID   METHOD  PATH                       STATUS  LATENCY_MS  RECEIVED_AT
  1  POST    /v1/chat/completions          200          12  2026-05-22T16:34:12Z
  2  POST    /v1/chat/completions          200           8  2026-05-22T16:34:14Z
```
Streaming calls show `~200` in the status column.

**`-json`** — writes the raw `{"calls": [...]}` response body verbatim (or the
filtered subset if `--since` was provided).

**`-since <id>`** — after fetching the full list, filter to `call_id > id`
before formatting or printing. Applied on the client; the HTTP API has no
server-side filter parameter.

### 4. `runListenerCallsClear`

Requires exactly one positional arg (listener name). Calls
`DELETE /_zolem/listeners/<name>/calls`.

Default output: `Cleared N calls from listener <name>.`
`-json`: prints `{"cleared": N}`.

### 5. Help string

Update the usage block at `cmd/zolemc/main.go:568` to include:
```
listeners calls list <name> [-since <id>]
listeners calls clear <name>
```

## Acceptance checks

- [ ] `zolemc listeners calls list <name>` prints a human-readable table for a
      listener with recorded calls.
- [ ] `zolemc listeners calls list <name> -json` prints the JSON response body.
- [ ] `zolemc listeners calls list <name> -since 3` returns only calls with
      `call_id > 3`.
- [ ] `zolemc listeners calls list <nonexistent>` exits non-zero with a
      `zolem: ...` error message.
- [ ] `zolemc listeners calls clear <name>` prints the cleared-count message.
- [ ] `zolemc listeners calls clear <name> -json` prints `{"cleared": N}`.
- [ ] `zolemc listeners calls clear <nonexistent>` exits non-zero.
- [ ] Existing `listeners list`, `listeners create`, `listeners delete`
      subcommands still work (dispatch is not disrupted).
- [ ] Help text updated.
- [ ] No existing tests broken.

## Out of scope

- Recording middleware (issue 1).
- Admin HTTP endpoints (issue 2).
- Fixed-listener JSONL recording (issue 4).
- E2E tests (issue 5).

## Resolved lookups (from review)

**JSON field names**: The `GET /_zolem/listeners/<name>/calls` response uses
these field names (from `docs/specs/2026-05-22-call-history-design.md`):
- `call_id` (int) — used by the `-since` client-side filter (`call_id > since`)
- `received_at` (RFC3339 string)
- `latency_ms` (int)
- `request.method`, `request.path`, `request.status` not applicable —
  `response.status` (int) is the column
- Streaming calls: `response.stream != null` → prefix displayed status with `~`

**`-since` flag registration site**: Define the flag on the `list` FlagSet,
mirroring the pattern in `runProfiles` / `runListeners create` (where a
`flag.NewFlagSet` is called and parsed before the positional argument check).

**Empty list behavior**: Print `no calls` when the list is empty and the output
is human-format (consistent with "no profiles" and "no listeners" in existing
code). With `-json`, print `{"calls": []}`.

**Listener name omitted**: If no positional argument is given to `list` or
`clear`, return `errors.New("calls list requires exactly one listener name")`
or `"calls clear requires exactly one listener name"` respectively, following
the exact phrasing used in `runListeners` for other subcommands.

Add these two acceptance checks:
- [ ] `zolemc listeners calls list <name>` on an empty history prints `no calls`.
- [ ] `zolemc listeners calls list` (no name) exits non-zero with the expected
      error message.

title: Admin HTTP endpoints for call history (GET + DELETE /calls)
description:
# Admin HTTP endpoints for call history (GET + DELETE /calls)

## Summary

Add `GET /_zolem/listeners/<name>/calls` and `DELETE /_zolem/listeners/<name>/calls`
to the admin server. Also add per-listener body-cap config fields to the
listener-create/upsert payload and the listener view.

## Context

Design spec: `docs/specs/2026-05-22-call-history-design.md`

**Depends on issue 1** (Recorder interface, inMemoryRecorder, recording middleware)
being merged first. This issue adds the HTTP surface that exposes the recorder.

Issue 3 (zolemc) depends on this issue.

## Current code facts

- `cmd/zolem/local_admin.go:275` — `buildLocalAdminHandler` switches on
  `req.URL.Path`. Listener-scoped routes start with `/_zolem/listeners/`.
- `cmd/zolem/local_admin.go:336` — `handleListenerResource` dispatches
  `PUT` (upsert) and `DELETE` on a single listener name. Call-history sub-routes
  (`/_zolem/listeners/<name>/calls`) are a new path segment beyond the name and
  do not match the current `localResourceName` check (which rejects paths with
  additional segments). A separate routing case is needed.
- `cmd/zolem/local_admin.go:39` — `localListenerPayload` (PUT body) currently
  has `Addr`, `Provider`, `Profile`, `TLS`. Three optional cap fields are added.
- `cmd/zolem/local_admin.go:46` — `localListenerView` (response) currently has
  `Name`, `Addr`, `Provider`, `Profile`, `Backend`, `TLS`, `BaseURL`. The three
  cap fields are added so callers can see effective caps.
- `cmd/zolem/local_admin.go:61` — `managedLocalListener` will have a `recorder
  Recorder` field after issue 1 lands.
- `cmd/zolem/local_admin.go:364` — `writeJSON(w, status, v)` is the shared JSON
  response helper.
- `cmd/zolem/local_admin.go:368` — `writeAdminError(w, status, message)` is the
  shared error helper. 404 response shape: `{"error": "<message>"}`.

## Work

### 1. Listener-payload and listener-view cap fields

Add to `localListenerPayload`:
```go
RecordRequestBodyCapBytes  *int `json:"record_request_body_cap_bytes,omitempty"`
RecordResponseBodyCapBytes *int `json:"record_response_body_cap_bytes,omitempty"`
RecordStreamEventCap       *int `json:"record_stream_event_cap,omitempty"`
```

Add matching fields to `localListenerView` (non-pointer, always present) so
callers can see the effective caps.

In `UpsertListener`, build a `RecordCaps` from the payload values, falling back
to `DefaultRecordCaps()` for absent fields. Pass the caps to the recorder
constructor and middleware (established in issue 1). Store effective caps on
`managedLocalListener` so they can be included in the listener view.

### 2. Routing

In `buildLocalAdminHandler`, before the existing `/_zolem/listeners/` prefix
case, add a case that matches paths of the form
`/_zolem/listeners/<name>/calls` (with an optional trailing `/calls` segment).
Route these to a new `handleListenerCalls` function.

A clean way to distinguish: after stripping `/_zolem/listeners/`, check whether
the remainder contains a `/calls` suffix on a single-segment name
(e.g., `strings.HasSuffix` after `localResourceName`-style extraction).

### 3. `handleListenerCalls`

```go
func handleListenerCalls(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request, control *localControlPlane)
```

Extracts `<name>` from the path. Looks up `c.listeners[name]` under the mutex.
Returns 404 with `{"error": "listener not found"}` if absent.

**GET** — returns the recorder's full list:
```json
{ "calls": [ ... ] }
```

**DELETE** — clears the recorder, returns:
```json
{ "cleared": 12 }
```

Other methods: 405 with `Allow: GET, DELETE`.

### 4. `localControlPlane` helpers

Add two methods that the handler calls (keeps the handler thin):
```go
func (c *localControlPlane) ListCalls(name string) ([]RecordedCall, error)
func (c *localControlPlane) ClearCalls(name string) (int, error)
```

Both return `runtimecfg.ErrListenerNotFound` when the name is absent.

## Acceptance checks

- [ ] `GET /_zolem/listeners/<name>/calls` returns `{"calls": [...]}` for an
      existing listener.
- [ ] `GET` returns 404 `{"error": "..."}` for a nonexistent listener.
- [ ] `DELETE /_zolem/listeners/<name>/calls` returns `{"cleared": N}`.
- [ ] `DELETE` on an empty history returns `{"cleared": 0}`.
- [ ] `DELETE` on a nonexistent listener returns 404.
- [ ] Listener upsert with cap fields stores effective caps and returns them in
      the listener view.
- [ ] Listener upsert without cap fields uses `DefaultRecordCaps()` values,
      returned in the view.
- [ ] Existing `PUT` and `DELETE` on `/_zolem/listeners/<name>` still work
      (routing is not disrupted).
- [ ] No existing tests broken.

## Out of scope

- zolemc CLI commands (issue 3).
- Fixed-listener JSONL recording (issue 4).
- E2E tests (issue 5).
- The recording middleware itself (issue 1).

## Resolved lookups (from review)

**`runtimecfg.ErrListenerNotFound`**: Confirmed at `internal/runtime/store.go:19`.
The `localControlPlane.ListCalls` and `ClearCalls` methods return this error
when the listener name is absent; the handler maps it to 404.

**Routing insertion point**: In `buildLocalAdminHandler`
(`cmd/zolem/local_admin.go:275`), add a new case **before** the existing
`strings.HasPrefix(req.URL.Path, "/_zolem/listeners/")` case:

```go
case strings.HasPrefix(req.URL.Path, "/_zolem/listeners/") &&
     strings.HasSuffix(req.URL.Path, "/calls"):
    handleListenerCalls(w, req, control)
    return
```

Extract the listener name by stripping both the `/_zolem/listeners/` prefix and
the `/calls` suffix. Reject names that contain slashes after stripping (same
safety check as `localResourceName`).

**Invalid cap values**: `UpsertListener` should return a 400 error if any cap
field is provided with a value ≤ 0. Add an explicit acceptance check:

- [ ] Listener upsert with `record_request_body_cap_bytes: 0` returns 400.

title: Recorder interface, in-memory buffer, and capture middleware
description:
# Recorder interface, in-memory buffer, and capture middleware

## Summary

Add a `Recorder` interface and an `inMemoryRecorder` implementation that record
every request/response a local listener serves. Wire the recorder as capture
middleware into the handler stack.

## Context

Design spec: `docs/specs/2026-05-22-call-history-design.md`

This is the foundational layer that all other call-history issues depend on.
Issues 2 (admin HTTP API), 4 (JSONL recorder), and 5 (E2E tests) build on top
of this one.

## Current code facts

- `cmd/zolem/local_admin.go:61` — `managedLocalListener` struct currently has
  `runtime`, `app`, `server`, `baseURL` fields. A `recorder Recorder` field
  is added here.
- `cmd/zolem/startup.go:52` — `startupApp` struct holds `handler http.Handler`
  and `close func()`.
- `cmd/zolem/startup.go:144` — `buildLocalStartupAppForRuntime(listenerRuntime,
  fixturesDir, counters, deps)` builds `handler` by calling `buildLocalHandler`
  (line 253). This is where the recording middleware is injected.
- `cmd/zolem/startup.go:253` — `buildLocalHandler(listenerRuntime, counters,
  validator, matcher, generator, wasmGenerator) http.Handler` returns an
  `http.HandlerFunc`. The recording middleware wraps the result of this call.
- `cmd/zolem/local_admin.go:171` — `UpsertListener` calls
  `buildLocalStartupAppForRuntime` and stores the result in `c.listeners[name]`.
  The recorder must be created here and stored on `managedLocalListener`.

## Work

### 1. `Recorder` interface

Create a new file `cmd/zolem/recording.go` defining:

```go
type Recorder interface {
    Record(call RecordedCall)
    List() []RecordedCall
    Clear() int   // returns count cleared; resets call_id sequence to 1
    Close()
}
```

### 2. `RecordedCall` type and supporting types

Define the full schema types in the same file:

- `RecordedCall` — top-level record (`CallID`, `Listener`, `ReceivedAt`,
  `CompletedAt`, `LatencyMS`, `Request`, `Response`)
- `RecordedRequest` — `Method`, `Path`, `Query`, `Headers`, `RemoteAddr`,
  `Body`, `BodyTruncatedBytes`
- `RecordedResponse` — `Status`, `Headers`, `Body`, `BodyTruncatedBytes`,
  `Stream *StreamRecord`
- `StreamRecord` — `EventCount int`, `Events []StreamEvent`,
  `EventsTruncated int`
- `StreamEvent` — `ReceivedAt`, `Event`, `Data`

Header maps are `map[string][]string`. Bodies are `string`; if a body is not
valid UTF-8, encode to base64 and set `BodyBase64` instead (omit `Body`).
JSON field names follow the spec (`call_id`, `received_at`, etc.).

### 3. `RecordCaps` struct

```go
type RecordCaps struct {
    RequestBodyCapBytes  int
    ResponseBodyCapBytes int
    StreamEventCap       int
}

func DefaultRecordCaps() RecordCaps {
    return RecordCaps{
        RequestBodyCapBytes:  262144,
        ResponseBodyCapBytes: 262144,
        StreamEventCap:       1024,
    }
}
```

### 4. `inMemoryRecorder`

```go
type inMemoryRecorder struct {
    mu      sync.Mutex
    calls   []RecordedCall
    nextID  atomic.Int64
}
```

- `Record` appends to `calls` under the mutex.
- `List` returns a copy of `calls` under the mutex (defensive copy to avoid
  race with concurrent appends).
- `Clear` drains `calls`, resets `nextID` to 1, returns old count.
- `Close` is a no-op.
- `call_id` values come from `nextID.Add(1)` invoked at request arrival (before
  handler runs), so IDs reflect arrival order under concurrency.

### 5. `recordingMiddleware`

```go
func recordingMiddleware(recorder Recorder, caps RecordCaps) func(http.Handler) http.Handler
```

The middleware:
1. Stamps `received_at`, calls `recorder.nextID.Add(1)` to assign `call_id`.
2. Reads and buffers the request body up to `caps.RequestBodyCapBytes`;
   records `body_truncated_bytes` if the body was longer.
3. Replaces `req.Body` with a reader over the buffered bytes so the underlying
   handler still sees the complete body.
4. Wraps `w` in `recordingResponseWriter` (see below).
5. Calls the next handler.
6. After the handler returns, stamps `completed_at`, builds the `RecordedCall`,
   calls `recorder.Record(call)`.

`recordingResponseWriter` wraps `http.ResponseWriter` and additionally
implements `http.Flusher` when the underlying writer supports it:
- `WriteHeader(status)` — captures status.
- `Write(b)` — copies bytes into the response body buffer (up to cap); if the
  response is SSE (detected via `Content-Type: text/event-stream`), additionally
  parses the chunk as SSE frames and appends to the in-progress event list.
- `Flush()` — delegates to the underlying flusher, records `received_at` on
  each event frame.

SSE frame parsing: split on `\n\n` delimiters; within each frame, `event:` and
`data:` lines are extracted. Malformed chunks are silently skipped (a partial
frame at a flush boundary is held in a running parse buffer and completed on the
next `Write`).

### 6. Wiring

In `UpsertListener` (`cmd/zolem/local_admin.go:171`): create a new
`inMemoryRecorder` and store it on `managedLocalListener.recorder`.

In `buildLocalStartupAppForRuntime` (`cmd/zolem/startup.go:144`): accept the
recorder as an additional parameter. After calling `buildLocalHandler`, wrap the
returned handler with `recordingMiddleware(recorder, caps)`.

Alternatively, pass `recorder` and `caps` from `UpsertListener` down through
`buildLocalStartupAppForRuntime`. Either approach is acceptable as long as the
recorder is stored on `managedLocalListener` and is accessible to the admin HTTP
endpoints in issue 2.

When a listener is deleted (`DeleteListener`, `local_admin.go:289`), call
`recorder.Close()`.

## Acceptance checks

- [ ] `Recorder` interface defined; `inMemoryRecorder` implements it.
- [ ] `RecordedCall` and supporting types match the schema in the design spec.
- [ ] `recordingMiddleware` buffers request body and restores it for the handler.
- [ ] Response body is captured (non-streaming).
- [ ] SSE responses: `response.body` is empty; `response.stream.events` contains
      parsed events.
- [ ] Body cap: bodies beyond the cap are truncated; `body_truncated_bytes` is
      non-zero.
- [ ] `call_id` assigned at arrival; concurrent requests get distinct IDs in
      arrival order.
- [ ] Listener deletion calls `recorder.Close()`.
- [ ] No existing tests broken.

## Out of scope

- Admin HTTP endpoints that expose the recorder over HTTP (issue 2).
- zolemc CLI commands (issue 3).
- Fixed-listener JSONL backend (issue 4).
- E2E tests (issue 5).

## Resolved lookups (from review)

**ID assignment**: Add `NextCallID() int64` to the `Recorder` interface. The
capture middleware calls `recorder.NextCallID()` at request arrival (before
delegating to the handler) and stores the result in a local variable. The
`RecordedCall` is built with that ID at completion and passed to
`recorder.Record(call)`. This preserves arrival-order IDs under concurrency
without exposing the atomic counter as a field.

`inMemoryRecorder.NextCallID()` calls `r.nextID.Add(1)` and returns the new
value. `jsonlRecorder.NextCallID()` does the same. `noopRecorder.NextCallID()`
returns 0.

**BodyBase64 struct layout**: `RecordedRequest` and `RecordedResponse` each have
two optional sibling fields:
```go
Body             string `json:"body,omitempty"`
BodyBase64       string `json:"body_base64,omitempty"`
BodyTruncatedBytes int  `json:"body_truncated_bytes"`
```
The encoder checks `utf8.Valid(rawBytes)`. If valid, sets `Body`; otherwise sets
`BodyBase64` with `base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString(rawBytes)`. `Body` and
`BodyBase64` are never both non-empty.

**Design spec**: `docs/specs/2026-05-22-call-history-design.md` exists in the
repo. Use it as the schema reference for JSON field names.

title: Add CEL expression matching for fixtures
description:
# Add CEL expression matching for fixtures

## Summary

Fixture matching currently requires a `match.wasm` module for any request-aware selection. Add a lighter-weight CEL-based matcher for common fixture predicates while preserving the existing WASM matcher as the advanced/custom scoring escape hatch.

## Current behavior

- Fixture directories use `meta.yaml` plus `response.json` or `response.json.tmpl`.
- `provider` and `version` in `meta.yaml` are cheap prefilters.
- Request-aware matching is only implemented through `match.wasm`.
- A fixture without `match.wasm` loads but never matches; startup warns that it has no matcher.
- The existing matcher selects the highest non-negative score and tie-breaks by fixture order.
- The project design docs mention future CEL support compiled to WASM, but no CEL support exists today.

## Desired behavior

Support CEL expressions directly in `meta.yaml` as an embedded, in-process matcher using `google/cel-go`; do not build a CEL-to-WASM compiler for the initial implementation.

Example fixture:

```yaml
id: openai-refund
provider: openai
version: v1
status: 200

match:
  cel: |
    body["model"] == "gpt-4o-mini" &&
    body["messages"].exists(m,
      m["role"] == "user" &&
      string(m["content"]).contains("refund")
    )
  score: 20
```

Proposed semantics:

- `match.cel` is a CEL expression that must evaluate to `bool`.
- `match.score` is optional and defaults to `1`.
- `true` means the fixture is a candidate with `match.score`.
- `false` means no match.
- Highest score still wins.
- Ties still use the existing fixture order behavior.
- `match.wasm` remains supported and keeps returning `float32` scores as today.
- A fixture may define CEL matching or WASM matching, but not both, for the first implementation.
- A fixture with neither CEL nor WASM still loads and produces the existing "will never match" warning.

CEL activation environment:

```text
provider string
version  string
labels   map(string, string)
body     dyn / JSON map
```

Document bracket access as the stable style:

```cel
body["model"] == "claude-sonnet-4-5"
labels["tenant"] == "acme"
```

Avoid promising `body.model` syntax unless implementation tests prove it works consistently for the chosen `cel-go` JSON activation shape.

## Suggested implementation

1. Add a matcher abstraction under `internal/fixture` so `Matcher.Match` no longer hard-codes WASM scoring.
2. Extend `Fixture` to hold a compiled match program, for example a `CompiledMatch` with kind `wasm` or `cel`, score metadata, and the existing compiled WASM module when applicable.
3. Extend `meta.yaml` parsing with a nested `match` block:

   ```yaml
   match:
     cel: <expression>
     score: <non-negative number>
   ```

4. Compile/check CEL expressions at fixture load/hot-reload time using `google/cel-go`.
5. Validate that CEL expressions type-check and return `bool`.
6. Validate that `match.score` is finite and non-negative.
7. Reject fixtures that define both `match.cel` and `match.wasm`/`match.wasm` file semantics for now, with a clear error.
8. Preserve current WASM behavior and scoring for existing fixture directories.
9. Treat CEL runtime evaluation errors consistently with WASM scoring errors during matching: skip the failing fixture and continue to other candidates. If this behavior needs operator visibility, add a follow-up counter/log note rather than blocking this issue.

## Documentation updates

Update `docs/local-runtime.md` fixture docs to explain:

- CEL is the recommended common-case matcher.
- WASM is still available for custom scoring or complex logic.
- The available CEL variables.
- The bool-plus-score selection model.
- The either-CEL-or-WASM restriction.
- Examples for matching OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini request bodies if practical.

If CLI help or fixture-authoring text mentions required `match.wasm`, update it in the same change so docs no longer imply WASM is the only path.

## Acceptance criteria

- A fixture with `match.cel: "true"` and no `match.wasm` can match and serve its response.
- A fixture with `match.cel: "false"` does not match.
- CEL fixtures can match on `provider`, `version`, `labels`, and nested request body fields through bracket access.
- `match.score` participates in the existing highest-score selection behavior.
- Tie-breaking remains first fixture in load order.
- Existing WASM fixture tests still pass unchanged.
- A fixture defining both CEL and WASM matching is rejected with a clear load-time error.
- Invalid CEL syntax, non-bool CEL results, and invalid scores fail fixture load with useful fixture-specific errors.
- Existing no-matcher fixtures still warn and never match.
- End-to-end fixture backend coverage includes at least one CEL-backed fixture served through the local runtime path.

## Out of scope

- CEL-to-WASM compilation.
- A web/online CEL editor.
- Arbitrary custom CEL functions beyond a minimal safe standard environment.
- Changing response templating behavior.
- Reading request body values from response templates.
- Changing the existing WASM ABI or score semantics.

## Size and risk

This is a medium feature. It touches fixture loading, matching, startup/hot-reload behavior, docs, and e2e coverage. The main risks are CEL dynamic JSON typing, unclear precedence between matcher types, and preserving existing WASM fixture behavior.

title: Fix local runtime documentation drift
description:
README.md and docs/local-runtime.md have drifted from the current local runtime surface.

Observed evidence:
- README.md lists lorem, faker, fixture, ollama, and error response modes but omits the supported wasm backend from the response-mode table while later mentioning WASM profile creation.
- README.md links to docs/local-runtime.md through stale absolute paths under /home/ketan/.codex/memories/worktrees/... instead of the repo-local docs/local-runtime.md.
- docs/local-runtime.md has a similar stale absolute link to scripts/generate-certs.sh.
- README.md says scripts/generate-certs.sh prints a YAML config snippet, but the current runtime docs and CLI expose TLS through -local-tls-cert and -local-tls-key; the YAML snippet appears to describe removed static config mode.
- README.md does not point readers from the short WASM generator example to the full WASM generator ABI and constraints documented in docs/local-runtime.md.

Scope:
- Update documentation only.
- Keep the README concise and link to the fuller WASM generator documentation instead of duplicating the ABI.
- Replace stale absolute worktree links with repo-relative links.
- Remove or rewrite obsolete static-config YAML wording.

Out of scope:
- No CLI, runtime, fixture, provider, or WASM generator behavior changes.
- No new examples for unsupported local providers.

Acceptance checks:
- README.md response modes include wasm.
- README.md WASM section links to the full WASM backend/generator docs.
- README.md and docs/local-runtime.md do not link to /home/ketan/.codex/memories/worktrees/....
- TLS certificate docs no longer imply a YAML config path for the current local runtime workflow.

title: Integrate refute for zolem agents
description:
Refute should be integrated into zolem so agents working in this repository can discover and use it consistently.

Context:
- Local checkout: ~/project/refute
- Upstream: https://github.com/shatterproof-ai/refute
- Goal: make refute available to agents working in zolem without each session needing to rediscover setup details.

Scope:
- Decide the repo-local integration point for exposing refute to agents, such as documented agent guidance, scripts, plugin/skill wiring, or another existing zolem convention.
- Add the minimal setup/docs needed for agents to invoke or reference refute reliably from zolem.
- Document any prerequisites, expected local path assumptions, and fallback behavior if ~/project/refute is absent.
- Include verification that an agent/session can find and use the integration path from a clean zolem checkout or linked worktree.

Out of scope:
- Changing refute itself unless a separate issue is filed in the refute repository.
