title: E2E coverage: pin SSE event counts and chunker invariants across all streaming backends
description:
## Background

The three streaming E2E tests landed in 9213c33 / 4a801d0 / dea2e71 verify SSE *shape* (headers, joined content, [DONE] terminator) but do not pin event count. Since zolem's value proposition includes precise SSE simulation, regression safety against chunker drift requires explicit count or invariant-based assertions.

## Scope

Add SSE event-count coverage to every streaming-capable backend exercised through the cross-process admin → listener path:

- **lorem** — internal chunker; assert invariant (e.g. one delta event per generated token, plus exactly one [DONE]).
- **faker** — internal chunker; same invariant style.
- **fixture** — fixture playback engine; invariant tied to the rendered template (e.g. event count == word count, or fixture-engine-defined unit).
- **ollama** — translator; assert exact 1:1 pass-through from upstream chunks to client events. The httptest stub controls upstream count, so the client side should match exactly (modulo translator-defined headers/footers; pin those).
- **error** backend streaming path — if it produces SSE, pin the exact event count for each provider's documented error-shape.
- **wasm** backend — if the generator emits multi-chunk streams, pin invariant tied to generator output.

For each backend: also assert exactly one terminating `data: [DONE]` event and zero events after it.

## Approach

- Use invariants where the producer is internal (count derives from input shape) so cosmetic chunker changes don't break tests.
- Use exact counts only where the producer is external/test-controlled (ollama httptest stub).
- Co-locate with existing tests in cmd/zolem/local_runtime_e2e_test.go and cmd/zolem/tls_listener_e2e_test.go. Helper for "parse SSE body, return (data_events, saw_done)" should live alongside existing sseRecords/sseDataPayload helpers.

## Acceptance

- For each SSE-capable backend listed above, a streaming subtest asserts: data-event count (invariant or exact per the rules above), exactly one [DONE], and no trailing events after [DONE].
- `go test ./cmd/zolem/...` passes.
- A regression that collapses any backend's stream into one event, drops [DONE], or emits events after [DONE] is caught by the new assertions.

## Out of scope

- Inter-event timing / stream_delay assertions (file separately if wanted).
- New backends or new SSE features.

## References

- cmd/zolem/tls_listener_e2e_test.go (TestLocalRuntimeTLSListener_E2E/streaming)
- cmd/zolem/local_runtime_e2e_test.go (TestLocalRuntimeLocalBackends_E2E/{fixture/sse, ollama/streaming}; TestLocalRuntimeErrorBackend_E2E; TestLocalRuntimeWASMBackend_E2E)
- internal SSE chunkers (per-backend; check internal/runtime/* and internal/backends/*)
close_reason: SSE event-count assertions landed in merge d9c5ef1; covers lorem/faker/fixture/ollama/wasm streaming; error backend excluded (no SSE path)

title: Route shatter scan through docker sandbox
description:
# Route shatter scan through docker sandbox

Shatter v2 (str-jeen.56) added an OS-level sandbox runner to its Go frontend. The sandbox is configured purely via env vars on the shatter binary — no zolem code changes needed beyond the invocation script.

## Acceptance criteria

- `scripts/shatter-full-scan.sh` invokes `${SHATTER_BIN} scan` with `SHATTER_SANDBOX_BACKEND=docker` set in the environment.
- `SHATTER_SANDBOX_DOCKER_IMAGE` is set to an image that contains whatever the zolem harness needs at runtime (Go toolchain if any target compiles, CA certs if needed). Default `debian:bookworm-slim` is glibc-only.
- Docker is documented as a runtime prerequisite.
- A post-run check confirms target writes do not land in the zolem repo or host `/tmp`.

## Implementation notes

- Single invocation site: `scripts/shatter-full-scan.sh` line ~19. Easiest to `export` the env vars near the top, after the SHATTER_BIN check.
- Docker backend defaults from shatter-go: `--network=none`, read-only rootfs, dropped capabilities, `no-new-privileges`, tmpfs for `/tmp` and home, bind mounts for the scratch project copy and staged launcher.
- Optional: `SHATTER_SANDBOX_DOCKER_RUNTIME=runsc` for gVisor isolation if installed.
- Caveats:
  - No network in the container — any zolem target that does network I/O will fail. Surface and triage these explicitly.
  - shatter does not yet *require* a sandbox for `local_fs` targets; if the env var is dropped, runs silently fall back to host writes. Consider a defensive check that aborts if `SHATTER_SANDBOX_BACKEND` is unset.
  - Image selection matters — pick something with the runtime deps the harness needs and pin the tag.

## Reference

- shatter-go/sandbox/runner.go — runner implementation
- shatter-go/CLAUDE.md "OS sandbox runner (str-jeen.56)" — env var contract

title: Add WASM content generator backend
description: Add a local runtime backend that lets users provide base64-encoded binary WASM in a profile. The WASM module generates assistant content only, returning UTF-8 JSON bytes that decode to []string from the same request context used by match.wasm: provider, version, labels, and body. Zolem remains responsible for provider-native OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini envelopes, usage fields, finish reasons, and SSE serialization. Non-streaming responses join the returned strings into one assistant message. Streaming responses emit one returned string at a time as provider-native content deltas with profile-controlled pacing, including empty strings.
design: Use wazero v1.11.0 with WebAssembly Core 2.0-compatible binary modules. Only base64 binary WASM is supported initially, not WAT. WASI and all other imports are rejected in v1. Profile shape includes backend=wasm, wasm_module_base64, wasm_generate_timeout_ms, and stream_delay. Validate at profile write time by decoding base64, compiling the module once for the profile, rejecting imports, and verifying the exact required export surface: memory, alloc(len)->ptr, dealloc(ptr,len), generate(input_ptr,input_len)->handle, result_ptr(handle)->ptr, result_len(handle)->len, result_free(handle). Extra exports, exported globals, extra exported memories, and exported tables are rejected; internal non-exported globals/tables/functions/data segments are allowed. Instantiate a fresh WASM instance for every generation request and close it after success or failure so calls cannot observe prior memory, globals, tables, allocator state, or language runtime state. Enforce a fixed host memory limit of 16 MiB per instance; module-declared max memory is not required. wasm_generate_timeout_ms defaults to 100ms, is profile-configurable with bounds 1ms..5s, and covers the whole interaction from alloc through cleanup. Result validation rejects result_len over 1 MiB, invalid UTF-8, invalid JSON, non-array JSON, and decoded arrays over 4096 chunks. Empty arrays and empty string chunks are valid. stream_delay supports fixed and deterministic random modes. Random pacing uses profile seed plus a profile-local stream ordinal so streams are deterministic but distinct; the ordinal resets on profile update and process restart. Requests must not provide or override seeds. WASM generation failures and guardrail violations return a Zolem internal error response rather than falling back to built-in generators.
acceptance_criteria: A backend=wasm profile can be created with wasm_module_base64 and validated at write time; invalid base64, invalid WASM, unsupported imports, missing required ABI exports, wrong ABI signatures, extra exported API surface, runtime traps, timeout, invalid UTF-8, invalid JSON arrays, result bytes over 1 MiB, and decoded arrays over 4096 chunks produce clear Zolem internal errors; the WASM generator receives the same input JSON shape as match.wasm; Zolem compiles once per profile update and instantiates a fresh instance per generation request; two calls to the same profile cannot observe each other's WASM memory/global/table/allocator/runtime state; host memory is capped at 16 MiB per instance; wasm_generate_timeout_ms is configurable with default 100ms and bounds 1ms..5s; non-streaming OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini responses wrap joined WASM chunks in provider-native JSON; streaming OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini responses emit each returned string as one provider-native content delta, including empty strings; empty arrays produce successful empty assistant responses; fixed stream_delay pacing is honored; random stream_delay pacing is deterministic from profile seed and distinct per profile stream ordinal; stream ordinals reset on profile update and process restart; request payloads cannot set or override pacing seeds; E2E coverage exercises admin profile creation plus listener data-plane calls for non-streaming, streaming, empty output, pacing, timeout, import rejection, and state isolation behavior.

title: Add zolemc CLI API client
description:
Design and implement a companion zolemc binary that acts as an API client for Zolem local runtime. It should wrap the existing local admin API for health, profiles, listeners, listener health/state, and provide a raw provider request helper for calling listener base URLs without hand-written curl commands. Prefer Go stdlib flag.FlagSet for the initial implementation to match the current cmd/zolem style and avoid adding a CLI framework dependency until the command surface grows. Consider adding an internal typed admin client shared by the CLI tests.

## Context
Current admin endpoints are GET /_zolem/health, GET/PUT/DELETE /_zolem/profiles, GET/PUT/DELETE /_zolem/listeners, and listener GET /_zolem/health plus /_zolem/state. Note: RuntimeProfile supports ollama_upstream, but localProfilePayload currently does not accept it, so either fix that API payload first or omit a CLI flag until server support exists.
acceptance_criteria: A cmd/zolemc binary exists; zolemc can health-check the admin API; profiles can be listed, created, and deleted; listeners can be listed, created, and deleted; listener health/state can be queried by base URL; request supports method, path, headers, and JSON body/file input; output supports machine-readable JSON and readable default output; errors include HTTP status and server error bodies; E2E coverage exercises zolemc against a real local admin server and listener; docs/README examples replace or supplement the current curl-only workflow.

title: Stale-doc fixes: design doc historical marker, anthropic-spec-snapshot broken links + phantom test, ERRORS.md stub, generate-certs.sh ghost config, Docker tag, CHANGELOG Unreleased
description:
**Current facts:**
- docs/2026-04-02-llm-mock-service-design.md still says "Status: Approved" while describing virtual-host routing, YAML --config, 6h spec refresh (never shipped) and omitting wasm/error/ollama backends, admin API, zolemc, call recording (shipped). It also lists faker both in the MVP table (line 51) and out of scope (line 309).
- docs/anthropic-spec-snapshot.md links twice into the deleted worktree /tmp/zolem-phase4-discovery-ingestion/... and tells readers to run `go test ./cmd/zolem -run 'TestBuildStartupApp_LoadsVendoredAnthropicSnapshot'` — no such test exists (silently runs zero tests).
- ERRORS.md is a 9-byte stub (`# Errors`), tracked intentionally (commit 40e93b3, durable error-log opt-in) but empty for 5+ weeks. Disposition: default to deleting it; keep only if the maintainer commits to the project-memory practice it was created for.
- scripts/generate-certs.sh tail prints "Add to your zolem config:" with a `server: tls:` YAML snippet for a config system that does not exist; README.md:279-280 describes the intended flag-printing behavior.
- README.md:70/INSTALL.md:76 document `docker pull ghcr.io/ketang/zolem:v0.1.0`; goreleaser publishes `0.1.0` (`.Version` strips the v). docs/local-runtime.md:917-923 omits the `tls` field from the /_zolem/state example; :218-227 omits the record-cap fields the API always returns.
- CHANGELOG.md has no Unreleased section despite ~30 commits since v0.1.0; "Requires Go 1.26+" vs go.mod `go 1.26.1` (cosmetic).
- docs/local-runtime.md:754 says the WASM export surface "must be exactly the required ABI exports" while wasm-modules.md:67-68 and `internal/wasmgen/generator.go:41-42` also accept `__data_end`/`__heap_base` (wasm-modules.md is correct).

**Recommendation:** batch of small doc/script corrections; mark the design doc "historical — superseded by the local runtime architecture" and move under docs/plans/.

**Acceptance checks:** no absolute /tmp links; documented test selectors run >0 tests; generate-certs.sh prints real flags; tags match published artifacts.

---

title: Recording subsystem fixes: SSE multi-line data frames, Clear race, unbounded buffering, info-as-warning header, signal handling
description:
**Current facts:**
- `cmd/zolem/recording.go:528-537` `parseFrame` keeps only the last `data:` line per SSE frame and TrimSpaces it; the SSE spec joins multiple data lines with \n — recorded streams can misrepresent what was sent.
- `inMemoryRecorder.Clear` (recording.go:204-211) resets the atomic ID outside the lock window; an in-flight request during clear can duplicate a call_id.
- Recording middleware (recording.go:312-315) buffers the entire request body before applying the cap.
- `cmd/zolem/startup.go:334` appends `"loaded fixture: %s"` info messages to the warnings slice, so they log as `warn:` and surface in the `X-Zolem-Warnings` response header on admin upserts.
- `cmd/zolem/main.go` has no signal handling; deferred control.Close()/recorder cleanup never run on Ctrl-C (mitigated by per-write fsync, but teardown should be deterministic via signal.NotifyContext + Shutdown).

**Recommendation:** accumulate data lines per SSE frame; reset ID under the lock; cap-while-reading (io.LimitReader); separate info channel from warnings; add signal handling.

**Acceptance checks:** unit tests for multi-line SSE frames and concurrent Clear; a request body larger than the cap is not fully buffered (memory-bounded read verified by test); warnings header contains warnings only; SIGINT runs deferred cleanup.

---

title: Provider fidelity nits: Gemini ?key= auth and finishReason "NONE"; OpenAI unconditional usage chunk; constant Anthropic message id
description:
**Current facts (verified):**
- Gemini: auth accepted only via `x-goog-api-key` header; `?key=` query param (older google-generativeai SDK, most curl examples) gets provider-native 403 API_KEY_INVALID. Streaming chunks carry `"finishReason":"NONE"` — not a valid enum in the real API (real chunks omit the field); strict typed clients may choke.
- OpenAI: the final usage chunk is always sent; the real API sends it only when `stream_options.include_usage` is requested. Harmless to SDKs but a fidelity gap.
- Anthropic: non-stream responses always use id `msg_zolem_generated`; breaks any client deduping by message id (streams already get unique ids).

**Code locators:** Gemini auth + streaming in `internal/provider/gemini/handler.go` (grep `x-goog-api-key`, `finishReason`); OpenAI usage chunk in `internal/provider/openai/handler.go` (grep `stream_options` / usage chunk emission); Anthropic id in `internal/provider/anthropic/handler.go` (grep `msg_zolem_generated`).

**Recommendation:** accept `?key=`; omit finishReason on intermediate Gemini chunks; gate the usage chunk on stream_options; generate unique Anthropic message ids.

**Acceptance checks:** per-item e2e assertions; SDK-compat suite still green.

---
notes: Aggregate go test ./... after landing found stale cross-process expectations for the provider-fidelity change: OpenAI tests still expected unconditional usage chunks and Gemini tests still expected intermediate finishReason NONE. Reopening to update the affected tests before final push.

title: CLI surface polish: zolem mode UX, usage synopsis, -version; zolemc trailing-arg and misc traps
description:
**Current facts:**
- `zolem`: 11 flags all prefixed `-local-*` (nothing non-local exists); mode chosen implicitly by which flag is set; bare invocation dies with `choose either -local-admin-addr or -local-provider` and never prints usage; no `-version` flag; record-cap flags apply only to fixed mode but help does not say so; admin bind success is logged before bind so port conflicts print success-then-failure (`local_admin.go:105-109`); startup logs alarming `warn: failed to fetch spec ... openrouter` lines for providers not in use (root fix in zolem-e9h; suppress or downgrade here).
- `zolemc`: trailing flags silently swallowed on no-arg commands (`zolemc health -json` ignores `-json`); fixture-dir failure surfaces at listener create with a server-flag message (`local fixture backend requires -local-fixtures-dir`) instead of at profile create phrased for the client; `-seed-set` companion flag exists although the same file already uses `flagWasSet()` for other flags (main.go:163 vs 178-179; note seed itself may be deleted by zolem-3j5); output voice drifts (`profile demo created` vs `Cleared 6 calls from listener l1.`); the singular/plural `listener` vs `listeners` two-plane split (-base-url vs -admin-url) is never explained in help.

**In scope (mechanical polish, no design decisions):** real usage synopsis for `zolem` (modes explained, per-mode flag grouping in help text) + `-version` flag; log admin bind success after bind succeeds; downgrade/suppress spec-fetch warnings for providers the listener does not serve (root fix in zolem-e9h); zolemc: reject unexpected trailing args on no-arg commands, validate fixture backend at profile-create time with a client-phrased error, unify output voice, document the two-plane model (-admin-url vs -base-url) in help.

**Out of scope (separate decision, do NOT do here):** restructuring `zolem` into subcommands (`zolem admin` / `zolem serve`, dropping the -local prefix) is a breaking CLI change requiring a maintainer decision — file separately as a design/decision issue if pursued; coordinate with the docs restructure (zolem-qfi).

**Acceptance checks:** bare `zolem` prints a usage synopsis naming both modes (exit non-zero); `zolem -version` prints a version; port-conflict startup prints no success line; `zolemc health -json` errors on the unexpected arg; `profiles create -backend fixture` against a fixtures-less admin fails at profile create with a message phrased for the client user; help text explains which commands need -admin-url vs -base-url.

---

title: A listener named "calls" is unroutable in the admin API
description:
**Current code facts:** `cmd/zolem/local_admin.go:372-374` routes by suffix: `PUT /_zolem/listeners/calls` matches the `/calls` branch and returns 405, so a listener named `calls` can never be created, fetched, or deleted.

**Recommendation:** route on path segments (count + position) rather than suffix matching, or reserve the name `calls` at validation time with a clear error. Segment routing is preferred.

**Acceptance checks:** either `listeners create calls` works end-to-end including its own /calls subresource, or it is rejected at create with an explicit reserved-name error.

---

title: Trim bd context injection: .beads/PRIME.md override + memories-only PreCompact + minimal setup block
description:
bd prime injects ~768 words (~1,220 tokens) of mostly static workflow boilerplate at every Claude Code SessionStart, and again at every PreCompact, in any repo with a beads workspace (the beads plugin auto-runs it; some repos also wire it explicitly in .claude/settings.json). bd ships the fix as a supported customization point (see `bd prime --help`): a `.beads/PRIME.md` file overrides the default output entirely; `bd prime --export` dumps the default for editing; `bd prime --memories-only` emits only persistent memories (~45 words).

Work:
1. `bd prime --export > .beads/PRIME.md`, then trim to ~150-200 words keeping only: the condensed session-close checklist (status/add/commit/push), the six core commands (ready, show, create, update --claim, close, dep add), the no-TodoWrite and `bd remember` rules, and a closing pointer ("run `bd prime --full` for the complete reference").
2. Check this repo's `.claude/settings.json` (and be aware of the user-global one) for explicit bd prime hook wiring; where a PreCompact hook runs `bd prime`, switch it to `bd prime --memories-only` — after compaction the summary carries workflow state, and memories are the only content genuinely at risk.
3. Verify before committing: does a PRIME.md override still append persistent memories to SessionStart output? (The help text says the override replaces output "entirely".) If memories are suppressed, either end PRIME.md with an instruction to run `bd memories`, or add `bd prime --memories-only` as a second SessionStart hook.
4. If this repo's AGENTS.md carries a `<!-- generated by bd setup codex -->` ... `<!-- END BEADS CODEX SETUP -->` block, shrink the content inside the markers to two lines: "This repo uses bd (Beads). Run `bd prime` before tracker work. This block is intentionally minimal — do not re-run `bd setup codex`." Only a manual `bd setup codex` re-run regenerates it; `bd doctor` does not rewrite AGENTS.md content.
5. Commit `.beads/PRIME.md` (and any settings/AGENTS.md changes).

Clarifications: step 0 — confirm `bd prime --help` in this repo mentions the PRIME.md override and the `--export`/`--full`/`--memories-only` flags (all verified present in bd as of 2026-06-12; if this repo's bd predates them, upgrade bd or skip with a note). User-global settings (~/.claude/settings.json) are OUT of scope — modify repo-level wiring only and report any user-global bd prime hooks you notice. The provenance reference (bento-jdg) lives in the bento repo's tracker, not this one.

Savings measured in the originating repo: ~1,000 tokens per session start and ~1,150 per compaction. Origin: 2026-06 bento marketplace review (bento-jdg carries the full analysis and the master copy of this workaround).

Acceptance: `bd prime` in this repo emits the trimmed content (word count <= ~220); PreCompact wiring (if explicitly present) uses --memories-only; memories still reach the session (verified per step 3); any setup block is <= ~40 words.

title: E2E coverage: lorem, faker, fixture, ollama backends through admin+listener
description:
TestLocalRuntimeErrorBackend_E2E and TestLocalRuntimeWASMBackend_E2E only cover backend=error (across openai/anthropic/gemini providers) and backend=wasm (openai only). The remaining backends listed in cmd/zolemc/main.go and the local runtime config — lorem, faker, fixture, ollama — have no cross-process E2E that drives them through admin profile create + listener data plane.

Acceptance per backend (one subtest each is fine, all in cmd/zolem/local_runtime_e2e_test.go):
- lorem: profile create, listener hit, assert non-empty content with provider-specific body schema (openai chat completion at minimum).
- faker: same shape; assert deterministic output if a seed flag is supported, otherwise just schema + non-empty content.
- fixture: profile create with a templated fixture (testdata/), listener hit, assert the rendered fixture content shows up in the response. Cover both non-streaming and SSE.
- ollama: profile create pointing at a local mock HTTP server (httptest.Server inside the test process — do NOT depend on a real ollama daemon), assert request shape sent to the upstream and response schema returned to the client. Streaming case included.

Out of scope: the existing handler-level / unit tests already cover provider dispatch and ollama protocol details; this issue is specifically the cross-process admin -> listener path.

References: cmd/zolem/local_runtime_e2e_test.go, cmd/zolemc/main.go (backend list), docs/local-runtime.md, docs/testing-e2e.md.
notes: Implementation complete on feat/e2e-local-backends. Adds TestLocalRuntimeLocalBackends_E2E with subtests for lorem, faker, fixture (non-streaming + SSE), ollama (non-streaming + streaming via in-process httptest stub). Plumbs ollama_upstream through localProfilePayload. Quality gates: go build ./..., go vet ./..., go test ./cmd/zolem/... — all pass. Awaiting lead landing.
close_reason: E2E coverage for lorem/faker/fixture/ollama backends landed in merge 4a801d0

title: E2E coverage: TLS listener termination
description:
docs/testing-e2e.md lists TLS as in-scope for E2E coverage, but no cross-process test under cmd/zolem exercises a TLS-terminating listener. TLS appears only in unit/handler tests (cmd/zolem/local_admin_test.go, startup_local_test.go), which do not start a real server process or run a real TLS handshake.

Acceptance:
- New E2E in cmd/zolem that starts the binary via 'go run', creates a listener configured for TLS (likely using scripts/generate-certs.sh fixtures or a t.TempDir cert pair), and issues an HTTPS request from the test process.
- Assert: TLS handshake succeeds against the configured cert, status 200, response body schema for at least one provider (openai is fine), and SSE framing on a streaming request.
- Negative case: client with a wrong CA / wrong SNI fails the handshake with a clear error (no 200 leak).
- Reuse generate-certs.sh or generate certs inline; do not depend on external CAs.

References: docs/testing-e2e.md (TLS listed under 'When E2E Is Required'), scripts/generate-certs.sh.
notes: TLS E2E implemented in cmd/zolem/tls_listener_e2e_test.go on branch feat/e2e-tls-listener. Tests pass (go build, go vet, go test ./cmd/zolem/...). Awaiting lead to land + close.
close_reason: E2E TLS listener termination coverage landed in merge 9213c33

title: E2E coverage: wasmgen boundary-export validation through admin API
description:
Add a cross-process E2E in cmd/zolem/local_runtime_e2e_test.go that exercises the wasmgen boundary-export allowance (zolem-qnt, landed 668f2b57) end-to-end. Today the only WASM E2E (TestLocalRuntimeWASMBackend_E2E) uses a hand-built blob with no globals, so the validator's accept-path for __data_end/__heap_base is only covered by package-internal unit tests in internal/wasmgen/validate_test.go.

Acceptance:
- Add a Rust/LLVM-compiled fixture module (or a generator script in scripts/) that emits the required ABI plus __data_end and __heap_base globals; check the binary in under testdata/ or generate it at test time.
- New test loads it via /_zolem/profiles/... with backend=wasm, creates a listener, hits a chat endpoint, asserts content matches.
- Add a negative case: a module with an unsupported extra export (e.g. an arbitrary function 'extra') is rejected by the admin profile create with a 4xx and an error message naming the offending export.
- Keep the test deterministic: no rustc invocation at test time; ship the .wasm fixture or a Go-side builder.

References: internal/wasmgen/validate.go, internal/wasmgen/generator.go (allowedBoundaryExports), cmd/zolem/local_runtime_e2e_test.go.
notes: E2E coverage implemented on feat/e2e-wasmgen-boundary (commit 761f5e7). New TestLocalRuntimeWASMBoundaryExports_E2E in cmd/zolem/local_runtime_e2e_test.go covers both accept (boundary globals) and reject (unsupported 'extra' function export) paths. Quality gates green: build, vet, ./cmd/zolem/... (60 passed), ./internal/wasmgen/... (7 passed). Ready for lead to land — not closing per swarm protocol.
close_reason: E2E wasmgen boundary-export validation landed in merge dea2e71

title: WASM ABI allow linker boundary exports
description: Kapow's nl-golden Zolem oracle is a no_std Rust WASM generator built with rustc for wasm32-unknown-unknown. The module only intentionally exports Zolem's generator ABI functions, but rustc/lld also emits standard linker-defined globals: __data_end and __heap_base. These are common WASM metadata globals that mark the end of static data and the start of heap space; they are not generator entry points. Kapow currently has to rewrite the WASM export section after compilation to remove these two globals before Zolem accepts the module. Zolem should allow these harmless standard boundary exports while still validating the required generator ABI.
acceptance_criteria:
- WASM generator profile validation accepts modules that export __data_end and/or __heap_base in addition to the required Zolem generator ABI exports.
- Validation still requires the generator ABI functions Zolem needs and still rejects missing or incompatible required exports.
- Extra-export handling is narrowly documented or tested so arbitrary unexpected callable exports are not accidentally treated as supported API.
- Add or update an end-to-end/admin-profile test using a Rust/LLVM-style module with __data_end and __heap_base exports.

title: Add -wasm-module-file and -wasm-timeout-ms flags to zolemc profiles create
description:
RuntimeProfile already supports WASMModuleBase64 and WASMGenerateTimeoutMS, and cmd/zolem/startup.go wires them into provider handlers via wasmgen.Compile. The admin REST endpoint PUT /_zolem/profiles/<name> accepts these fields. But cmd/zolemc/main.go's profiles create subcommand exposes only -backend, -backend-model, -error-type, -response-model-policy, -response-model, -fixture-namespace, -seed, -seed-set. There is no flag to set the WASM module or its execution timeout, so callers must bypass zolemc and curl the admin API directly to install a WASM-generator profile.

Acceptance criteria:
- zolemc profiles create accepts -wasm-module-file <path>; the file's bytes are base64-encoded and placed in the WASMModuleBase64 field of the PUT payload.
- zolemc profiles create accepts -wasm-timeout-ms <int>; the value populates WASMGenerateTimeoutMS in the PUT payload (omit when unset to preserve existing default behavior).
- Mutual exclusion is reasonable (e.g., -wasm-module-file requires the resulting profile to be usable by the wasmgen path; passing it without an obvious backend conflict should not silently no-op).
- Unit/e2e coverage for the new flags following the existing zolemc command test patterns.
- README/help text updated to document the new flags.

Caller context: kapow's planned d6rr lane (Zolem NL oracle eval lane) needs to install a WASM-generator profile from a build script. Without these flags it must curl the admin endpoint directly. Adding the flags removes a cross-repo workaround.
notes: Implemented on branch feat/zolem-0bv-wasm-profile-flags and pushed to origin. Verification: go test ./... passed (203 tests in 13 packages); post-rebase go test ./cmd/zolemc passed (3 tests). Issue remains open until the branch is landed.
